# Thread: John Schott Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-schott-journal.18869/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Symbiohead — 2016-12-18T02:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-schott-journal.18869/post-205421>

hi tribe,
I'm really excited to be here and finally taking the membership plunge I've listened to pretty much every podcast that Doctor Kruse has been on, read his book, sent tons of blog posts to my kindle to study more in depth about this incredible work.

I've been in the holistic wellness field for over 11 years and yes, spent too much time in the food/ nutrition area . When I found the doc's info not only did it blow my mind but got me excited and (re) fired up about my passion of knowledge, growth, and above all helping others

I'm working on a big project to bring a rewilding minded type of wellness center/ community in Costa Rica. This work is a key component for what I want to share there and hopefully globally. In the ideal world I want to invite Doctor Kruse for our first immersion late February beginning of march. But ultimately I'm hoping to humbly learn so much from everyone here and hopefully contribute as much as well

Excited to be here and eager to move deeper.

Thanks so much

John

### Jason Siegel — 2017-01-12T05:17:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-schott-journal.18869/post-206660>

Hey John, welcome to the frontier of Quantum Biology! I just recently joined the forum also and started a journal here, and as someone who has been investigating and questioning any issues that pose a challenge to individual freedom and health for awhile, I have also been feeling really excited to be here learning and experimenting now at the most fundamental level of our health and potential- physics.

Getting started with your journal, it would be very helpful to share some more information about yourself starting describing with your environment(s) that have been around you, and a little about what things have been like for you and your health- physically, psychologically, and otherwise. You can also share basic health and environmental information about your mother and her mother, as all of the mitochondrial genome of a new person is received from their mother.

I lived in the Diamante Valley in Costa Rica last year for three months and began immersing into some important training and volunteering, and building some special connections with new friends both Costa Rican and international. I wrote about it in my first journal post here on the forum. I am plotting to go back to Costa Rica around January 28. I would like talk to you about your rewilding project and help in any way I can.

I recommend that you strongly consider the pacific Southern Zone region of Costa Rica for your project (everything beyond San Isidro de el General going south and east towards Panama). In this region we have some of the lowest population densities, lowest levels of tourism and development, and amazing beaches, beautiful mountains, primary forests, the greatest waterfalls and cool water rivers to explore and do cold thermogenesis. More specifically there are two very important areas I would like to talk to you about- The Diamante Valley, which I will tell you more about here, and the Osa Peninsula.

The Diamante Valley- Turning onto an unmarked dirt road, you begin to descend gradually downward as what appears to be a wall of steep old-growth rainforest begins to rise above you. There are 3 villages- Las Tumbas, La Florida, and San Salvador, with friendly local people and some special communities and organizations that have formed to work on things such as permaculture/organic farming self-sufficiency, natural/holistic health, alternative education styles encouraging critical thinking, creativity, and connection to each other and to the Earth, and regenerative/sustainable community/economic development. There is an organic farmers' market every Tuesday morning near the entrance to the valley, where we come together to catch up with everyone, meet new allies, play with the little kids, play with animals, and to have access to all the foods we need grown or gathered by people we know, crafts, and services such as chiropractic and energy medicine. My friend Eric created an organization called Upward Spirals which is "building tools, templates, guides, business plans, and social benefit programs for leaders of regenerative projects, to help them accelerate the co-creation of a sustainable culture, in balance with nature"- on their website there is some good information including a calendar of events such as training, meetings, courses, and retreats in the area.

With the sanctuary-like topography and physical properties of the valley, strong 9°N sunshine, abundant deep cold springs for water sources, relatively low nnEMF and population density, primary and redeveloping forest all around, cool water mountain rivers for cold thermogenesis, nearby Pacific ocean and awesome waves and seafood, and very low risk of crime or government issues, the Diamante Valley is a pretty good place to heal and experiment with quantum biology principles. I would like to make a laboratory soon too. The temperatures are typically in the 80s during the day and the 60s at night. From the valley entrance, it is about a 20 minute drive southwest to the pacific coast with beautiful beaches and excellent surfing and 30 minute drive northeast to the small friendly city San Isidro de el General, where many of my new friends live including students in the Diplomacy/English program at the Universidad Nacional where I was going every week to help and participate in Culture Class. There are two amazing waterfalls- the Diamante Waterfall with a cave under that we can sleep in, and the Nauyaca waterfall with multiple cascades and many good pools to swim and play in. They are part of the two main rivers which provide many places to swim and a way to travel in the valley- through the water or on the big rocks.

The Osa Peninsula is "the most biologically intense place on Earth" due to its biodiversity density. the southernmost region of the Pacific coast. It is mostly undisturbed/undeveloped forest, with a few small communities in the forest there, and the majority of the territory being part of the Corcovado National Park, and there are many miles of peaceful beaches with no people.

Let me know in a message and/or let us know what's going on with your rewilding project and some of your ideas when you get a chance. I would love to share some of my passion, skills, and resources so that we could work together. I am also really into survivalism/self-sufficiency, developmental/regenerative biology, and exploring ways to illuminate and overcome fear and conditioning, including worrying about what somebody else thinks of us, that get in the way of curiosity, creativity, and the ability to connect with our bodies, our feelings, with each other, and with the Earth. As for getting started with the journal here, I look forward to learning more about your experiences and what's been going on with you.

My job is to initiate the food and beverage from the land and (to me) most important is the development of the wellness program. I'm looking to introduce these concepts, spearhead the education & application in this, and offer a new nature based paradigm for those who wish to participate.

More specifically there are two very important areas I would like to talk to you about- The Diamante Valley, which I will tell you more about here, and the Osa Peninsula.
>
> The Diamante Valley- Turning onto an unmarked dirt road, you begin to descend gradually downward as what appears to be a wall of steep old-growth rainforest begins to rise above you. There are 3 villages- Las Tumbas, La Florida, and San Salvador, with friendly local people and some special communities and organizations that have formed to work on things such as permaculture/organic farming self-sufficiency, natural/holistic health, alternative education styles encouraging critical thinking, creativity, and connection to each other and to the Earth, and regenerative/sustainable community/economic development. There is an organic farmers' market every Tuesday morning near the entrance to the valley, where we come together to catch up with everyone, meet new allies, play with the little kids, play with animals, and to have access to all the foods we need grown or gathered by people we know, crafts, and services such as chiropractic and energy medicine. My friend Eric created an organization called Upward Spirals which is "building tools, templates, guides, business plans, and social benefit programs for leaders of regenerative projects, to help them accelerate the co-creation of a sustainable culture, in balance with nature"- on their website there is some good information including a calendar of events such as training, meetings, courses, and retreats in the area.
>
> With the sanctuary-like topography and physical properties of the valley, strong 9°N sunshine, abundant deep cold springs for water sources, relatively low nnEMF and population density, primary and redeveloping forest all around, cool water mountain rivers for cold thermogenesis, nearby Pacific ocean and awesome waves and seafood, and very low risk of crime or government issues, the Diamante Valley is a pretty good place to heal and experiment with quantum biology principles. I would like to make a laboratory soon too. The temperatures are typically in the 80s during the day and the 60s at night. From the valley entrance, it is about a 20 minute drive southwest to the pacific coast with beautiful beaches and excellent surfing and 30 minute drive northeast to the small friendly city San Isidro de el General, where many of my new friends live including students in the Diplomacy/English program at the Universidad Nacional where I was going every week to help and participate in Culture Class. There are two amazing waterfalls- the Diamante Waterfall with a cave under that we can sleep in, and the Nauyaca waterfall with multiple cascades and many good pools to swim and play in. They are part of the two main rivers which provide many places to swim and a way to travel in the valley- through the water or on the big rocks.
>
> The Osa Peninsula is "the most biologically intense place on Earth" due to its biodiversity density. the southernmost region of the Pacific coast. It is mostly undisturbed/undeveloped forest, with a few small communities in the forest there, and the majority of the territory being part of the Corcovado National Park, and there are many miles of peaceful beaches with no people.
>
> Let me know in a message and/or let us know what's going on with your rewilding project and some of your ideas when you get a chance. I would love to share some of my passion, skills, and resources so that we could work together. I am also really into survivalism/self-sufficiency, developmental/regenerative biology, and exploring ways to illuminate and overcome fear and conditioning, including worrying about what somebody else thinks of us, that get in the way of curiosity, creativity, and the ability to connect with our bodies, our feelings, with each other, and with the Earth. As for getting started with the journal here, I look forward to learning more about your experiences and what's been going on with you.

Wow Jason!! Thanks so much for sharing all that you have. I read your journal entry as well and it would be great to see you in Costa Rica. I'll be relocating on tues with my partner and 3 kids and very excited for the new adventure & opportunity. We will be in a nice remote area very close to San Isidro del General- so based on what you wrote, I'm even more excited now. I have very limited knowledge on Costa Rica so far- it's very new for me. Reading your comments has been helpful and is getting me excited for the limitless explorations- especially with CT, grounding, etc.

I'm a big fan of Daniel Vitali's wok. A big part of my intention is to make the [www.risecostarica.com](http://www.risecostarica.com)
Project one based on a rewilding, ecosystem driven principles. And of course what Dr. Kruse teaches is paramount- I'm extremely passionate & resonates with what he teaches. Can't wait to learn more and master the concepts- and more importantly share them with those who will apply & benefit.

I'm initially setting up the food & beverage portion of the project in an incredible mandala permaculture garden & food forest. I helped designed a bad ass kitchen for the main clubhouse and we will begin retreats & inmersions while the development of eco community based homes and wellness resort gets built. During and at that stage I will be creating the movement & wellness programming and more of a nature based medical outlet based on proper labs, hair tissue analysis. I'm an iridologist so I'll be applying that with clients as well. I'll be integrating near infrared therapy and bodywork right away as well. When we meet I'm happy to hook you up with a few sessions of my combination of trigger point therapy, fascia release & decalcification bodywork. I think this can help tremendously with your recovery goals

I empathize with what you've shared concerning circumcision and the emotional components. I too believe that we need honest, clear, and heart based communication around those very sensitive subjects. I'm here for support and to help as much as I can.

Thanks again for writing here and I look forward to more interaction and hopefully exploring with you in Costa Rica soon

Big hug

### Symbiohead — 2017-02-10T20:23:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/john-schott-journal.18869/post-208366>

Update & Finally Posting N=1 details which is what i should have done in the first place:0

first, as crazy as it sound, I find myself back in Miami. Unfortunately (for now) the move to Costa Rica didn't have the correct timing- especially for my wife and family. There were factors there that didn't make her feel safe and most important we were going to be the only family at the property which made it difficult for her to truly see herself supported, fulfilled, and socially engaged.

so with that, I'm definitely back to mitohacking mode where i will do my best to offset the high amount of nnEMF, population density, and all the other unwanted factors of being here. Thankfully there is lots of sun year round and grounding opportunities abound- both which i will be taking advantage as always.

more about me and my history...

I was born in the coast of Colombia in Cartagena. My mom and grandma are both from there. My mom lives here in Miami and her mom passed away awhile back around age 67 i think. my grandma lost her husband who was a doctor and great man to stomach cancer. After that and many of the lifestyle elements that quickly did away with her mitochondrial health she passed on with diabetes, overweight, and with barely any mobility and losing her site.

im 38, tan and in pretty good shape. I recently had some blood work from a holistic doctor and will post some results later when i grab the test. As far as wellness history, I've been in the health field (and recovering food "guru" LOL) for over 11 years. I began with alkaline diets and went through the ful spectrum of experiments. Unfortunately in the beginning I was very passionate about veganism and did raw, fruitarian, and all programs in between for a good 7 years :0 I have recovered a lot from that but definitely created some major imbalances- especially with low libido/ testosterone, knee joint challenges, extreme weight loss, slowed metabolism, and an exacerbation of a fungal condition. I introduced a weston A. price approach while recovering and when we learned that we were having our first child. There was no question that we were going to experiment with her health. So as i always do, i dove deep and took in all of the ancestral minded information about diet. I learned a lot from that and experimented with all those concepts as well.

To not make this too long, my approach with myself and clients is one now focused on Light, water, magnestism, movement, and very close version of the Epi Paleo Rx eating style. I do bodywork on myself and clients to help dissolve calcium deposits, fibrotic tissue (stiffness), collagen cross linkage, and to regain pizoelectric function. I use near infrared sauna therapy and will start incoorporating some hair tissue analysis with Dr. Garret Smith. I'm always learning more and passionate about helping others.

I'm a huge advocate of proper & living water for ultimate health. In the city going to hunt down pristine springs is quite challenging so I have been successfully drinking Pristine Hydro water for the last 8 years or so. Anyone interested in a hydrological cycle mimicked water solution a la Viktor Shauberger should definitely check out pristinehydro.com

anyways, heres were i am right now and hungry for more

# Thread: Dec 2016 - New Jack Kruse Podcast - How to become a mitochondriac - The Health Wellness Show
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dec-2016-new-jack-kruse-podcast-how-to-become-a-mitochondriac-the-health-wellness-show.18872/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2016-12-20T03:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dec-2016-new-jack-kruse-podcast-how-to-become-a-mitochondriac-the-health-wellness-show.18872/post-205505>

I really enjoyed this, Jack was on top of his game and laid it out very coherently and I had a few aha moments like reminding me what is made at end of the [ETC process](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electron_transport_chain) (ATP + EZ WATER) and why it is important. Also lots of shout outs to UK folks, Faraday, Mitchell, Nick Lane, Martin Chaplin

[https://www.sott.net/article/336894...-to-become-a-mitochondriac-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse](https://www.sott.net/article/336894-The-Health-Wellness-Show-How-to-become-a-mitochondriac-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse)

"The final **electron** acceptor is oxygen. Together with oxygen, **electrons** and protons form molecules of **water**. ... These protons flow through ATP synthase, a channel protein that uses this power to phosphorylate ADP to make ATP. The **electron transport chain**, therefore, produces 28 ATP molecules as well as **water**."

### freesia — 2016-12-20T05:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dec-2016-new-jack-kruse-podcast-how-to-become-a-mitochondriac-the-health-wellness-show.18872/post-205507>

Agreed, this podcast is excellent. Jack on top of his social engagement game...as well as the usual areas. I've shared this one far and wide.

### Valuation Man — 2016-12-21T01:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dec-2016-new-jack-kruse-podcast-how-to-become-a-mitochondriac-the-health-wellness-show.18872/post-205566>

I just listened to this today and it was pure gold. It was what I needed to hear just one day from the Winter Solstice. The lack of sun and the darkness in Ohio has started to wear me down and I found myself this past month going back to my old ways of looking for quick easy fixes that I knew intuitively were not the answers (gizmos, supplements and Rxs). I stumbled onto this site about 8 months ago while looking for some quick answers to a few physical issues. What a paradigm shift that has occurred in my thought process in regards to being optimal since diving into this site. I have spent a least 20 hours a month since last April with the blogs and webinars here and was becoming frustrated that I was not fully grasping all the concepts JK was discussing. I am a finance guy that hated biology and chemistry in school.

Like Paul I had many aha moments in this podcast and confirmed that I am on the right path in becoming a mitochondriac. I also do not feel so dumb in that JK said in this podcast that it takes a good five years to assimilate this information and love the hope he provides in the podcast. Much better than my docs of saying your getting older and just live with it. I have gone from "just tell me what to do" to wanting to learn more and figuring out what is best for myself.

### Butters — 2016-12-20T15:33:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-hacks.18876/post-205519>

Hi guys.
I have a question regarding blood pressure.
Is it a good idea to get a blood pressure measuring device for a 26 year old healthy male to do hacks?
Or is it not worth the money?

Things I would look at:

\-during sports activity (weights and HIIT)
\-before and after CT
\-sunrise/sunset (waking up/bedtime)
\-before morning, midday, afternoon sun/after morning sun
\-before and after meals

That are my ideas. But I dont know if its worth to look at?
Maybe you can help me.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-20T21:35:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-hacks.18876/post-205543>

# Thread: Hello from North of 60
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Samy Colddigger — 2016-12-20T19:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205530>

Im from Northern Canada and love what Jack has taught me about life. As soon as I started reading his blogs everything clicked! I have always wondered about what makes health and what makes illness. This likely has to do with the fact that I had a very disabled sister who was born with hemi-encephalepathy (half her brain wasn't there). She couldnt talk or walk or speak but she could sing. She died when I 6. I recently found out that my mom got a microwave during the pregnacny and used it the entire time. Given that I was born 2 years earlier with strong redox and low heteroplasmy....as well as my older brother.....it is no question in my mind what caused it. Given all the big questions I have, I have always been a learner. I was going down the food guru path for the longest time until I heard about EMF and then discovered Jack....so glad I woke up!

I live in a small cabin with no Wifi and no cell service. I sleep on a grounding sheet at night and eat heaps of oysters and salmon. Have not been sick since I started following Jacks blog about 2 years ago (I immediatley uped my seafood intake)

Now I would love to get Jack to come up a do a talk here somehow. My fellow citizens (of northern Canada) are completely unaware of the dangers and we need someone with authority to tell us its bad (so far, all Medical doctors up here have denied there is any biological effect from nnEMF).

Now I just need help brainstorming on how I can get him up here. I know some doctors around and but dont they will help much, as they are very skeptical of anything I say coming from 'some guy' on the internet). I also have zilch for funding so I was thinking of starting a go-fund-me campaign and perhaps approaching some of the non profits to try and help with funding.

Any big ideas peeps? Or any ideas at all!

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-12-22T10:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205655>

Thanks Caroline! Happy Solstice. I am up to late on my computer as usual but I did celebrate the longest night with a bon fire and some snow rolling. So hopefully I tunneled enough for a decent quantum trade off.

Have had my Cornet RF meter for about a week now and it is so amazing and reassuring to know where the good places are (less than or equal to .0005 mw/m2.) It is however disheartening to see how so many people pollute the environment with their choices. I just found out that the cabin I live at that I thought was out of cell service range is not actually. My old friends with their new friends came over and their phones worked here and the meter spiked into the red danger zone! But I am not too concerned, I wont let it happen to often now. I am thankful for what I have learned from Jack's work and also from members like yourself caroline! You may not know that I have already learned from you over the past year. I send may thanks to all the other member's on here who have made so much effort to convey important and inspiring lessons over the forum!

### caroline — 2016-12-23T08:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-of-60.18877/post-205705>

love that ^^^^^^^^

keep going!

**I have had a sterling silver necklace, chain and tiny sterling cross with diamonds on my wish list for a very long time.** Found a what I thought was a perfect one in a local jewelry store last month when my husband I went in for him to get a new battery for his watch... I usually research most everything I can think of that I am in touch with... but while in that store I totally forgot about the metal factor with EMF until I checked here.

I have a mild sensitivity to nickel so gave up stud earrings 20 years ago and I've worn very little jewelry since. I went bra-less then too. I did however buy a Q-link about 12 years ago and have worn it on a black cloth cord daily 24 /7. I lost it for about a month and noticed a loss of energy so I replaced it. I only take it off to bathe. I even wore it during radiation treatments. Online reviews say Q-Link® is a technology based on what is called _Sympathetic Resonance Technology_™. SRT™ is based on the fundamental scientific discovery that every physical system has fields of energy that permeate and surround that system. From there site
**Q-LINK® SRT™ SCIENCE & RESEARCH** [https://www.shopqlink.com/blogs/research-summaries](https://www.shopqlink.com/blogs/research-summaries)

# Thread: Any Progress With Eczema
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 16

### Hunger4More — 2016-12-21T01:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-205568>

Is anyone having any luck fixing eczema? I've been following quantum principles for about 18 months now... big improvements in health, but my eczema hasn't improved much.

Full disclosure - it's in an area that is quite difficult to get sun.

My personal experience has been that caffeine/coffee use for me may slightly exacerbate it.

Interestingly enough, I visited Jamaica in the winter of 2016 and my eczema disappeared. It reappeared when I came back home. Visited Mexico (playa Mujeres) summer of 2016 and the eczema remained. Leads me to think that there is a nnEMF link as well...

Have not had any luck using coconut oil, shea butter, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-21T13:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-205590>

No area is difficult if you do it. That is why the eczema remains. It is a baseline acute immune reaction of a de-programming of T regulator and helper cells from a lack of sunlight.

[https://www.eurekalert.org/pub\_releases/2016-12/gumc-sos121616.php](https://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2016-12/gumc-sos121616.php)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-205602>

Sunlight has so many benefits, I'm thinking about making a simple bullet point list for newbs. There are some hidden benefits people don't get. For me, I use it to reduce my overall nnEMF exposure in addition to the dopamine, serotonin, Vitamin D, gut biome populations, charging my exclusion zones up, etc.

### DLO — 2017-01-13T20:32:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-206780>

In the past when I got eczema it was just in one finger and would swell and be itchy but behind the knees is a new thing...

Jack at PDC I noticed what I thought was a bugbite or rash behind my R knee now it's on both with slighty itchyness on the L.
I got plenty of sunlight @ PDC but the area is bigger than it used to be.
I dont know if there was anything before I got to PDC as i never paid attention to the back of my knees until I had to put on swim trunks for the beach.

If I understand that article correctly sunlight speeds up the t-cells but isn't eczema an autoimmune condition could the sun have caused this to become more active?

I've also been using the sperti fiji sun lamp on my calves for about 2 months now
While at PDC I was abit more lax with diet eating citrus/ egg white/corn/ which were triggers for me in the past. IGG

### Hunger4More — 2017-01-13T22:05:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-206789>

Interestingly enough, I've noticed my eczema has improved (but not gone away) this winter with intermittent fasting.

I'd really love to get to the bottom of this. Been following Jack for a while now, got a ton of sunlight in the summer, winter is more of a challenge, but still hit the tanning beds regularly. ### Sean Waters — 2018-07-17T16:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246055>

Looking back on this my issue was actually the Vitamin A cycle... it's the only thing that has made a difference to my skin is going to bed at 8pm to be asleep for before 9pm (best).

If i do get to sleep before 9 my skin doesn't itch during sleep, i don't wake up intermittently, and the next day it is not inflamed and just peels off. It is actually healing and smooth for first time in years - It's all to do with the Cortisol/ DHEA/ Melatonin axis and the Vit A cycle. I thought 9.30/10pm bedtimes were sufficient for Vit A cycle but I was wrong (for me that is - others may be different).

My problem is i've found it hard to be consistent at getting to sleep before 9pm.

But on the days i do, going to bed EARLIER and even before sunset in the summer has made such a difference to me. Just 1 hour.

Matthew Walker (Why We Sleep author) said on a podcast that every hour asleep before midnight is "worth" 2 hours to our cells. Double points basically. I found that really interesting. So sleep by 9pm with a 5am wake up = a "feels like" 11 hours sleep, not 8 hours. Usually i wake up at 4am, so i get a "feels like" of 10 hours.

At first i woke at 2.30am and so did my best friend who has also tried it. That would count as 8.5 hours according to Walker. I said to my friend that the Growth Hormone phase 3-5am shouldn't be missed, but he said he felt good so he rolls with it. After doing it for a while, i noticed if i use black out blinds on the windows i don't wake at 2.30am, and it extends me an hour or 2 to a closer to sunrise wake up time.

His adrenal fatigue has disappeared though, and he gets up at 2.30/3am everyday now and has a Bulletproof Green Tea and Salmon/Seaweed epi paleo breakfast. He feels amazing. so maybe that growth hormone phase is brought forward?! I don't know. Maybe noone does. But the early bedtime + black out blinds and a cave-like dark room that is cooled with a fan in summer seems to be best practice for me...

In indonesia i was eating past 8pm on the beach (prolactin secretion @ midnight/GH destroyed by leptin), and i didn't use blue blockers back then. I woke at 10am most days absolutely drained. It was early adrenal fatigue. I'm finding a lot of my posts from 2016/17 are wrong or misinformed - i've learned a LOT more since then.

This post could be wrong too. So as always.... try it, observe, and report back with your n=1

### drezy — 2018-07-17T16:14:32+0300

Sean Waters -- Circadian zero to Circadian Hero?

> But the early bedtime + black out blinds and a cave-like dark room that is cooled with a fan in summer seems to be best practice for me..

I'm completely with you here. I don't write about it and probably should, but doing sun and cold as much as I try to I naturally get tired earlier and sleep before most residents at senior homes. If the sun is down then whatever it is isn't important anymore.

If you're "wrong" then your wrong with skin that's not itchy...

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-17T16:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246060>

Hahaha yeah i've just bought 4 books: Matthew Walker's one i just mentioned, and then: Rhythms of Life (Frietzman and **Foster - discovered melanopsin**), Seasons of Life (same authors but 2010 updated research on seasonality) and the Circadian Code (Satchin Panda - june 18).

I just read a little free e-book today on amazon kindle too called: Circadian Rhythms: Below Our Awareness (C.D. Shelton)

I'm fascinated. I **assumed** my circadian rhythms were in tune until recently. But little details matter so much. 1 hour earlier - that is it!! to stop my lifelong eczema!! 1 hour man. Incredible.

Yes the daytime Sunshine has a huge affect on my sleep - i'm way more drowsy if Grounded in Sunlight all day. Me and Shah would be so tired at 7pm sunset in mexico... i dubbed the term "Mela-stoned" to describe the melatonin drowsiness we got everyday. We were out at 5am at the beach and then all day in the water/sun until 7pm. Blue blockers on at 7pm made it even more serious.

Don't get it as much now i'm back at work in London of course.... but a big difference is waking up early, forcing it a little bit with an alarm clock for a few days, and that will tire me earlier in the evening.

Cold shower too or CT helps so much (as long as bathroom isnt mouldy then you get a stress response). Any kind of stress fucks this up.. arguments, cooking dinner, watching tv, literally anything... I have zero activity at night now. Sit in my garden and relax with some water before i go to bed.

Another factor is dim light. Any amount of light can fuck this up. My GF checked me on this last night. She loves the black out blind now, before it was "too hot" for it, but the fan sorted that out.

Just got to make this consistent.. see what results i get. **full skin clear up would be immense**

### Hunger4More — 2018-07-18T02:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-progress-with-eczema.18880/post-246099>

To add my update to this:

It's been said before on this site that eczema is an "allergy" to nnEMF. I believe this to be 100% true.

Where did I have my worst eczema? On my left groin area. What did I often carry there? My car keys - which use RF to start my car (I haven't carried my cell in my pocket for a long time). Moving the keys made a huge difference in my eczema.

It still flares up from time to time with excessive cell phone use. I've used the app "Moment" to track my cell usage and it correlates with any flare ups.

I live in a big city - the eczema only fully goes away when I can get sunshine in more remote areas (ie Vermont June 2018).

But I'm going to give Sean's hack above a try as well and report back.

Hope this can help others.

### Penny — 2016-12-23T21:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fcc-chairman-tom-wheeler-steps-down.18884/post-205711>

**Or we could all start bombarding the potential replacements... :**

**Trump May Appoint Pence Ally And Telecom Deregulator To FCC**
[Aaron Pressman](http://fortune.com/author/aaron-pressman/)
Updated: Dec 02, 2016 8:26 PM Pacific | Originally published: Nov 30, 2016
A former ally of Vice President-elect Mike Pence with a strong focus on deregulation could be in line for an appointment to the Federal Communications Commission, possibly as the next chairman.

Brandt Hershman, a longtime Indiana state senator, is rumored to be the leading candidate for an opening on the agency, which oversees the telecommunications, broadcasting, and cable industries, [Politico reported on Wednesday](https://www.politicopro.com/technology/story/2016/11/early-chatter-starts-around-potential-trump-fcc-picks-139172) citing unnamed sources. Hershman worked closely with the vice president-elect after Pence was elected Indiana's governor in 2012.

Hershman declined to comment.

The current senate majority floor leader, Hershman authored a massive law to deregulate telecommunications in Indiana a decade ago. The bill ended government regulation of phone rates, freed up cable companies from needing to get dozens of local licenses to offer service, and stopped cities and towns from setting up their own municipal broadband services.

_[Get Data Sheet](http://fortune.com/getdatasheet/), Fortune's technology newsletter._

The FCC under chairman Tom Wheeler, appointed by President Obama, has pressed several major regulatory initiatives, including net neutrality rules to prevent Internet providers from blocking or charging more for access to any web site or online service. Wheeler also pushed online privacy rules that apply to Internet service providers. The chairman dropped his efforts to break the cable industry's set top box monopoly, however.

Analysts expect an FCC run by a Trump appointee would roll back those rules, which have been opposed by Republicans in Congress and major communications companies. The president's party typically occupies three of the commission's five seats, and the president designates one member as chairman. Trump could name a new appointee such as Hershman chairman or possibly designate current Republican FCC member Ajit Pai as chairman.

Trump has already named three prominent opponents of net neutrality to his transition team overseeing the FCC. [Two weeks ago](http://fortune.com/2016/11/21/trump-net-neutrality-fcc/), he picked Jeff Eisenach, an economist who has been on Verizon’s [(vz, -0.24%)](http://fortune.com/company/vz) payroll, and Mark Jamison, who formerly worked on Sprint’s [(s, +0.18%)](http://fortune.com/company/s) lobbying team and now heads the University of Florida’s Public Utility Research Center. This week, trump added Roslyn Layton, a telecom industry consultant and visiting fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, who has opposed both the net neutrality and privacy rules.

### Matty_M — 2016-12-24T21:19:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fcc-chairman-tom-wheeler-steps-down.18884/post-205756>

**[FIRST DRAFT OF LETTER TO TRUMP (and/or Surgeon General/FCC/FDA/EPA); Please suggest additions, deletions, and modifications.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)**

\- [It is long, however I do not see any redundancies. Please point any out.
\- Anyone, including Jack, please optimize the "health effects" section for the greatest effect and clarity, and provide the best evidence you have](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

\---------------------------
****[LINK TO GOOGLE DRIVE DOC WITH HYPERLINKS, ability to comment (first draft)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj\_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)****

\-----------

[Dear Mr. President,

I believe that fierce protection of the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens from any threat, external _and_ internal, ought to come before all other considerations of a government of the People, by the People, and for the People. Not only is such my belief, but it is guaranteed in the preamble of the Constitution: “…to insure domestic Tranquility, provide for the common defence, promote the general Welfare, and secure the Blessings of Liberty to ourselves and our Posterity…”

Not only is the government of the United States of America now actively attacking the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens, who created it to protect themselves; it is allowing a specific regulatory body to commit a sick violation of human rights upon its own citizens.

On July 14th, the _Federal Communications Commission_ (_FCC_) voted to allow the unregulated use of a new band of radio/microwave radiation nationwide with “5G Spectrum Frontiers”. This is due to the push of Chairman Tom Wheeler since his appointment and unanimous confirmation in 2013, despite the fact that he was previously president of _The Wireless Industry_ (_CTIA_\- formerly the _Cellular Telephone Industries Association_), the world’s largest Telecommunications Lobbyist Organization. This represents a conflict of interests that fundamentally undermines the rights of American citizens and the democracy of the United States of America as a whole.

The ongoing debate about the safety of cell phones and wireless radiation in general was suppressed by claims of major media outlets and the American Cancer Association that the evidence does not support a link between radio/microwave radiation and cancer. These claims are based on industry-conducted research. For research to be considered scientific, it must be falsifiable. However, the Telecommunications Industry will never publish evidence that proves the harms of its technology; in fact, on numerous occasions the Industry has not released findings of this type. Because the Industry is only able to publish data that does not contradict the use of Wireless Radiation, it is fundamentally unable to falsify its hypotheses; its research, therefore, does not constitute science. Using it to make public policy is akin to letting a King pass whichever laws best suit his endeavors. Our nation was born to prevent this, and you must not be the president that allows it to continue.

The FCC’s decision to allow the use of 5G technology was made despite scientific consensus that **5G is not safe, it threatens the safety of, and is actively harming the health of Americans**, **especially of children, who are astronomically more susceptible to the toxic effects of wireless radiation**. Likewise, there is scientific consensus that the FCC’s safety limits, which only consider _thermal_ effects of wireless radiation (such as literal microwaving of tissue), are more than 30 years out-of-date. They do not protect the population from, rather they **completely ignore all non-thermal effects**, including:

· Brain Tumor Formation and DNA Breakages, confirmed by the US National Toxicology Program’s $25 million dollar study in 2016

(http://www.saferemr.com/2016/05/national-toxicology-progam-finds-cell.html , http://microwavenews.com/news-center/ntp-cancer-results )

· DNA Breakages, Chromosomal Aberrations, Increased Oncogenic Activity, Altered Blood Pressure, Increased Brain Cancer, Altered Melatonin Levels: study

· Anxiety, Depression, and Suicide:

· Do You Have Microwave/EMR Sickness? , Study, Study, Study

· Lowered Sperm Count, Sperm Damage, and Infertility across Society:

study , study , The Disappearing Male

· Alzheimer’s: study

· Asthma: study , study

· Meta-Analyses:
Bioinitiative: Inadequacy of Standards, Gene Expression, DNA Damage, Stress Response, Immune Alteration, Brain Tumora, Childhood Cancers, Alzheimer’s Disease, Fertility
Powerwatch: Peer-Reviewed Scientific Studies on EMF Related Subjects

EMF-Portal: Thousands of Studies on Effects of EMFs
Prove-It: over 5000 Studies on Effects of EMFs

At a press conference on June 20th, the FCC confiscated the Credentials of Bloomberg Reporter Todd Shields because he was speaking with a former Congressional candidate, Kevin Mottus, about health concerns of 5G. Mottus asked Wheeler “The NTP study shows wireless causes cancer sub-thermally. How can you proceed with more wireless expansion with FCC standards only recognizing thermal effects, ignoring thousands of studies showing cancerous effects, neurological effects, and reproductive harm?” and was ignored. He was then prevented from displaying a sign and taking a t-shirt out of his back to inform the press of this information, and was repeatedly threatened by security. This suppression of truth is reminiscent of Fascist regimes and is not just unconstitutional, but sick and evil.

](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)
_[“5G will use much higher frequency bands \[24 to 100+ GHz\]… antennas that can aim and amplify signals… massive deployment of small cells… tens of billions of dollars in economic activity… hundreds of billions of microchips… if something can be connected, it will be connected…”](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)_
[
](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)
_[“Unlike some countries, we do not believe we should spend the next couple of years studying what 5G should be or how it should operate. The future has a way of inventing itself. Turning innovators loose is far preferable to expecting committees and regulators to define the future. We won’t wait for the standards.” – Tom Wheeler, FCC Chairman (LINK)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)_
[

The 5G buildout is infrastructure intensive, and requires towers on top of lamp posts and telephone poles every twelve homes. This puts every American, especially children, at high risk for the carcinogenic and other effects of this technology. To ignore this massive risk is the highest form of negligence. Those responsible will pay, **but the true damage will be unjustifiable and irreparable harm to the lives of hundreds of millions of American citizens.**

The longer we ignore this reality, the higher the price American citizens will pay in the form of disease and skyrocketing healthcare costs. **Insurance giants are aware of these facts, as are Cell Companies**. Lloyd’s of London has now excluded any liability coverage for injuries, “directly or indirectly arising out of, resulting from or contributed to by electromagnetic fields, electromagnetic radiation, electromagnetism, radio waves or noise.” In its 2013 Emerging Risks report, Swiss Re moved the risk of EMFs affecting the insurance industry in the future from “medium” to “high”, due to courts ruling in favor of EMF claimants and “unforeseen consequences of electromagnetic fields”. In its 2014 annual report, AT&T states “as we deploy newer technologies, we also face current and potential litigation relating to alleged adverse health effects of customers or employees who use such technologies including, for example, wireless handsets”, as does Verizon “… our wireless business also faces personal injury and consumer class action lawsuits relating to alleged health effects of wireless phones or radio frequency transmitters, and class action lawsuits that challenge marketing practices and disclosures relating to alleged adverse health effects of handheld wireless phones. We may incur significant expenses in defending these lawsuits. In addition, we may be required to pay significant awards or settlements.”

People want faster connections because they trust that if there were any significant evidence of harm, the responsible authority (the FCC) would make it known through stringent regulations, warning labels, and media attention. Ironically, the FCC is creating massive consumer demand for this technology by ignoring hundreds of researchers and thousands of studies contradicting industry interests, and suppressing scientific discussion at press conferences. The FCC has employed, and continues to employ its power to assist the wireless industry in constructing as many Cell Towers and Cell Antennas as they can, as fast as they can, wherever they want, including atop public schools. The FCC is acting unconstitutionally and is not working for the People, but for the Industry. This gross Violation of Democracy and Human rights must end immediately to preserve the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens and all Posterity.

**America will never be “Great Again” as long as our government allows this Orwellian suppression and ignorance of scientific evidence and the propagation of carcinogenic technologies to continue without any consideration of the Health, Welfare, and Safety of American citizens.** We will continue to pay in the form of America’s massive epidemic of chronic diseases until the FCC is rebuilt with non-industry experts to put the People first, before all considerations, including industry profit, as the government of the United States of America ought and was ordained to do by the Constitution of the People.

To end this unconstitutional violation of the rights of American citizens, four things must occur:

1) Repeal or Amendment of the 1996 Telecommunications Act in alignment with the precautionary principle, i.e. that antennae should not be erected in residential areas or in the vicinity of childcare centers.

a. This act has stripped all States and local governments of their power to consider the potential adverse health impacts of RF radiation from Cell Towers and Cell Antennas, when a wireless company files any type of zoning application seeking to install them.

b. In addition to imposing this new time constraint, the FCC further ruled that wireless companies can sue local governments in court if they fail to render a decision within the deadlines which the FCC has imposed upon them.

c. Furthermore, this act prevents anyone from suing a Telecommunications company for damages incurred due to the use of their technology.

2) Appointment of an FCC Chairman who will stop the 5G rollout and take a cautionary stance on the further development and current use of wireless technology or,

3) The dissolution of the FCC and replacement with a balance body of non-industry researchers who are qualified to protect Public Welfare and not industry interests alone

4) A cautionary stance on the development of technologies in general where there is significant dissent from qualified scientific researchers, in order to protect the safety of unknowing citizens

To echo Martin Luther King Jr.: “Nothing in the world is more dangerous than sincere ignorance and conscientious stupidity”. We have not forgotten our rights as Americans, and we will hold the industry and responsible governmental authorities responsible for any and all damages occurred due to this negligence. Please do not be a president who irreparably insults the Health, Welfare, and Safety of future generations by allowing the FCC to disregard scientific evidence and suppress discussion on the biological effects of wireless technology, particularly 5G.

\- Matthew Maruca

Narberth, Pennsylvania](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UZ6BT0auVbxj_03rjALjvMMRQySb4Elqf5e0Trn20sQ/edit?usp=sharing)

Matt,

Both of the links you cited have **disappeared into a black hole** on my web browser, so no dice.

I did do a search on _Blushield_ and poked around that company's home page.

As I suspected, they are peddling another 'frequency' device that is supposed to cancel out harmful EMF frequencies in your environment by working at a subtle, not physical level.

There have been other threads here on these unproven devices, and I believe I am correct in saying Dr Kruse does not believe they are effective.

I am not saying they are snake oil-- I haven't tried any similar devices except for the **Q-Link** pendant. They might possibly have a beneficial effect, but unless you can put an RF meter near one and see radiation go down to near zero you have to color me a glowing neon pink skeptic.

I hope any Kruse members that purchase and use these devices will do us a favor and give us their anecdotal reports on whether they noticed a benefit that is not placebo-like.

Yeah, my gut feeling is that it's a band-aid at best... although I am open to band-aids if they work well, until I can make more significant lifestyle shifts to my environment.

But I own every EMF meter needed to test RF, Electric Fields, Magnetic Fields, and Body Voltage separately, and most of these devices I've tested show hardly any change in the past... doesn't mean they don't do ANYTHING, but it's definitely hard to quantify.

Schumann resonance generators are great, but you're not overriding the source in my opinion. (and my opinion could change, I have a lot to learn!)

# Thread: Patty's journal of growing younger
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PattyW — 2016-12-22T05:25:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205643>

Hello to all! I am excited to be on this journey where I feel younger and younger. What a surprise at the age of 56!

I was diagnosed with "adrenal fatigue" 15 years ago with symptoms way before that. My cortisol tested low several times. And from July 2013 to Nov 2015, I took hydrocortisone tablets. I was diagnosed with leaky gut. I consulted specialists and researched a ton, but I kept saying "There is something everyone is missing here". Bingo! Light, magnetism and water is it! Anyway I started getting things right starting November 2015 after a particularly bad episode of "adrenal fatigue" that left me unable to work. I was off the hydrocortisone by January and symptom free by May. I have been increasing my efficiency at living naturally. And it blows me away at how well this works. The better I feel, the more energy I have to do a better job at this. My vision has improved and I swear my hair is getting less gray. I have had a life of asthma and allergies (foods and airborne) and some of those still persist. I have had issues of low estrogen and severe vaginal atrophy. Estriol vaginal cream cured that and I still use it for fear that will return. My libido is average, I guess, but higher than a lot of women my age. It has been low in the past, though.

I live at ~39 latitude.

I'm 56 yrs old, female, fair skinned with red hair. I weigh 110 lbs and am 5 foot 2 inches tall. My skin does tan fairly easily. I do have some melasma and some non-pigmented spots (vitiligo?) and freckles. My ancestry is Celtic with a dash of Irish. My eyes are green. My mother died of Scleroderma. My maternal grandmother had diabetes and I think she died of a stroke. My father died of cancer.

I live in a house in a suburban-type neighborhood. My wifi was only on a few hours a day during the past year and off completely since November 2016. But the neighbors have wifi, of course. I started turning off electricity to bedroom 1 week ago. I limit the use of my cell phone and keep it as far away from me as possible.

I have used red bulbs after sunset for a year and mostly I just do stuff in the dark. I don't turn on any bulbs during the day when I'm home except for Solar Glo during winter and IR for healing. If I have visitors, they complain of being unable to see anything so I get my glasses on while they turn on a light. Geez! I even have red bulbs in my refrigerator. And when I drive at night, I wear the glasses. One thing that irritates me is all the lighting at night! It takes a lot of work to block out the freaking street lights and yard lights from lighting up the inside of my home. And when there is snow on the ground, these lights are magnified and obnoxious.

In the Spring, Summer, and Fall I put my water outside for several hours. In winter I put it under a UV bulb. I will be getting a r/o system next week. We have fluoridated water and I have been using the PUR filter which doesn't get rid of all of it. I'm sure there's more I do to improve my water.

My biggest issue is my job as a pharmacist which for most of my life required 4-5 days of 10 hr shifts with no breaks. Lunch was eaten while working. The nnEMFs are high and the blue light intense in pharmacies. In the past year, thankfully, I'm not working as much and I now work where I can usually get 30 min for lunch and a couple of breaks. I wear blue blocker glasses, keep my skin covered (I'm about to begin wearing a hat at one location), and I take my solar glo bulb with me at 2 of my locations. I take lunch and breaks outside and go barefoot. I can definitely feel the effects when I work, yuck! If I work several days in a row, it takes me a lot of sunshine and earth to recover.

I currently sleep quite well and I wake up refreshed. I go outside barefoot or if it's cold I wear soleless moccasins and I wait for sunrise. When I am not working, I spend as much time as possible outside. I lay on the ground if the weather permits.

I use cool water at the end of my shower. I used to run from the cold and now I go outside in winter purposely to get cold.

I eat fish most days and animal flesh most days. I eat seaweed daily. My diet has consisted of mostly organic, whole food for 15 years. I have been low carb for a lot of years, but now am having carbs in season. It makes sense! In the past year I have transitioned to not eating after sunset, eating the bulk of my calories in the AM and at noon, eating first thing upon arising and only eating 3 meals instead of 5. I have been obsessive about diet for 20 years and couldn't understand why I was not healthy. Now I know!

I was taking tons of supplements, but have decreased those. I had a Spectracell analysis done last year showing I needed vitamin E and choline so I still take those. I'm sure my status is not anywhere the same now. I take fish oil by Nordic Naturals which is one of the best, but I know eating fish is best. I'm plan to stop the capsules soon as I have increased my fish. I also take minerals, vitamin A, vitamin K2, a small amount of vitamin C and B complex. I take 10mg DHEA in the AM. I use progesterone and bi-est cream at hs (10mg/.625mg). I take a curcumin and boswellia combo and colostrum prn asthma and allergies.

I know I have a lot more work to do and I am sucking up information on this stuff daily. It's truly fascinating, but there is so much!! I read the Epi-Paleo book a year ago. I am reading the blog, listening to podcasts, watching webinars, and reading Facebook posts. Thank you Jack for all your hard work and for making this available to everyone.

### caroline — 2016-12-22T06:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205648>

welcome Patty...... you are doing great!

with your training/career ..... you must be able to inhale a lot of this stuff! I would guess that your head doesn't hurt nearly as much as mine!

where do you live?

### PattyW — 2016-12-22T18:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-journal-of-growing-younger.18888/post-205669>

He was supposed to be dead by now.

hey everybody,

did any one of you experience increasing thirst when you started eating more electrone-dense food like eggs, fish etc? My thirst is really big.

### LieselK — 2018-02-20T20:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-thirst.18890/post-235208>

Reviving this thread- I too feel way more thirsty and I'm almost 4 wks into Epi-Paleo Rx and 3 wks into Leptin Reset. I changed from our well water to Costco bottled water a number of days ago and that seemed to make it worse- wondered if it was the minerals? There is sodium added. Today I have Icelandic, but I'm not sure if I can keep up with it financially. I have enough here for the next 2 days so we'll see if I notice a difference. I'm drinking 2L of water a day (I'm 5'5" and 112 lbs).

The first couple weeks on Leptin Reset I felt less thirsty, but then it seemed to change. Not much sun to be had lately here so I bet that factors big. I am being really careful about nnEMF.

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-22T17:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-thirst.18890/post-235387>

Yeah get into the cold baths and showers and then see what happens. Immediately after you shouldn't be thirsty, but it may wear off. Do you work in an office? Own an iphone? Wi-Fi at home? Drive a new car? Live near the city? Block of flats?

For every Yes, you will likely need more CT and Sun and DDW to off-set that. You don't get much sun, or enough, so do CT or expect to pay for Icelandic.

Living in UK, when I went Epi-Paleo Keto/ Leptin Rx I had to move from tap water/regular water to full time San Pellegrino. Costed me £150 a month for water for over a year. I'm now moving onto Voss and Icelandic...

But in a few weeks I'll be living at the Equator. It's just too difficult to survive at these Northern Latitude/nnEMF hell hole cities these days, and it will get worse with 5G...

I am a stay-at-home-mom so my days under blue lights for work are over thank goodness. No iPhone, but smartphone though it's on airplane mode at night and wifi off during day. Working on plugging the tech into the modem directly so no wifi needed at all. No new car. Live in outskirts of a city on some acreage, but 5G is infiltrating. I have Hashimoto's disease though- that's my biggie. Already feeling so much better though! Prepping for the long haul of taking care of ME  The sun will be coming- we've been in an unusual gray spell here though.

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-23T11:47:25+0200

Well that all sounds positive, and so it may be a part of the change that the Leptin Rx instigates... I know that I had huge die-off symptoms and struggles with that diet, so keep going!

But just in case it is some hidden nnEMF, I would definitely pursue the CT, once you do it and get used to the cold, it becomes an extremely valuable tool to have in this world. Especially as Mitochondrial Heteroplasmy rates rise every decade, and as you say about 5G... even Doc has struggled lately with the biological perils of this modern world...

# Thread: Sex and acute head pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ShannyM — 2016-12-22T20:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205679>

Oh my, such a personal question but here it goes:

A month ago, seconds before the point of climax I had an intense rush of pain in my head. It felt like the lining of my brain or skull was being pulled from the back of my neck and traveled around my head over the top to my forehead and behind my eyes. So freaking intense and painful it brought tears to my eyes and afterward I bawled like a little girl. Wtf? It happened twice more after but no where as painful and sustained.

My body is lacking something...???

Has anyone else experienced this or am I odd? I don't believe it's only a female thing, I'll assume men have experienced this as well.

### Lahelada — 2016-12-24T01:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205727>

Hehe Caroline ! Lahelada stumbled over this thread twice already,wondering how to phrase an answer. Well,here goes.
From what I could figure it migraines at point of orgasm are due to insufficient Prolactin being available at the time .I think in my case I was a bit happy go lucky with the MB dosis. Mine took a bit of tweaking only and it disappeared in a week at the most never to return.

### ShannyM — 2016-12-24T02:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205729>

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) thank you for responding. very interesting to say the least. Not sure what MB dosis is, it's not listed in the glossary. Do you mean Mg?

### Lahelada — 2016-12-24T03:25:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205730>

HI Shanny. MB is methylene blue. However, prolactin is only supposed to be behind this pain. No data as asuch anf no data that my MB had something to with it. I suggest to wait and see if it goes away. Pain like particles, is best left unobserved until it actually happens.It may just have been a passing thing for you .
Don't stress,test..

### ShannyM — 2016-12-24T05:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205731>

Ok got it. That sort of discomfort happened 3 times in the past 5 years and then I could easily rationalize it as a tension headache during a very emotionally stressful time then 3 more times in the last 6 months (mentally and emotionally I'm in a great place in my life). The excruciating episode happened after I started the epi-paleo rx which is why I posted about it and was very puzzled by it. After the mentioned episode I began the Mitochondria Rx and the pain didn't happen but I could feel it try to roar it's ugly head and I "willed it away" so to speak - I meditate and can control tensions and discomforts - but that puppy snuck up on me when I least expected it. I haven't done any of the reading on methylation so I'm in the dark as of yet. I'll study up, I assume it has to do with how your body processes hormones which explains partly why my sex hormones are out of balance.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-24T15:45:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sex-and-acute-head-pain.18891/post-205740>

coital thunderclap headache is usually due to superior cervical ganglion blood flow changes............I think it happens because you go into sex with a high PVN tone and with orgasm you have a rapid change in blood flow to the brain and the superior cervical ganglion cannot maintain CMRO2 of the brain and the headache is the result. I think it is related to breathing during the excitement leading up to the orgasm.

# Thread: Finally Diving In! Biohacking Portland, OR.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shane O — 2016-12-22T23:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/post-205684>

Howdy Gang!

My name is Shane, I'm 38 yo, living in a suburb of Portland with my terrific wife of 8 years and our 4 kids. Life is a zoo! But wouldn't trade it.

We have been orbiting the Paleo-sphere since 2011, and did find some health improvements there, but I always felt like there was more meat on the bone. I actually tried to read some of Dr. Kruse's blogs back then, but my brain pretty much overheated and I fell into the more shiny and superficial marketing of some of the Paleo gurus. I recently found quite an appetite for Jack's podcasts and have been listening to them on repeat, taking notes, and employing some of the hacks that I gleaned from the audio. The whole family has been wearing Uvex at night, eating much more seafood, and I've been experimenting with CT. Wifi is off at night, as are all phones and devices. Found out the other day that airplane mode doesn't really change the emf output of my phone. Shocking!!!

Thankfully myself, my wife, and the kids are all in reasonable health these days. I want to become an active part of this community to ensure my wife and I are performing optimally, and that the kids are set up to understand the context of our modern world and ramifications for their health and performance as they grow.
The other reason I've joined now is that my mother has just been diagnosed with a golf ball sized glioma. I'll likely post an optimal journal for her prior to my own, as she has a lot coming at her these days and hasn't really bought into the Jack Kruse optimal living lens. We are meeting with a neurosurgeon today (**wish it was Dr. Kruse!**) to learn more about her tumor and options for treatment.

I look forward to connecting with as many of you as possible to learn, share and grow together.

### prime1 — 2016-12-23T03:03:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/post-205689>

.
Finally decided the only safe place to live is near the equator in a nudist colony - a ton of sun and seafood. A small island outside of Fuji perhaps.

.

### Shane O — 2016-12-24T00:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/post-205720>

@[prime1](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19252/) Agreed, but not terribly realistic for me right now. Hoping to learn about the hacks others are using to improve their environments...until they get to the equatorial nudist colony

### caroline — 2016-12-24T00:38:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-diving-in-biohacking-portland-or.18892/post-205722>

welcome Shane..... You are in the right spot for quantum healing .....

### Celeste — 2016-12-28T23:42:15+0200

# Thread: Patty's Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shane O — 2016-12-23T00:22:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/post-205685>

I'm starting this journal for my mom, Patty. Hopefully one day she will join the forum and participate, but for now, she has more pressing matters, like the attached image. Plus, she isn't yet aware of Dr. Kruse and his work. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My brother and I took her in last week for a CT based on some concerns about abnormal behavior over a few months and what we thought might be a stroke or other neurodegeneration. After the scan was completed, we were immediately sent to the ER and found out that she has a golf-ball sized glioma in her right frontal area. The on-call neurologist was empathetic and also to the point telling us that this was a very severe condition. He conferred with a neurosurgeon and an appointment was made (for this afternoon) to sit down with the surgeon and get a lay of the land. Too bad it's not with @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/). I'll post additional details afterwards.

For now, here is a bit of context on my mother and her mother.
Age: 71
Haplotype: ? (mine is J2b1a, and I don't think she has found out what hers is)
Lifestyle:

-   Born and raised in Portland, OR
-   2 children, ages 35, 38 (me)
-   Divorced when I was 6, never remarried or seriously dated
-   Occupation: Secondary school teacher for 33 years
-   Not particularly active, chronically overweight
-   Never smokes, rarely drinks.
-   Although I have convinced her to to make some dietary changes over the past few years, she mostly eats a SAD template.
-   Lives in a townhome, surrounded by other townhomes
-   Sleeps on the second floor
-   Spends most of her time in a chair downstairs, using her phone and laptop

Diseases:

-   Prediabetic
-   Depression
-   HPV
-   Glaucoma
-   Currently taking: simvastatin, levothyroxine, sertraline
-   After discovery of tumor, prescribed: an anti-seizure, steroid, and sleeping aid.

Labs:

-   She had a recent blood draw, believe it was for hormone levels, I don't have this yet, but will post when I do.

Misc:

-   Here are some thoughts she provided me recently on herself and her mother (Helen):
-   Hi Mom,

    I'm doing some research on my genetics and how they relate to my health. Mitochondria are passed down on the maternal side, and they play a large role in overall health and disease resistance, so I'd like to learn a bit more about Grandma Helen and you, hoping you can tell me a bit more about some of the following:

    -   Where was she born, and where did she live throughout her life (city, state)

    she was born in Portland (parents planned ahead for an expected twin birth) along with her brother Kenneth

    -   Activity level throughout her life...did she exercise like a daily walk or....?

    she walked a lot. I don't think she had a bike.

    -   Vices like smoking, pills, and alcohol, when did she start and what were her consumption levels?

    She smoked because her girlfriends did in college (but likely didn't inhale)

    She did not like beer.
    She got comfortable drinking sweeter things like martinis, wine, manhattans and Old Fashions
    Helen's mom was a teetotaler.

    -   What ailments or diseases did she have?

    I don't think she had any diseases and she was not a risk taker.

    -   What was her diet like?

    She ate what was put on the table (as a child)
    When she was out of the house as an adult, she indulged in high fat, sweet items and always talked about being healthy. **_HA !_**

    -   For you, I'd be most interested in these items before I was born and while you carried me in utero.

    I always craved sweet foods. I didn't crave salt, etc.
    I liked scalloped potatoes as well as baked potatoes.
    My recollection is that if I didn't eat a starch like rice, or potatoes......I'd be awakened in the early morning - simply starving
    I walked a mile to school each way: ages 5-14.
    Sometimes I had too much sugar and got a bit "flat".
    I do have a food diary that I used with one of the two of you. Maybe we can find it next time you're over.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Thank you all for reading and considering her story. I would love to dig into any resources that come to mind for you on the subject of brain tumors . I have read the EMF, Brain Gut, epi-onco-genesis series, as well as a few other random ones that have caught my eye. I also listen to many of Dr. Kruse's podcasts on repeat and generally take notes. I feel like I have a very basic idea of the three-legged stool and how it relates to cancers.

### Shane O — 2016-12-24T00:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pattys-story.18893/post-205724>

So we met with the neurosurgeon yesterday afternoon. Here are some of the highlights (not the most appropriate word, I know) of the meeting:

-   He is recommending left frontal craniostomy to remove as much of the tumor as possible. Appointment is made for 1/4/17.
    -   Radiation/Chemo and other non-surgical procedures are not being recommended until after removing as much as possible...won't be effective without a tissue analysis
-   He didn't want to discuss much about mid to long range outcomes until his oncologist is able to analyze some of the tissue during the procedure
-   This tumor is **brand new** since February of this year when my mom had a CT for concerns about hearing loss.
-   The doc said that this is in a relatively easy area to access, with lower risk of bleeding or permanent loss of function than other regions of the brain.
-   She won't be able to live alone for some time after the procedure, and is not driving now or for the foreseeable future.
    -   Would love to get recommendations from you all on how to best hack the ICU/Floor/Rehab environments that she will be spending time in post-op
        -   Was thinking to make sure she wears her Uvex, get her outside in the AM, tourmaline bracelet, check on what type of garbage they might intend to feed her orally or via IV and see if there are better options...etc...
    -   Although a burden, my wife and I are considering moving her in with us after the procedure. This will be a positive thing in that I can more effectively control her environment and diet.

My mom seemed pretty numb afterwards and displayed very little emotion. She has had a very flat affect lately. I asked her if she wanted to get a second opinion but she doesn't unless I hear of something "she would want to know about". She has always had a "passenger" mentality when it comes to her health, has never really taken an active role in improving it. Getting her to buy-in on JK-Optimization hacks is going to be a battle I'm sure.

To be honest, surgical removal seems wise asap given that this thing has such a head start and is very aggressive. I'm certainly going to continue reading and am open to other ideas and your inputs.

Going to begin the process of ensuring her will is in order, my brother and I have POA & are co-signers on her checking account, her end-of-life care form (POLST) is filled out etc...

Thanks for reading!

# Thread: Spectral comparisons of growth lights
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### findinghealth — 2016-12-27T17:13:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/post-205823>

Chameleon Plasma achieves pretty complete spectrum according to their measurement.
[http://chameleongrowsystems.com/grow-light-grow-lights-grow-lighting-spectral-specific/](http://chameleongrowsystems.com/grow-light-grow-lights-grow-lighting-spectral-specific/)

They seem to compare their measurement against "Action Spectrum" for plants. File below gives som explanation of what "Action Spectrum" is:
[https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What light do plants need\_5.pdf](https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What%20light%20do%20plants%20need_5.pdf)

"An absorption spectrum defines the wavelengths that are absorbed. An action spectrum defines the wavelengths that are most effective for photosynthesis. In other words, it is the portion of the spectrum that does the work. This is what is most important in plant growth and metabolism. It is important to note that light absorption and light utilization are two different phenomena."

\-------------------------
Newbie Question: Is there any work done on "Absorbtion spectrum" vs "Action Spectrum" curves for humans?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-27T17:27:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectral-comparisons-of-growth-lights.18898/post-205824>

Welcome. You need a human pic up as it is a forum rule. Please adjust it.
