# Thread: Electricity at night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lisbeth — 2016-12-28T11:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-205851>

hi

Does anyone here turn off their electricity supply at night (at fuse box or elsewhere?)

Have you noticed any difference in self or cohabitants?

How does that work with fridge/freezer? Any other problems?

Thank you

### Dimitri — 2017-01-12T03:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-206647>

I keep the electricity permanently off in my bedroom which is not a problem since I don't do anything else in there. Luckily my fuse box allows me to turn off the electricity just to my bedroom. It's convenient keeping the electricity off so I don't have to go up and down to the basement everyday to turn it on and off. Using a body voltage meter I got a reading of around 3 volts when lying on my bed with the power on. With it off I get a reading of .175 volts which is a big difference. EMF help center recommends getting your living spaces to .100 volts or lower, but I'm not sure if there's anything else I can do to get the reading even lower.

I've only been doing this for a couple weeks so haven't noticed any different in the quality of my sleep yet. I still fall asleep in about 20 minutes and wake once in the middle of the night and sleep for about 9 hours. I don't wake up feeling very refreshed many days but maybe with time this will change.

One caveat according to EMF help center is that when you turn off the power to one part of the house, the voltage in another part of the house could potentially increase I guess because there might be some stray voltage in your house's circuitry. I tested multiple rooms of my house before and after turning off the breaker and found that there was no difference. I would definitely test the voltage before and after of your bedrooms at the very least to make sure you're not accidentally frying someone else.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-12T03:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-206657>

Kill switches at night are wise.

### Just Breathe — 2017-01-26T23:11:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electricity-at-night.18901/post-207407>

Yes. Cut it in the bedroom and nearby rooms. Keep your kitchen one on, of course!

### Diana Ugoretz — 2016-12-28T17:27:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-205860>

In November I listened to a Ben Greenfield podcast entitled "How To Explore Your Body & Your Body’s Electrical Charge In Unprecedented Detail: The Future Of Body & Brain Imaging" which featured Jack Kruse.

I have used a tens unit, but after listening to that podcast I had a question about where it would be best to place the patches.

If I have degeneration in my L4 and L5 and also have SI joint pain especially the left SI Joint due to Ankolysing spondilitis, where would be the best place to put the patches.

If anyone has insight on that, I would appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-29T03:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-205873>

The key to getting it to work is not just where to put it........if your BUN/creat ratio is high and your D low, which is likely with that autoimmune condition a TENS wont work unless both are fixed at the same time.

### Diana Ugoretz — 2016-12-29T04:04:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-205876>

I'll have to look into the BUN/creat ratio. Have never heard of that in relationship to my condition. I was just looking it up quick and sounds like high ratios usually are indicators of renal disease and could be off due to dehydration, heart issues, or a really high protein diet, but I'm not sure how it affects my joint disease.

### Allin — 2016-12-30T03:53:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-205899>

When you live in a large metro area it is much harder to get your BUN/Creat ratio (dehydration) in the 10:1 ballpark and D >50....

### Michalis — 2017-01-08T00:00:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206290>

Hi Jack,

Lets say the dehydration issue gets fixed, how do we go about using the TENS unit and try re hydrating a disc that is failing?
Can you shed some light on what you did? I listened the podcast too and i am really keen to find out.

### prime1 — 2017-01-08T06:10:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206300>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=205873)
>
> The key to getting it to work is not just where to put it........if your BUN/creat ratio is high and your D low, which is likely with that autoimmune condition a TENS wont work unless both are fixed at the same time.

good bet it's mold - so you're TH2 dominate

### Allin — 2017-01-10T20:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206537>

In the morning session, I would drink a 16 oz of cold water, do CT on my lower body by sitting in tub from belly button on down for 30 minutes, then attach the four leads of the tens unit to my lower back bony area (1 on right and 1 on left) and to the back of my left and right mid-thighs. Set the TENs unit to 100 Hz and time for 15 minutes. Repeat a session in the evening but add a candle and meditation, Pandora has a Yoga channel which is free.

After 2-3 days, I would add to the morning session 20-30 minutes of sun to my back side as naked as possible, even when it is cloudy. I would do sun after my CT session and would add eating a can of sardines everyday. After doing this for 2-3 weeks, I would add 2-3 drops of MB to my morning cold water, 4-5 days a week.

If your back has been going downhill of 10 years, I would target doing this protocol for 10 months. In my experience you do not get better a little each and everyday. You will improve in steps and not in a linear fashion.

This template will get you started and after doing it for a couple of weeks, you will start picking up changes or modifications from your body to this basic template. Somedays you might need to do 40 minutes of CT, TENs unit for 2 - 15 minute sessions or 60 minutes in sun split into 30 minutes a side. What is this going to cost you, very very little money and mostly for the sardines to get the DHA you need. Get it done!!

### Michalis — 2017-01-11T18:10:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tens-unit-usage.18902/post-206619>

Allin, thank you very much indeed!

### JanSz — 2017-01-12T02:41:12+0200

>
> If anyone has insight on that, I would appreciate it.

Ankylosing spondylitis is a type of arthritis in which there is long term inflammation of the joints of the spine. Typically the joints where the spine joins the pelvis are also affected.

No real need to read what they say here:
[https://www.spectracell.com/clinici...center/online-library-mnt-arthritis-abstract/](https://www.spectracell.com/clinicians/clinical-education-center/online-library-mnt-arthritis-abstract/)

Get this test:
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/patient-products-mnt-hormones-intro-page/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/patient-products-mnt-hormones-intro-page/)
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)

Micronutrient Test (MNT)
$390

Get test results, supplement deficiencies and borderlines.
If you want, attach pdf file with results to a post here, I will give you my suggestions.

Similarly with this test
to be done at LabCorp or Mayo Clinic

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

# Thread: A random musing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-random-musing.18905/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2016-12-29T17:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-random-musing.18905/post-205883>

Jack, I've been going back and trying to read all of your old blogs so that I can get up to speed in the forum; and so I'm trying to be quiet and not post questions until I learn more......I hardly know any Science, but I do know some Finance, and while reading your old CT1 blog, I immediately felt as if I had read this sort of thing before.
Then I realized, this sounds like George Soros, but on Science! (Note: I find his politics intolerable, but doesn’t change the fact that he is probably the greatest pure Trader of all time.) Specifically, Soros has highlighted the fact that the theories that modern Economics and Finance are predicated upon, actually make a number of massive assumptions, based upon Newtonian Physics, in order to present an appearance of mathematical precision, and to try to bring a sense of “certainty” to what are really Social Sciences. The only problem is these theoretical assumptions frequently bear no resemblance to reality!! (See any of the numerous “bubbles” we have experienced in the last 20 years).

Uncannily, he named his fund THE QUANTUM FUND ! He chose that name because of the linkages he observed between the Heisenberg Principle and market observation and participation.

To this today, the economic “establishment" does not really take him seriously (although behavioral economics has made some progress); and he has always said that the only reason anyone pays any attention to him is because his track record is factually undeniable. But in general, even most Financial professionals still do not, or cannot, understand how he perceives markets, nor can they make it through his book, the Alchemy of Finance.

So, as I am learning about your work, I think I’m hearing the same message from you in connection with how Biochemistry has had to subtract water from proteins to conduct studies, and/or how evidence based medicine seeks to draw conclusions predicated upon generalized population data, when in reality we are all radically different (n=1).

Here is a link to a brief overview of this work if any one is interested.

_[http://www.politicsforum.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=30747](http://www.politicsforum.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=30747)_

### JanSz — 2016-12-30T19:03:44+0200

**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### Flawed — 2017-11-02T13:57:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/household-lighting-problem.18906/post-227170>

I ended up buying a screen filter for blue light from lowbluelights.com, then i made a conic structure around my bedroom light using an opaque fabric. So all the light from my standard bulb went through the filter. Worked quite well although i think it got quite hot under there.

This could have potential business promise if anyones motivated - a cubic structure with no upper surface made of blue light filtering material that encases your household lighting.

# Thread: Hello from San Francisco (37.7749° N)!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-francisco-37-7749%C2%B0-n.18907/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SFBaySwimmer — 2016-12-30T06:26:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-san-francisco-37-7749%C2%B0-n.18907/post-205904>

I discovered Jack Kruse's ideas while Googling about cold water thermogenesis last month. Ended up down so many rabbit holes, bought Epi Paleo RX, reading Nick Lane's recent book and bought Body Electric.

I'm an avid swimmer in the Pacific -- SF Bay Area waters (50 degrees currently). I'm adding in some nude sunbathing to get more UV's and also getting my blood tests done tomorrow, so I can hopefully post those results soon.

Been lurking and reading/listening to Dr. Kruse's podcasts/blogs for the past month. Just got my trimeter for my home and now I'm testing out magnetic & electric measurements everywhere. Surprising to find a wall near my entryway with a ton of magnetic field.

My sleep has been pretty good this month (except for nights with too much social drinking/blue light). It's been part of my habit to get out of bed and into the light down the street -- I stand at the Embarcadero waterfront in my sports bra and shorts and do sun salutations as people walk by in their winter wear.

I wear my blue blocking orange safety goggles around at night everywhere and also inside fluorescent blue light environs. I'm getting used to ppl looking at me funny. Although I've actually gotten a ton of compliments on my glasses -- ppl think I'm really trend forward or I'm from the future. Kinda reminds me of Ali G (Sasha Cohen's character) from the early 2000s. <-- blue blocking, not sunglasses. Gave my entire family the same glasses for Xmas.

Bad side effect, is that it's been hard to focus on work since I'm so worried about my exposure to EMFs, RFs, Blue Light during the day. I feel like half my day is taken up with cold water swimming, sauna, sun exposure. :-/

### Martha Ray — 2021-01-21T03:47:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l-tryptophan.18909/post-294799>

Thanks Cpt.Tired, I am improving my environment ....
**RE: L-Tryptophan as a supplement** For about 6 months I was taking [_Lidtke_ brand L-Tryptophan](http://store.lidtke.com/products/l-tryptophan-30capsules) every other night- because a _Cardio Ion Test_ indicated I was very Low.
I stopped about Nov 15, 2015 as I was not sure about the information I read in this article:

[Lesser Known Facts About Tryptophan Side Effects, The Case Against Taking L-Tryptophan Supplements](http://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html)
[**https://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html**](https://www.supplements-and-health.com/tryptophan-side-effects.html)

I was not sure of the motivation behind this article and I thought that it might be unfair bias but **I did stop taking** it because I was also having some nightmares on the nights I took it.

Flash forward to about a year ago I found a 100% Food Supplement, Vegetarian Tryptophan
_Item No. 797 - By Food Research and_ I've been taking it occasionally and feel it does helps my sleep and causing pleasant dreaming.

I really need to retest and study L-Tryptophan more to reevaluate. Because I only want to supplement what I really need and or only take supplements short term or as needed.
**I'd like to know what some others think of that article** **and what they might know about the pros and cons of supplementing with L-Tryptophan.**

**RE: Sleep**
Keeping room very cold, (IBT) Incline Bed Therapy and Mouth Taping at bedtime have all greatly helped our sleep in the past few years.

Sweet Dreams.

l-triptophan binds at 340 nm - the UV spectrum - if I were to take it, I would take it in the sun or run a UV light - use black out shades and expose your body to the UV light - it will knock you out - I have also been experimenting with inositol - really good - but a cold room seems to be everything - I would use 5-http instead of tryptophan - and one might also try with it P5P - which emits light like the sun...

### Martha Ray — 2021-01-22T06:07:11+0200

**Thank you Penny** "it will knock you out' as in put you to sleep, right? I don't know a good UV Light...Can you recommend one?

I have and older GoLite M2 that I used to use for SAD ..not sure if still works or it is useful.

I used to use 5-htp but never had much result from it..may alternate and try with P5P. I also take GABA product _**Zen Mind**_ sometimes especially when I travel.

I took _Food Research_ 100% food Inositol called **_Inositol Complex_** (see below) for a year or so...based on something I read in Dr. Ben Lynch's Dirty Geens book. I'm not low in it per my recent _**SpectraCell**_ test 77 in >58 range. Is it good for sleep ? Did you know if your were low or borderline in it?

..cold room I do... but I can't go to sleep if my extrimities are cold ...mostly my nose and feet. They have to be comfortable or getting to sleep is hindered. And sometimes my feet feel being cold to the bone...strange feeling. I am mildly hypothyroid not sure if that is a factor with cold feet.
**Inositol Complex** ...Not sure why they called it a Complex but it does have palm kernel and silica listed as Other ingredients.
_Item No. 455 - By Food Research [https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/products/inositol-complex?\_pos=1&\_sid=5107aab9b&\_ss=r](https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/products/inositol-complex?_pos=1&_sid=5107aab9b&_ss=r)_

-   Supports emotional well being
-   Eases stress and anxiety
-   Promotes positive mood
-   Lipotrophic factor

    Again Thanks for your comments !

### Penny — 2021-01-22T22:47:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l-tryptophan.18909/post-294848>

I just use the reptile light from Petco - but it's kind of bright - the UV light that JK recommends is really difficult to find - but my standard routine for sleep these days is: one can of oysters with garlic and siracha and turmeric/black pepper - chase it with 6 Kalamata olives (=salt) - one glass of Kirkland Malbec ($6.99/bottle and it's good) - then do not go to bed early - if you do the oysters too late (short of 3 hours of sleep) take a digestive enzyme so it doesn't screw up sleep... sometimes I add natto powder to a glass of water and drink it - but then before bed I do l-serine/magnesium citrate/inositol - all of these, I buy by the sack... just the straight powder... I was doing passion flower which is also great to knock you out - and now I have passion flower leaves (by the sack) and it appears to be too much of an effort to make the tea... but passion flower is great to take the edge off... when I get to bed, I read with a lamp that is red incandescent and I may or may not have the overhead light on - usually before bed, I play the piano and guitar - no screen time... lots of times I wake up with my hand still holding the book open... also, have a cool room and I use black out shades - the following is all micro ingredients brand...

**Micro Ingredients Pure Inositol Powder, Inositol B8 Powder, 1KG (2.2 Pounds), Strongly Supports Liver Health & Antioxidant, Super Inositol for Hair and Inositol for**

**US Origin Pure Magnesium Citrate Powder, 2 Pounds (32 Ounce), Powerfully Supports Cardiovascular Function, Relaxation and Nutrient Utilization, No GMOs and Vegan Friendly**

**Pure L Serine Powder, 500 Grams, Supports Production of L-Cystine, L-Tryptophan and Serotonin for Brain, No GMOs, No Gluten**
[Brand: MICROINGREDIENTS](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=bl_dp_s_web_0?ie=UTF8&search-alias=aps&field-keywords=MICROINGREDIENTS)

Here is the natto powder - you can actually ferment yogurt with this too - great for K2 and gut flora...
[https://www.amazon.com/Soybean-Natu...c&sr=1-1-526ea17f-3f73-4b50-8cd8-6acff948fa5a](https://www.amazon.com/Soybean-Natural-Nattokinase-Freeze-Dried-Fermented/dp/B077T6HDZF/ref=sxts_sxwds-bia-wc-rsf1_0?cv_ct_cx=natto+powder&dchild=1&keywords=natto+powder&pd_rd_i=B077T6HDZF&pd_rd_r=426f1526-04bd-4662-b698-931bd330af77&pd_rd_w=snVYX&pd_rd_wg=SvLlE&pf_rd_p=e0f994a8-a359-40a9-8917-dadca71c7184&pf_rd_r=49B3TP5G3FGMK5E5AP1D&psc=1&qid=1611348153&s=hpc&sr=1-1-526ea17f-3f73-4b50-8cd8-6acff948fa5a)

These sacks will last you a very long time...

I also swim in the ocean in the afternoon a few times per week - which is very helpful for inflammation - oh, and when I take a shower, I rub epsom salts and baking soda all over my body - you can do a foot soak if you like -

Sometimes I use lavender essential oil and progesterone cream when I remember...
**NOW Solutions, Natural Progesterone, Balancing Skin Cream, 20 mg of Natural Progesterone Per Pump, Unscented, 3-Ounce**

**It is important to get out into the sun during the day and use blue light blockers at night - no led lights anywhere - plus see the sunrise/sunset - lots of times I will actually fall asleep watching the sun set...**

I know this is an old thread but I was researching how to halt and heal the progression of cataracts. I searched my own data and came up with a quote from an article **Lesser Known Facts about Tryptophan Side Effects.**

“The biologist Raymond Peat, PhD, pointed out how an animal study on **L-tryptophan demonstrated that the amino acid may increase the risk for cataracts by disrupting the energy metabolism of the eye lens, and thus Peat cautioned against the practice of using tryptophan supplements** (Peat, Spring 2006).”

Not sure if that is true but I looking for more information.

# Thread: Very low carb for women
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Verena1028 — 2016-12-30T17:14:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/post-205912>

Because it s winter time, I do a ketogenic diet.
I try to eat mainly fish, seafood, seaweed,
very little veggies like onion.
There are positiv aspects like I am more mindfull, more clear in my head.
But my sleep and my energie sucks,
now I try to add some carbs like raw honey, dark choclat but I got really bad cravings ..
So what to do? Any experience?

### JanSz — 2016-12-30T18:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-low-carb-for-women.18910/post-205916>

> [Verena1028 said:](/goto/post?id=205912)
>
> Because it s winter time, I do a ketogenic diet.
> I try to eat mainly fish, seafood, seaweed,
> very little veggies like onion.
> There are positiv aspects like I am more mindfull, more clear in my head.
> But my sleep and my energie sucks,
> now I try to add some carbs like raw honey, dark choclat but I got really bad cravings ..
> So what to do? Any experience?

Eat is such a way that when you do blood test,
your fasting insulin is no more than 2-3

Eat in such a way that when you do blood test,
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
on that test
all fatty acids are
ideally at the mid range of respective laboratory range
practically plus minus 25% of laboratory mid range is acceptable

Eat in such a way that when you do blood test,
your (natural) Total cholesterol is more that 160, ideally 180-220

Read attached chart for your height,
get your weight from mid-range of recommended weights (dark blue area)
unless you are bodybuilder, then 15-20% for woman

Use water fast few times a month if you need loose weight.
Liver holds about 2 days worth of fuel, fast must be longer than 2 days.
After 2 days, if insulin falls down to 2-3 (sometimes it is big if), it will allow body fat to be used,
so that is the point when one starts loosing body fat.
Sometimes one must wait more than the above 2 days, (3-4) waiting for insulin to come down.
..
/////////////////

# Thread: Justine's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 48
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-01-01T04:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-205979>

I wasn't sure if I was supposed to start a journal in the forum or on a separate blog.

[Here it is on blogger right now.](http://justineoptimaljournal.blogspot.com/) Posted below:

Hooray! One Month of CT & Ending 2016 with a Cold Swim 50 degrees!!

I started being more regimented about my cold thermogenesis protocol starting in December. I was already swimming in the bay, but hadn't really done much during the month of November (with not as much sunlight and really cold weather/rains).

But I read up more on CT & weight loss and decided to give that a try instead of water fasting this month. Then I ended up reading or hearing Jack Kruse on a podcast and became more intrigued by the other health benefits of CT.

So began my journey down another rabbit hole w/ regards to blue light, EMF's, RF's, and getting enough UV every day. It's now like an obsession.

My weight hasn't really changed much, I think it was around 119lbs yesterday morning, but it can range between 118-121lbs. It's also hard for me to judge changes to my body composition. it's minute if anything.

The biggest change is probably getting really restful sleep. There are still some nights where it's hard to sleep, but I can usually pinpoint it to alcohol consumption.

I've been wearing my blue blocking safety glasses everywhere, work, at parties/restaurants, etc. I get a lot of stares but mostly people are friendly and interested in why I'm wearing them. Some or most compliment me on them, thinking they are trend forward/fashionable.  Who knew that being a dork about blue light would make me hip?

I got my trimeter a few days ago and started testing EMFs around my home and then my parents' home. It's been pretty fascinating to see what has a lot of MF. We took a BART ride for 30 min, and we were pretty much being microwaved for that entire time... yikes! 100 milligause + for 30 min. That's crazy. I can't believe it.

My car only gives off 4-7 milligause, not great but a lot better than BART.

I found electrical plugs, lights that were bad at my parents' home. Letting them know what to change. I also got all of them blue blocking glasses to wear while on their devices or at night. My mom seems to like her, the others aren't as good about wearing them.

I really push myself out of bed each morning into the cold weather to find some sunlight. It doesn't help when it's a cloudy or rainy day. I'm curious what other folks do on those day. Is that a day when I should just give up on UV light or just go to the sauna instead?

Other than that, I do feel like it's hard to fit in real work at a computer while I'm doing CT, getting sunlight (tried nude bathing at beaches 2 times already!), or grounding/hiking. I feel like this could be a full-time job being as connected to the earth.

Not sure how to balance this with needing to focus and take the time to learn Ruby on Rails and also finish up work projects. :-(

But overall, I'm grateful for finding out about Jack Kruse and for all his riddles and tidbits that got me interested in reading more about mitochondria. It really pushed me to do a lot more swimming in the bay in December. I've gotten used to the 50 degree temps now and it doesn't hurt as much in the beginning anymore. I can swim about 25-30 minutes each time before my right hand starts cramping up. I don't want to be too far from the shore when that happens in case I need to get back to safety. But I'm also trying to absorb UV light outside after the swim, but man is it FREEZING after being in the water. I may need to bring a towel outside to help me dry off and a t-shirt to help me warm up a bit. I've done pushups, squats to try to warm myself up but I still end up shivering a bunch and feeling light headed.

Thanks for coming to my Optimal Journal and I'll update it around 1 X a week. Let me know if you have any questions about my protocols or systems.

### caroline — 2017-01-01T07:18:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-205981>

You are in the right spot Justine ..... Hi!

please give Dr.K. some health history....... Your Mom and Gran and siblings

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-01-02T20:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-206059>

**My maternal family history:**

\- Grandmother was born in Shanghai (PuDong district) she was adopted so don't know anything else about our maternal history.

She never really had any health issues, very independent, maybe a bit angry/anxious.

She immigrated to HK then migrated to the US when my mom got married to my dad. She died in 2004 in her late 80s from abdominal cancer. It came very suddenly and she didn't let anyone know. She did drink CocaCola every day (cans of it) and walked all over SF to do her errands.

\- Mom was born in Hong Kong. Came from an abused childhood background since daughters weren't valued. So she was focused on success in business world at a young age hence moving to the US. She worked in banking (VP) and then CFO at various companies. Now she's retired with my dad, she does a lot of gardening work. But she also spends many hours in front of a computer screen and in front of the TV.

She is very very critical of my sister, me and my dad. We try to avoid spending too much time with her since she is such a drain on our energies. She constantly criticizes the little things and makes a scowling face. She can turn on her charm with strangers or not immediate family so most people don't understand what we have to deal with.

She had pre-diabetes before, obese/fat (not sure percentage yet), white hair since her 30s, depression/bipolar (we never know what mood she'll be in and she takes it out on everyone in the immediate family), angry/aggressive/needy are her traits. Inside, I know she can be kind but she's like a porcupine.

She had one surgery back in the late 80s for a huge uterine fibroid. She ended up getting some other fat removed as well. She's far-sighted now and uses reading glasses and can't read things up close.

\- One older sister by 1.5 years. She lives in Bali, likes being away from everything, not much internet out where she is, she goes barefoot most of the time over there. I think it's been good for her health, but she still seems to have some frenetic energy -- but that could just be leftover from her 20 years in NYC in the fashion world. She has PCOS and has had multiple fibroids removed from her uterine area. Myopia and astigmatism but she got Lasik decade ago and can see fine now. Melasma on her face.

\- Myself -- I haven't had any surgeries. But I have had ankle sprains on one ankle from a basketball accident in college, persistent neck pain from a ski accident 10 years ago, and last year injuring my knee while backpacking around Patagonia --nothing is hurting at the moment though.

Three years ago I had my eggs frozen and found my FSH and AMH levels weren't at an optimal level. I became very depressed during that time period since being a mom was very important to me at the time. I worked on improving it with acupuncture and supplementation and seemed to be able to improve it after traveling around SE Asia for 3 months (probably all that UV light I was getting and being away from internet and wifi a lot in Myanmar).

I was pre-diabetes over a year ago but was able to reverse that with diet changes (water fasting and potato only diets). Got my labs done last week and I'm waiting for the results to post them.

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-01-05T22:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-206182>

Brought my Trimeter to work yesterday and last night when at dinner/lecture. It was fun to show my coworkers the levels of MF's at their laptops and with different lights.

Levels weren't too bad at work, I also try to work right underneath a roof window so I get some UV, although it's mixed with the fluorescents in the building.

At dinner, I took out my trimeter at a fancy restaurant with a date. It was surprising to see the trimeter show extremely high MF's in the restaurant pretty much all over. At 100 or more, I used the trimeter to find a place with slightly less -- 10-30 milligausse. I didn't want to be a pain and find another place at the last minute. I had chosen this place specifically for the liver so that I could up my iron intake, too.

Food was delicious, but afterwards I really felt dehydrated and all night I needed lots and lots of RO water. Still feeling it today and I had pretty anxiety-filled dreams even though there isn't much going on in my life that should cause them. Career is going well, dating too. Working on relationships and wellness though this site and other books.

I think I need to take a swim in the bay tonight to try to counteract all the MFs. :-/ Anyone else have MF's affect them so much?

Also my date was curious about the type of MF my Trimeter is measuring and if that is indeed what's causing body harm. I assumed it was the right type of MF but I'll have to research this more.

Okay, just looked it up. It's measuring AC magnetic fields. That seems to correlate with what Dr. Kruse says is harmful for your body -- will need to look at my book Body Electric to learn more.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-03T23:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-207901>

SF has a lot of nasty nnEMF.........and good geo-magnetic opportunity

A large number of studies have identified significant physical, biological and health effects associated with changes in Solar and Geomagnetic Activity (S-GMA). Variations in solar activity, geomagnetic activity and ionospheric ion/electron concentrations are all mutually highly correlated and strongly linked by geophysical processes. A key scientific question is, what factor is it in the natural environment that causes the observed biological and physical effects? The effects include altered blood pressure and melatonin, increased cancer, reproductive, cardiac and neurological disease and death. Many occupational studies have found that exposure to ELF fields between 16.7 Hz and 50/60 Hz significantly reduces melatonin. They are also associated with the same and very similar health effects as the S-GMA effects. The cell membrane has an electric field of the order of 105 V/cm. The ELF brain waves, the EEG, operates at about 10-1 V/cm. Fish, birds, animals and people have been shown to respond to ELF signals that produce tissue electric gradients of ULF/ELF oscillating signals at a threshold of 10-7 to 10-8 V/cm. This involves non-linear resonant absorption of ULF/ELF oscillating signals into systems that use natural ion oscillation signals in the same frequency range. A long-lived, globally available natural ULF/ELF signal, the Schumann Resonance signal, was investigated as the possible plausible biophysical mechanism for the observed SGMA effects. It is found that the Schumann Resonance signal is extremely highly correlated with S-GMA indices of sunspot number and the Kp index. The physical mechanism is the ionospheric D-region ion/electron density that varies with S-GMA and forms the upper boundary of the resonant cavity in which the Schumann Resonance signal is formed. This provides strong support for identifying the Schumann Resonance signals as the S-GMA biophysical mechanism, primarily through a melatonin mechanism. It strongly supports the classification of S-GMA as a natural hazard. [https://researcharchive.lincoln.ac....EMR\_Schumann\_Resonance\_paper\_1.pdf?sequence=1](https://researcharchive.lincoln.ac.nz/bitstream/handle/10182/3935/90_n1_EMR_Schumann_Resonance_paper_1.pdf?sequence=1)

### SFBaySwimmer — 2017-02-14T18:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justines-optimal-journal.18913/post-208576>

EMF Consultant & Snow Camping Updates...

\- I went on a snow camping trip 2 weekends ago up near Lake Tahoe. It was a trial run, my fave part was building my snow trench and cave for sleeping in -- a little faraday cage.

\- Last weekend I had an EMF consultant over for 4 hours or more. Good news, my place is actually really good! No bad wiring or electrical fields. My bedroom has measurements of 0 in electrical and magnetic. I do have a neighbor's wifi router by my bed that I need to mitigate. It's not super high but it's definitely a concern. I'll look into fabric or a canopy to help with that.

\- Turns out no cell towers can get into my apartment since there are buildings blocking the way and I'm on a lower level. Phew! I know that may not last with 5G but so far it's good news.

\- The other area I need to mitigate is my wifi router (which I turn off at night mostly). It was interesting to see how much higher the radio frequency was all over my home with the wifi router on. Seeing the numbers with my own eyes made me more vigilante about turning off my wifi and getting all my devices wired in.

\- Just got my Jins night vision prescription glasses - even though it's less orange than my cheaper glasses it seems to make me sleepier at night.

\- Also got my blood drawn yesterday for DHEA and other tests to see how my supplementation is working out.

Quantum yield is a the amount of sunlight that actually reaches you. In a lot of places you have smog,cloud cover EMF domes that impede a varying percentage of sunlight from reaching you. The second part of the equation is whether you can use all the light you are getting yet. Hydration and correct lipid levels play a role here.

### ryan dawn — 2017-01-02T02:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-206019>

I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.

I have not adhered to any gradual protocol and am suddenly plunging into the cold - am I experiencing cortisol flooding and overwhelming stress as a result?

I thought that the benefits would be experientally available through my attempts yet I think I am actually doing harm to myself.

\[age 27\]
\[CT DURATION: 5-20 min\]
\[ocean temp: around 60 degrees\]

n=1 ...... always

### Penny — 2017-01-05T23:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-206185>

> [ryan dawn said:](/goto/post?id=206019)
>
> I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.
>
> I have not adhered to any gradual protocol and am suddenly plunging into the cold - am I experiencing cortisol flooding and overwhelming stress as a result?

> I thought that the benefits would be experientally available through my attempts yet I think I am actually doing harm to myself.
>
> ...ge 27\]
> \[CT DURATION: 5-20 min\]
> \[ocean temp: around 60 degrees\]

Well, look at the bright side - when Dave Asprey went straight to ice baths, he landed in the hospital with hypothermia and frost bite...

Go slow, always -in fact, I have found that face dunks alone have some amazing benefits for the weak of flesh among us...

Drink 16 oz of water before CT - eat lots of fat/protein - if you can't hack the cold, maybe you have too much omega 6 fatty acids vs omega 3 - in my house, everyone is eating a SAD - when the water heater died, everyone was quite sad taking a cold shower but not me

I eat seafood every day and could care less about cold showers - it has something to do with your cell membranes - and seafood is great for your cell membranes -

### brent-next — 2017-02-12T12:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-non-gradual-ct-damaging.18914/post-208451>

> [ryan dawn said:](/goto/post?id=206019)
>
> I live in HH, SC and have been CTing in the ocean, after which I often feel drained and weakened.

I swam in the river every day last winter at 50 deg. north. When I started the water temperatures were about 12 C and when I stopped in January they were about 3 C and and people were chipping a hole in the ice to do it.

My time in the water was typically 15 to 30 seconds. One time I stayed in 10 deg. C water for four minutes and got yelled at by the locals. This is the eastern european tradition of winter swimming.

I did start with face dunking before I tried it and my first cold water swim was in and out as fast as I could.

I noticed a wild day to day variation in how good I felt after this. Some days I felt so good I thought I must be in heaven, other days, cold and slightly tired.

If I were to do this again, I would measure my blood sugar and ketones before each swim to try to make sure I had an ATP surplus. Measure again when you get out to see how much it has changed.

I'm guessing that if I started my swim slightly hypoglycemic without a good dose of ketones to power my swim, that is when I felt tired and cold.

If you come out of the ocean feeling drained and weakened you are probably not having the intended effect. Cut your time way down and see if you can trigger the good feelings.

# Thread: Mary G - Optimal Journal

### Mary G — 2017-01-02T06:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/post-206028>

Hello,

I hope I’m doing this correctly

Basic Info: 43 yo female, married, with 2 children with very significant health challenges.

Location: currently in suburban Chicago, IL area

From: Immigrated from Eastern Europe when 16 yo; was 14 yo when Chernobyl nuclear explosion took place in close proximity (less than 15-20 miles) and was definitely strongly affected by it. My ancestors were originally from Middle East.

Family history: Grandmother and mother both hypothyroid, both of my children hypothyroid. Grandmother: heart failure; mother: sleep apnea, severe insomnia (cannot stay asleep), extremely poor energy levels, left knee meniscus tear requiring surgery 3 years ago. History of cancer for many family members.

MTHFR:heterozygous C677T and A1298C mutations

Immediate surrounding info: Work in the office setting, on a laptop, under fluorescent light. Live in 2-story home; extensive mold remediation 2 years ago in the basement.

**A brief history**

\- Born with twisted neck, straight via almost daily massage therapy treatments over a year

\- Sickly child, many colds and ear infections; adenoids removed when was 2 yo

\- Hair loss since 14 yo

\- Scoliosis

\- Goiter-like neck (same shape in all females in the family)

\- Myopia since 16 yo

\- No problem getting pregnant and carrying full term; however, both children born with small weight - 5.5 lb and 5.7 lb with health issues

\- Significant weight gain during pregnancy (40 lb) with inability to lose weight and inability to maintain any weight loss after 1st pregnancy. HCG was successful x2 cycles (2011) but again very rapid weight gain even on clean diet.

\- Difficult 1st baby with colics and other health issues who was unable to sleep at night. Completely disrupted my sleep/wake cycle. Felt like a zombie during the 1st year.

\- Completely exhausted, unable to lose weight, deterioration of executive function

\- Autoimmune thyroiditis 6 months after 2nd child was born – Thyroglobulin 57 H (norm <20). Within normal since then and TPO have been always within normal limit (but not zero)

\- Diagnosed with scalp lichen planus (autoimmune) and long telogen shedding phase of hair growth. I refused oral treatment and was given topical melatonin 0.75% topical solution in the evening and minoxidil 5% topical twice a day. This definitely helped with some hair re-growth. After reading and listening to Dr. Kruse’s take on the damage of melatonin supplementation, I stopped and hair loss resumed despite minoxidil.

\- Left knee pain on/off since 2007

\- Energy levels plummeted and very difficult to get thru the day, mental fog, further deterioration of executive function

\- Spring 2016: extremely severe back pain in L4/L5/S5 region (pain 10/10) with complete loss of daily function/pain/stiffness. Was able to get over the crisis with integrative manual therapy (IMT) practitioner.

\- Spring 2016: lab results (blood) many abnormals, including thyroid panel, liver function tests, cholesterol panel, female hormone panel, high leptin, higher A1C; nodules on thyroid ultrasound

\- Was told that have leaky gut, small intestinal bacterial overgrowth among other things

\- Have been free of gluten/dairy/soy/corn/nightshades, very few occasional grains, seafood-centric diet

**Current health concerns:**

_Metabolism, hypothyroid/HPA axis/female hormones disbalance_

\- Inability to lose weight – 5’2’’, 163 lb

\- Prior shape – pear, currently also weight gain around the middle

\- Low energy level and mental fog

\- Noticed sagging skin, lots of new wrinkles

\- Started on Cytomel 5 mcg in the morning (very low dose) to help with increasing T3

\- Irregular menstruation (delayed some months, breakthrough bleeding others)

\- Estrogen dominance, low progesterone – using Topical progesterone 20 mg cream on days 14-28 and EstroDIM Orthomolecular

_Inflammation, Intermittent low back, knee pain (on/off)_

\- Continuing integrative manual therapy, some supplements

_Digestive concern:_

\- Indigestion, have to be very careful with foo. Can’t tolerate red meat, any fried food, etc. Indigestion leads to back pain

\- Increased seafood intake – mackerel, salmon, scallops, shrimp, seaweed instead of meat

\- Taking digestive enzymes

**Current supplements:**

\- T-150 Xymogen – thyroid support

\- HPA Adapt Integrative Therapeutics – adrenal support

\- Adrenal Essence Xymogen – adrenal support

\- Vitamin D Supreme (vit D/vit K) Designs for Health

\- Vitamin C -buffered and liposomal

\- Glutathione Recycler K-57 Apex

\- CoQ10 Orthomolecular 200 mg per day

\- D-Hist Orthomolecular

\- Opti-Mag Neuro Xymogen

\- Tracheal Bovine Cartilage by Dr. Ron

\- Tumero Apex

\- Saloxin Xymogen

\- Cod liver oil by Rosita

\- Vitamin butter by Green Pastures

\- Chlorella Manna

\- Viracid Orthomolecular

\- Olive leaf Thorne

### JanSz — 2017-01-03T22:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mary-g-optimal-journal.18915/post-206094>

Hi Mary G;
**I would like you to know that I am not a doctor.**
I do not have any formal medical education,
and I newer worked in medical field.
If you would follow any of the doodles that I write,
you are doing it totally on your own.
\--------------------------------------------------------------
FreeT3(2.3-4.2)
We want to have it in upper 1/4 of laboratory range
slightly over is ok too
2.3+(4.2-2.3)\*0.75=3.725
so FreeT3 should be ok if it is within (3.7-5)
Preferably it should be supplemented after one is already on established
protocol with Lugol's and selenium.

When eating NTH, it is good idea to do it right at wakeup.
On a day that you do blood draw for FreeT3
draw blood early in the morning (7;30AM) BEFORE taking NTH.
Also around 6;00AM drink 16oz water.

Assuming that your pituitary is responding correctly,
another criteria you may want to use,
when supplementing with thyroid hormones,
attempt to get enough of it to get:
TSH~0.5
but not much more.
You should not feel overly hot all the time.
Body temperature, underarm should be 36.6C

For me one pill a day of selenium from LEF.org works fine
selenium and other items must be checked using

Spectracell Micronutrient analysis (twice/year)

after 3-4 weeks on selenium one can assume good selenium level
and start slowly, drop by drop on Lugols, must be Lugols nothing else.
Read the protocol in my earlier post, specially if you find this process difficult (at first).
Using 2% Lugol's solution one want to be on protocol of
50-300mg/day
50mg=20drops or one cc or one mL
one squeese of the rubber topper on the dropper is 0.8cc=40mg=16 drops
usually 50mg/day is adequate, but I think that you should go at least 2x of that

Using at least 50mg/day of Lugol's for many years continuously is major requirement,
think of it as part of detoxification project.

Best is to supplement with
NTH, natural Thyroid Hormone from pigs
(most Jews are allowed to do that, not sure about Muslims)
There is variety of NTH, any is ok, but make sure to chew it,
and when you start with new brand or change source, follow up with testing.
There is also Cytomel and similar T3 only (synthetic) products.
Less desirable.
Usually they work too, specially if one is (completely) not able to convert T4 into T3.
Selenium and Lugols are helpful in that conversion.

1Grain=(38-T4)+(9-T3)
T3=4(T4)
1 Grain=74(T4) or 18.5(T3)
3Grains=(222mcg-T4) or (55.5mcg-T3)

Dose of 3grains/day is relatively common.
so you may have perspective that your 5mcg of Cytomel can't go to far.
\========================================================================================
You have low DHEAs=124.3
LEF.org sels DHEA pills, but I buy them from amazon.com
try first 100mg/day
or if you are already taking some, increase by that much.
Only those pills, no creams.
goal
Women 275-400 μg/dL
when you are going to be near the upper range,
watch E1, E2 and testosterone (specially FreeT)
also we can talk here about
progesterone and cortisol.
and pregnenolone steal
From your tests I am getting impression that
progesterone is about just right
but cortisol is low. (and aldosterone is likely low--->low sodium)
We need to make double sure where you stand here.
because;
if you are low on cortisol, you will not be able
to process thyroid hormones.
Thyroid hormone must be inside the cells to work.
That may be one of the reasons why you energy is low and you are tired
and
you are loosing hairs
Eventually, you will have to do

DUTCH

test I mentioned previously.
Get ready to do this test few times.

If indeed we face situation that you have
progesterone high enough that additional increases are not justified
but
your cortisol, (specially the one at wakeup and noon) are low
then
you will have to supplement (HC or Medrol)
lets leave this open for now...................
except
make sure that you mind your light and darkness.
That is a major separate project.

Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178)

\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Your vit D looks decent, specially considering that you are in Chicago.
Likely you are using supplemental vit D.
Eventually try to wean out of supplementation
and
you need much higher levels of vit D
this
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
or 1.5x of that
Use natural sun to the max,
and Artificial lighting as above.

\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Your sodium is low.
likely you have low aldosterone. ---->>lowish blood pressure

Aldosterone is a brother of cortisol

First, try to eat more salt.
Hope,
that when we get cortisol in better shape aldosterone will follow.
If it is tough to live like that,
use Florinef,
that will require also lots of salty water.
Start with really small dose of Florinef.
Treat that as temporary project, that you will eventually wean of off it.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Best for last.

Your Fatty Acids Profile is a sick joke.

next time do better test.
do this test at LabCorp or MayoClinic.
Lets hope that you will not have to do it at Kriegers.
this test, do about every three months, until you stabilize at good levels, then less often.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

I hope that your bad fatty acids are due to your diet that is high in
fish/krill oil
seafood
if not, you may have actual problem that must be figured out.
so for now, do not eat nothing of the two above.
After 3 months or so do
Fatty Acids Profile
again.

Do not feel bad, you are not the first with super high EPA and DHA.
To find this out is impossible without testing.
Some tests are made (on purpose) in such a way that they will tell you only the truth,
but it is incomplete truth.
Well, they have to make a living somehow, right?
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your insulin is high (6.7)
until it is about 2-3
it will not allow your body fat to be used for energy
insulin can be raised by anything,
few drops of sweet juice in your plain water or Stevia can do it.
And then you stuck, fat and tired.
Make sure to not loose your morale at point like that, because this can happen to the best.

Make sure that you work your self up into about month long fast, or as long as it takes.
After the big one,
think of IF (Interminnet Fasting) and one/day meals

keep in mind what is ahead of you:

1.--3-4 days of hunger
2.--few days of bad body smell (burning old infections and other undesirable elements)
3.--it should be much easier from now on
4.--monitor your
blood pressure
temperature
weight
go little lower than your long term goal

Good luck.

Likely your consultation with
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
is over now.

### Debbieinbend — 2017-01-03T01:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/debbies-journal.18916/post-206070>

It's official I am starting my optimal journal. I joined Dr Kruse site as a member Oct 2016.
I started a ketogenic diet in Feb 2016 it took 6 weeks to adapt. I am 65 yrs so that might explain why it took so long, I am grateful that I was able to adapt. Feel the best I ever have.
My sub-optimal story is that I am hypo-thyroid (diagnosed at 30), grew up in Seattle, with much SAD. Moved from Seattle 25 yrs ago and currently live near Bend Oregon.
Have been diagnosed with osteoporosis, and high cholesterol. Took Statins and Boniva for two years. Off Statins now for only a few months.
Family history is not so great. Mother died of breast cancer at 48, Father died of a heart attack at 38 (had rheumatic fever as a child and was injured in the war) my half sister died of colon cancer at 63. One Brother has had irregular heart beat and the other has had high blood pressure most of his adult life.
My Maternal Grandmother lived until 84, Maternal Grandfather died young in accident. My Paternal Grandmother lived until 90, Paternal Grandfather died young but I don't know why.

I have just gotten my 20 gauss sleep pad, ordered the Gauntlet, eat fish two times a week, and am following the supplementation recommeded for the osteoporosis Rx. I live and work around Wifi, cell phones, and computers. Have all off at sleep time. Wear blue-blockers regularly, and seek sun everyday. Live in a small city, no smart meter, get out in nature often.

My test results that I currently have are as follows: Oct/2016

Hemoglobin AIC 108
HGB AIC Beckman 5.4
Apolipoprotein B 149
BUN/Creatinine ration 19/1
CPK 160
HS-CRP 2.30
Homocysteine 11
Vit D 34
Glucose 100
CO2 24
TSH 3.32 Thyroid Antibodies TPO 8 iu/ml,
TgAb <1
Cholesterol 279 (off Statins),HDL 79, LDL 182 , Tri 86
Bone Density 3/2016,AP Spine Tscore -4.2,Neck -2.6, Hip -3.0
\*\*Heavy metals testing results just received. High in Alum, Lead, and Mercury, and cadmium. Am going to start oral chelation ordered by local integrative medicine Doc.

**\-----------------------------------------**
Justine Lam

a4 Haplotype, San Francisco resident. Bay swimmer., Female, 37, from San Francisco, CA
Age: 37 Sex: F Joined JackKruse.com: December 2016
Dx: Melasma (upper lip and some of my cheek) for the past 1.5 years. Near sighted -6.5 in one eye, -4.5 in the other. Tired sometimes at work, neck pains
\----------------------------------------------------------
Melasma is thought to be the stimulation of melanocytes (cells in the epidermal layer of skin that produce a pigment called melanin) by the female sex hormones estrogen and progesterone to produce more melanin pigments when the skin is exposed to sun.
The incidence of melasma increases in patients with thyroid disease.\[6\]
It is thought that the overproduction of melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH) brought on by stress can cause outbreaks of this condition.
\----------------------------------------------------------
Health Goals: Focus on work (programming), Body composition, Fertility, Vision, Melasma gone
\----------------------------------------------------------
**Age: 37------->Fertility---------> hurry up**
\----------------------------------------------------------

as on my previous post, you want
FreeT3= (3.5-4.2) pg/mL
That will require you to eat selenium and Lugols
and NTH (Natural Thyroid Hormone) from pigs.
But also you will also need to have good cortisol status.
The cortisol will push NTH into cells, where it is need.
Life Extension - Super Selenium Complex & Vitamin E - 200 Mcg
[https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb\_sb\_noss?url=search-alias=hpc&field-keywords=Life Extension - Super Selenium Complex &](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss?url=search-alias%3Dhpc&field-keywords=Life+Extension+-+Super+Selenium+Complex+%26)
eat 1/day
after 2-4 weeks get
J.CROW'S¨ Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&th=1)
use 1 dropperful/day
make sure it it this one
Rarely,
some encounter problems with Lugols
if you do, stop
read manual attached to this post

To raise your FreeT3 you will need NTH (Natural Thyrod Hormone) fom pigs.
Any kind would do, chew it before swallowing.
Use it at wakeup.
After a month get tested (FreeT3 and Cortisol,serum(7;30AM)
draw blood for this tests at 7:30AM before taking your daily NTH dose.
adjust dose after seeing test results.

\========================:

IGF-1 level which is 156ng/mL
\===============================
You are starting low.
Good numbers in this area are
IGF-1=(220-300)ug/l
IGFBP3=3000ug/l
problem is that likely you have not used the desirable test.
Usually this tests are done using automatic chemolumonoscence technoque
much more reliable is manual RIA (Radioimmunoassay)

It is important and worth digging in little more here.

\===============================

I'll order those extra tests you mentioned.

I'll also start eating liver along with more fats, too. Looking forward to it. Also ordered 7-Keto DHEA supplements 25 mg to start a day, I'll measure in a few weeks to see if that's the right amount.

### JanSz — 2017-01-06T21:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lifeextension-labs-thus-far.18921/post-206244>

DHEA is an active hormone.
7keto-DHEA is different hormone than DHEA.
7keto-DHEA is known to be thermogenic, you may possibly feel warmer using it and be able to shed few pounds (maybe).
You will not raise DHEAs by eating 7keto-DHEA.

I do not know much about 7keto-DHEA, could use help.

You can find DHEA on simplified chart here (right side, blue):

You can find DHEA & DHEAs here.
There is also (I hope) 7keto-DHEA
but I do not know which one it is.

Oh, JanSz, I've been googling around for the right IGF-1 manual Radioimmunoassay test. I can't seem to find which company provides that, do you know of any?

### JanSz — 2017-01-12T01:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-lifeextension-labs-thus-far.18921/post-206638>

> [SFBaySwimmer said:](/goto/post?id=206628)
>
> Oh, JanSz, I've been googling around for the right IGF-1 manual Radioimmunoassay test. I can't seem to find which company provides that, do you know of any?

The requirement about Radioimmunoassay type of testing comes from
Thierry Hertoghe The Hormone Book 2nd ed, page 58
He says that IGF-1 must be done using Radioimmunoassay, because chemoluminescence is not accurate.
(Assume) that IGFBP3 is ok any way it is done.

Really you want only IGF-1 and IGFBP-3.
But I had it done (2014) using:
Growth Assessment Test Group X (Growth Hormone, IGF-I, IGF-II, IGFBP-3)
Test Code 500901 ... CPT Code 83003, 84305, 83519, 83519
\===============================================================
Very recently LabCorp changed their website.

LabCorp have Esoterix division (Endocrine Sciences)
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------
I just went on LabCorp site and cannot find Group X
plus
I was not able to get IGFBP-3 done at Esoterix (Radioimmunoassay)

500632 Growth Hormone, RIA (Endocrine Sciences)
503660 Insulin-like Growth Factor 1 (IGF-1), Pediatric With Z Score (Endocrine Sciences)
141770 Insulin-like Growth Factor 2 (IGF-2) (Endocrine Sciences)

\------
Insulin-like Growth Factor-binding Protein 3 (IGFBP-3)
TEST: 140152
CPT: 83520
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------
To run your GH protocol you need just thise two, IGF-I, IGFBP-3
plus you need to know your goals.

Goal is to achieve
IGF-1~(300-350)ng/mL (men)
IGF-1~(220-300)ng/mL (women)
IGFBP3~3mg/L

If you can't get IGFBP3 exactly, then observe (IGF-1)/(IGFBP3) ratio, that would be (for me=men)
300/3=100, 350/3=116
Must be something really similar ti that for women.
At this time I do not remember on what page that was written, leave it for latter.

\--------------------
You really need the two tests,
but,
you must oblige your doctor.
I got above as described. Your doc may want something else.
He writes script for GH, he says what kind backup he wants.
Norditropin is really handy with its pen.

/////////////////

### MSH — 2017-01-03T23:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol-and-depression.18922/post-206100>

I don't drink much, maybe 3-5 times a year on special occasions and usually only a couple of glasses of wine. But, for the next 3-5 days afterwards my mood is very low and I feel very irritable. Why would such a small amount of alcohol cause such a strong reaction? It seems to me that this would be evidence of something being way out of whack! But what is out of whack or what to do about it ( other than not drinking) eludes me. I'm more concerned about its profound effect on me and what that says about my health that I need to correct than I am about drinking alcohol as I can do without that. Thanks to anyone who has any ideas.

Perhaps CT would help. It teaches the mitochondria to uncouple, and uses the heat generated to spin the ATPase separate from the ETC.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T18:08:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alcohol-and-depression.18922/post-207004>

Some of my friends ask me: if Glyphosate is in say.... your wine.... is it safe to consume?
I sold all my Cali wine because every bottle I tested including the Organic stuff had it present.
What is the problem fundamentally with glyphosate? It replaces glycine in proteins and collagen is the number one protein in your body (71% by volume) that uses glycine. Glycine is unique among amino acids in that it is achiral. Chiral amino acids offer the ability of cells to polarize sunlight. Glycine is used in places in cells where we do not want to use the Faraday effect of polarization so this changes optical signaling. Glycine can fit into hydrophilic or hydrophobic environments, due to its minimal side chain that contains only one hydrogen atom. Glyphosate is chemically unusual and very different than glycine because of its "achiral ability" because glyphosate's nitrogen atoms chelate to surrounding metal atoms in proteins ruining their stereochemistry and tertiary and quaternary structure. When this pestacide's nitrogen binds to the copper ions in collagen then all versions of collagen's sterochemistry becomes fixed atomically even when sunlight hits it and the protein loses its ability to become piezoelectric and release light or a DC electric signal to water shells around collagen. This stimulates ubiquitin marking and lead to many problems in tissues. This bonding also affects the protein side chains electric fields and this ruins the Faraday effect in tissues and tissues loose their optic-magnetic coupling of growth and metabolism pathways. The amino acid glycine does not chelate metal atoms and this allows sunlight to be trapped in cells and have that light polarized in a specific way that cells use for signaling. Any food with glyphosate residue cause the colllagen in those cells not to transfer energy to the EZ and it loses its ability to remain piezoelectric. For those who do not know collagen is the major N-type semiconductor in humans, so when glyphosate replaces glycine all proteins where this replacement occurs under go dielectric collapse. This means energy leaves drop in these tissues because of the effect on the exclusion zone of water which is dielectric crystal. This causes you to have a lower redox evne if you were naked on the equator with no clothing on. This is something most food guru's whiff on. Too much physics for them to explain.

# Thread: What does CT make me more tied?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lydz — 2017-01-05T08:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206150>

I have CFS. I started CT in Sept swiming at a cold lake 1x per week. Overall I feel better but as it's gotten colder I notice I'm exhausted after my 2o min in the water. I did the polar bear swim here in Vancouver Jan 1st in the ocean. With the frigid air temp it's the coldest I've been. Water was 7 degrees C. I slept for 2 days after. It felt like post workout exhaustion. Why is this? It's hard to get in the cold water knowing I'm going to be out of it for the next 2 days even if the next 5 after are great.

### caroline — 2017-01-05T09:59:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206152>

Did you follow the C/T protocol?

### Lydz — 2017-01-05T16:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206160>

No because I started months before discovering this blog (which was about a month ago). There is a lot of information here to process! I have red this blog every day since I found it and still feel like I don't know much!
I used the wim hof method. I started with cold showers every day and worked up to swimming in the lake. I combined that with his breathing techniques. I've notice I've got more neg effects as the outside temp dropped.

### DLO — 2017-01-13T21:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206788>

Lydz why dont u simply try doing CT in the bathtub during winter months I just measured my cold water at 48F all you really need is 55F.
Which lake are you using?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-14T00:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206795>

Start with water that is warmer........and then go lower Lydz.

### freesia — 2017-01-14T01:08:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206796>

Agreed...you might be pushing yourself harder now than you're able to adapt to those cooler temps. I have me/cfs and it took me a long time...gentle and consistent is the way.
Best thing I did btw and I think there's more to BAT that we know yet....too much focus on diabetes obesity - possibly because that's where the funding is - and not to begrudge those areas. But perhaps not enough research yet on BAT and energy metabolism in general....and immunity. IMHO.

### Lydz — 2017-01-14T06:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-ct-make-me-more-tied.18925/post-206801>

Thanks for the advice guys! I was using Buntzen lake. I will switch to the bath tub, measure the water temp and go slower.

# Thread: Hello From 44.5192° N, 88.0198° W aka Green Bay, WI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-44-5192%C2%B0-n-88-0198%C2%B0-w-aka-green-bay-wi.18927/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### the lovely brenda — 2017-01-06T06:06:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-44-5192%C2%B0-n-88-0198%C2%B0-w-aka-green-bay-wi.18927/post-206201>

Hello my name is Brenda! I am 37 and have been suffering external and internal hemorrhoids since I returned from Gerald Pollack's conference on the Physics, Chemistry, and Biology of Water in Sofia, Bulgaria in October. [http://www.waterconf.org/](http://www.waterconf.org/) I drank too much red wine and not enough water while on a 12 hour flight. I then drove 9.5 hours to get to a conference on Hydrogen in Ohio and have been in lots of pain since. I have been searching non stop for something that reduces the pain and swelling. I am probably a grade three. It used to feel like I was shitting a dozen razor blades. I am down to only shitting one to three. I take MSM three times per day to soften my stools. I have been opening my window and sunning my ass the past few days around noon time. I am wondering if neighbors can see me and are enjoying the view. I am on the second floor and not grounded.

I need to figure out what is the optimal water for me. Currently, I filter fluoridated and chlorinated city water with a Berkey counter top system. I am a college student studying Environmental Engineering with a focus on drinking water and waste water. I learn the basics of water in school. Most of what I know I have learned from volunteering at conferences like Tesla Tech ExtraOrdinary Technology Conference in New Mexico [http://teslatech.info/ttevents/prgframe.htm](http://teslatech.info/ttevents/prgframe.htm) or the US Psychotronics Association Conference in Chicago [http://www.psychotronics.org/conference.php](http://www.psychotronics.org/conference.php). I am going to be transfering to studying cellular molecular biology in fall.

I followed Dr. Terry Wahls and her mitochondria stuff since the beginning because so many of the moms of my friends growing up had MS. I practiced sun gazing while I lived in NYC back in 2008. I used it to get off all antidepressants I was on for 13 years and significantly reduced my asthma medication.

I am a fluoride activist and appreciate Jack's simple break down of fluoride. I was one of 5 mothers who petitioned EPA in November along with Food & Water Watch, Organic Consumers Association, American Academy of Environmental Medicine, International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology, Moms Against Fluoridation, and Fluoride Action Network. [http://fluoridealert.org/articles/epa\_fluoride\_petition/](http://fluoridealert.org/articles/epa_fluoride_petition/)

I delivered my last child at home having an orgasmic birth. I potty trained him from birth and he was out of diapers by 3.5 months. I write lots of complaint letters about school food, cosmetic pesticides, and fluoride in drinking water. I volunteer at an indoor grow space and work under LED grow lights. The lights seem to make my skin feel strange. We raise perch and mixed greens salad mixes. I like to do experiments with different types of water and grow plants to compare how tap water differs from vortexed tap water programmed with special frequencies. This is the device I use [http://junglesecretwater.com/](http://junglesecretwater.com/).

One day I hope to have a flying Vision VTOL by Mike Waters for my transportation device and to be disconnected from the current power grid system. [http://cureforglobalcrises.weebly.com/vision-vtol.html](http://cureforglobalcrises.weebly.com/vision-vtol.html)

I also want to reverse all my allergies and to have an ass that is healed up.

I like to teach others and I enjoy meeting new people. This is my facebook. [https://www.facebook.com/thelovelybrenda](https://www.facebook.com/thelovelybrenda)

# Thread: How concerned should I be of lightning striking my earthing rod?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-01-07T08:43:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206259>

Will my earthing system attract lightning to an extent that I should be worried?
The grounding rod has a 100kOhm resistance built into it and possibly also the earthing sheet aswell.

I am sleeping in a tent in a forest lying on an Earthing bed sheet connected to a grounding rod that is connected to the ground. I am concerned that the grounding rod will attract the lightning significantly. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Once, I was caught in a thunderstorm and I immediately disconnected the earthing product from the grounding rod and ran to my home 10 minutes away. This was an insanely unpleasant experience and I am now very terrified of getting struck by lightning. I know the odds of getting struck are very small but that assumption is based on a sample of people mostly not connected to the earth.

Location: Denmark, northern Europe, and yes it is cold, -13 degrees Celsius the other day

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T23:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206421>

I would not worry about it that much. It could be an issue but I would imagine in your location I seriously doubt it is a huge risk

### Inger — 2017-01-10T17:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206517>

I would just watch the weather news and it is very seldom lightnings anyways and when there are some on the way the weather forecast will always tell
then those thunderstormnights you could sleep inside in your home

You are one lucky guy to sleep like that in the woods  How does it feel?

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-01-11T06:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-206600>

I feel extremely well rested the following day, that is however, if my cortisol levels have not been spiked due to the fear of the weather conditions such as lightning and heavy storms.
I can truly recommend it!, I feel so in spirit the following day

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-02T20:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-concerned-should-i-be-of-lightning-striking-my-earthing-rod.18929/post-236158>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=206259)
>
> Will my earthing system attract lightning to an extent that I should be worried?
> The grounding rod has a 100kOhm resistance built into it and possibly also the earthing sheet aswell.
>
> I am sleeping in a tent in a forest lying on an Earthing bed sheet connected to a grounding rod that is connected to the ground. I am concerned that the grounding rod will attract the lightning significantly.
>
>
>
> Once, I was caught in a thunderstorm and I immediately disconnected the earthing product from the grounding rod and ran to my home 10 minutes away. This was an insanely unpleasant experience and I am now very terrified of getting struck by lightning. I know the odds of getting struck are very small but that assumption is based on a sample of people mostly not connected to the earth.
>
> Location: Denmark, northern Europe, and yes it is cold, -13 degrees Celsius the other day

If you don't sleep in an area that's particularly exposed to lightning, and where the rod is the highest object, then the rod itself will not attract lightning more than any other object, like a tent, bushes or trees. Lightning is attracted to the highest objects, whatever they are.
Rather than getting a direct hit, there is much greater concern of being injured or killed by ground currents from lightning striking nearby. That's how most livestock and people are killed by lightning. The only protection from this is good insulation from the ground, however that also negates the purpose of grounding.
It would also be important to have a 1 mA fuse in the connection to the rod, but this does of course not protect from currents from lightning that strikes close.

[http://www.aztrail.org/lightning\_safety.pdf](http://www.aztrail.org/lightning_safety.pdf)
