### brandie — 2017-02-10T07:19:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-208353>

As far as tanning beds go the salons don't seem clear on what the percentages are for each bed. I believe all beds have UVA and UVB. I am almost certain that the beds they say are "all UVB" are actually about 7-11% UVB, the rest being UVA and maybe a little UVC, maybe. The US regulated tanning salons in the last few years. Started charging tax and limiting allowable minutes spent in each type of bed. I own a tanning canopy for home use, I couldn't find a bulb stronger than 9% UVB. During the process I learned a tanning bulb needs the ambient temperature to be near 80° F to be optimal. See even light needs optimal. So tanning in a bed after someone else just used the bed will give stronger results than if the bed was "cold".

### Just Breathe — 2017-02-13T19:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uva-vs-uvb-the-tanning-salon.18951/post-208522>

Hi Brandie, I agree that salons are not clear at all on percentages! Their staff is usually missing this information. I learned from one educated staffer at a tanning place and take that knowledge with me. An "Open Sun" tanning bed is 100% UVA. Anytime they tell you its "got the light that bronzes you and you don't burn from it" that's code for UVA. If they tell you this has more of the light that "burns" you, it has UVB. I also totally agree that using a bed after someone else will give stronger results! I went to a place over the weekend and they had had back to back users in the bed, ten people in a row. It was too hot for me to even touch with my skin! It felt like a microwave in there and I declined to actually tan in that bed. Peak tanning time in the year seems to be February-March...spring breakers getting a "base tan."

any thoughts would be greatly appreciated!

# Thread: Will all worlds/ planets support Earth like  life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-all-worlds-planets-support-earth-like-life.18952/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-14T05:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-all-worlds-planets-support-earth-like-life.18952/post-206800>

Life is built on Earth to be built far from equilibrium.

Might life have an equilibrium bias on other worlds?

Might life still be asleep in a palce like Jupiter waiting to awaken when the planet stops spiinign so fast?

Before there is life, there must be structure. Our universe synthesized atomic nuclei early in its history from exploding stars. Those nuclei ensnared electrons to form atoms. Those atoms agglomerated into galaxies, stars, and planets. At last, living things had places to call home. We take it for granted that the laws of physics allow for the formation of such structures, but that needn’t have been the case.

Over the past several decades, many scientists have argued that, had the laws of physics been even slightly different, the cosmos would have been devoid of complex structures.

This tells us that the charge differential present on stars and planets alter the scale of the forces. As the scale of things are changed the physical laws of nature also become dynamic. Most of us fail to relaize this because on Earth scale is perceived by our five senses as relatively constant.

Is the same true in our cells at the mitochondrial level? How about at a subatomic scale on the inner mitochondrial membrane? My April 2016 webinar explored this in some detail for members.

Astrophysicists have discussed the fine-tuning of worlds as scale changes so much that many people take it as a given that our "observable universe" is preternaturally fit for complex structures. Even skeptics of the multiverse concepts (me included) accept fine-tuning at some level; On Earth I think fine tuning is changed by scale. What happens at the cosmologic scale in nature is not equivalent that what we observe at the subatomic scale. This implies the laws of nature are dynamic and plastic. These scientist simply think it must have some other "physical explanation". Is this true?

But in fact the fine-tuning has never been rigorously demonstrated by experiment, it has just been assumed by cosmology.

We do not really know what laws of physics are necessary for the development of astrophysical structures, which are in turn necessary for the development of life. Recent work on stellar evolution (what I call speculation), nuclear astrophysics, and structure formation suggest that the case for fine-tuning is less compelling than previously thought. A wide variety of possible universes could support life. Our universe is not as special as it might seem.

The first type of fine-tuning involves the strengths of the fundamental forces of nature in working stars. If the electromagnetic force had been too strong, the electrical repulsion of protons would shut down nuclear fusion in stellar cores (assuming you believe this), and stars would fail to shine because no light would be created. Without light no life could evolve.

If electromagnetism had been too weak, nuclear reactions would run out of control, and stars would blow up in spectacular explosions. Could this situation be how matter was originally created as the scale of the universe changed? If gravity had been too strong, stars would either collapse into black holes or never ignite. the point is the forces of nature determine thermodynamics of matter by determining size and shapes of things. All size and shape tie to frequencies because all matter vibrates at a certain frequencies. It turns out light also vibrates too. Light and matter that form resonances tend to interact. In this way light binds things made up of charged particles to make things made of complex structure.

Light is critical to life and biology:

The allowed strengths for the gravitational and electromagnetic forces depend on the nuclear reaction rate, which in turn depends on the strengths of the nuclear forces. If the reaction rate were faster, stars could function over an even wider range of strengths for gravitation and electromagnetism. Slower nuclear reactions would narrow the range. What do all these variables mean for life? The April webinar of 2016 examines these implications.

In addition to these minimal operational requirements, stars must meet a number of other constraints that further restrict the allowed strength of the forces. They must be hot. The surface temperature of a star must be high enough to drive the chemical reactions necessary for life. In our universe, there are ample regions around most stars where planets are warm enough, about 300 kelvins, to support biology. In universes where the electromagnetic force is stronger, stars are cooler, making them less hospitable. Stars liberate many frequencies of light.

In nature, UV light builds pressure photoelectrically by storing energy in a cell structure; pressure builds during this storage and heat (IR) is emitted when electric and magnetic forces are confined to a tight space. Cells have things that are "tight spaces" like a mitochondria. When mitochondria are energy efficient in a cell, they become more capable of confining light using the electric and magnetic charges in mitochondrial membranes and spaces. This is why healthy cells release less light and why overly stressed cells release more ELF-UV. We know that light slows down in a dense media and density links to buoyancy in a cell. **Snell’s law says and proves these relationships exist in nature. That law is important evidence of light being used as a wave and not as a particle. Usually when light is used as a wave its frequency must be lower than we see in the ultraviolet range. This means light is more "wave like" as it is red shifted and more particle like when it is ultraviolet and purple shifted. In the visible spectrum, the lowest frequency of light is red and infrared. 42 % of sunlight is IR-A light. Most of the critical proteins of the inner mitochondrial membrane are red light chromophores. So is water.
_**
It is interesting that fat oxidation by mitochondria, creates protons, infrared light, CO2 and water. Those products react on the surface of mitochondria inside of cells to change each other's phsyical characteristics by changing the scales of the atoms that make them up. It appears that respiratory proteins shrink most when our surfaces are absorbing ultraviolet light and out mitochondrial matrix is emitting infrared light. Infrared light changes water’s physic characteristics and makes it more dense. Liquid water has more density than ice, hence why ice floats in liquid water. As a result, ice also has less light energy sotred in its lattice. This is also why ice reflects sunlight to create the albedo effect at the poles. This physical change is how Snell’s law works in our cells because water density is linked to its refractive abilities to light. This simple small alteration, changes how light can interact with it at our surfaces. A long term stressor overwhelms the regenerative side of the coupled feedback system and this results in energy loss and because energy cannot be stored. **When energy cannot be stored in a cell, the energy must be thermalized (heat).** This is why all mammals are warm blooded. This happens routinely in mammals mitochondria. This heat provides the scale and complexity that life on Earth can operates at.

_**Life is clever.**_ It has figured out how to transform electrons out of everything we eat and keep them under control in cells until we need their energy. Electrons must flow in order for energy to be gained elsewhere. Electrons can be stored in tissues in semiconductors, but when they are stored they cannot act like a wave. They only have particle like behavior. When the electron leaves the tissue its energy has to be thermalized to affect energy flows elsewhere in a cell to be used to power physiologic work of living.**_**

# Thread: Improving In Home Grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-in-home-grounding.18955/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bionaut — 2017-01-14T18:47:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-in-home-grounding.18955/post-206825>

Does anyone know what labs are useful for determining a positive/negative effect from grounding using a pad?
I only have anecdotal evidence from sleep.

I own a grounding mat that I don't use. The wire that connects this to a stake out my window is pretty dinky.

I was thinking about using shielded cat6 cable to connect from the mat to the stake outside

Cat 6 is used in high emr environments to prevent signal degradation in datacenters.

The individual wires are insulated then there is a second jacket. I was thinking about connecting the interior wiring to a grounding rod and earthing mat. Then taking the exterior either braided or foil jacket and grounding that to a separate stake. In theory this should reduce the ambient emr running through the cable by having a grounded shield enveloping the wiring. Foil jackets provide heavier shielding that braided forms but also have a higher resistance which could be a problem for the ground.

### JanSz — 2017-01-15T21:51:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-in-home-grounding.18955/post-206874>

> [bionaut said:](/goto/post?id=206825)
>
> Does anyone know what labs are useful for determining a positive/negative effect from grounding using a pad?
> I only have anecdotal evidence from sleep.
>
> I own a grounding mat that I don't use. The wire that connects this to a stake out my window is pretty dinky.
>
> I was thinking about using shielded cat6 cable to connect from the mat to the stake outside
>
> Cat 6 is used in high emr environments to prevent signal degradation in datacenters.
>
> The individual wires are insulated then there is a second jacket. I was thinking about connecting the interior wiring to a grounding rod and earthing mat. Then taking the exterior either braided or foil jacket and grounding that to a separate stake. In theory this should reduce the ambient emr running through the cable by having a grounded shield enveloping the wiring. Foil jackets provide heavier shielding that braided forms but also have a higher resistance which could be a problem for the ground.
>
>
> Any of you electrically inclined folk out there, your thoughts are welcome.

My electrical experience with grounding.

My house is about 60' long.
On one end comes electrical power.
There is a ground there.
Third wire everywhere in the house is grounded thru that ground.
When I was planning to have a spa I need 100A outlet with GFCI on the other side,
straight from main box, up to the attic above 2nd floor, across the attic, then down, then about 20' out.
I got about 100 feet
4/3 With Ground Electrical Wire

Hooked it up to main box on one end
hooked it up to sub-box on the other end
hooked in to GFCFI

The GFCI was tripping.
So I stuck ground wire similar to what I had at the main side.
The GFCI was tripping.
I was tired at that time, and did not want to make to many trials any more.
Got 10 feet long 1"dia copper bar (plus my son with the sledge hammer to get it in).
It worked.

My city electrical inspector was wondering about my installation.
Way over of what he sees every day.
I told him that I did it all my self with advice from my electrical friends.
The problem was, I was working at the time tending to Nuclear Power Plants and so did they.

What is the lesson here?

One of them is that good ground counts in a major way.
And I had lousy ground at the main box.
Few years latter I got a house generator and a deal to replace my main box.
I agreed under condition that contractor will give me serious grounding bar (while I was watching him doing it).
Now I am good.

//

Hi Tammy ....

Seafood is the most important. Cans of sardines and mackerel and salmon are great - you could mash them up.

eggs, liver wurst, pate

can you take cold showers?

good bottled water is very important. You will save money by buying lots of sardines!

### caroline — 2017-01-18T01:41:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207030>

Good Morning Tammy ..... I am in Oz

The leptin reset is very simple.....

Have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising - outside sitting in the dirt and looking at the sun! ......naked!

leave 4 hours between meals and at least 4 hours before bedtime.

Drink tons of good water

Eat lots and lots of seafood

Get outside as much as possible - without your glasses on. Think of your body as your solar panels! - so bare feet on the ground and looking towards the sun...

Blue blockers

limit computer time /phone..... turn off wifi

all electronics out of your bedroom?

can you sleep outside in your garden?

Can you get in the ocean everyday? walk in the surf at sunrise ....and sunset?

Do you garden?

### Inger — 2017-01-18T18:36:36+0200

When the body heals the excess weight will come off withouth effort. I think it is wrong to concentrate on losing weight. Becoming healthy and increasing energy is what we need to concentrate on!
The lucky ones become fat, do not forget this. It is for a reason the body accumulates fat. The thin ones that are sick might do worse! I would just connect to nature, do all the changes possible to gain energy back, and the body will not need the fat anymore.

I would not advice a obese person to diet or fast......
Instead, to ground, avoid technology, tan (as much as possible and preferrably nude!), drink plenty of good water, and eat according to seasons and what is available around. Wild foods are magic! To me they are the most energizing ever
Like wildcaught fish, herbs, berries, whatever grow and moves around you
and... relax
and.. love.. and kiss.. hug.. laugh, cry.. and skin contact to a beloved human.

Do not be scared Tammy  It will be a beatiful journey, I promise!
To help the mental part you copuld buy and read the monk who sold his Ferrari, it is a great read to come back to when life feels tough  It is super cheap on amazon  It was the book that helped Jack change...

> I have worked from home on the computer and phone for the past 17 years and have just recently figured out that
> this si why I am sick. I am in the first stages of cleaning up my environment and turning off the computer and phone
> every minute that I am not using them.
> I have wanted to stop working on the computer altogether but being so sick, I cannot go get a regular job and really
> need to keep my place to live.

You can use corded internet and switch off wifi, and use f.lux to avoid bluelight (it is a free download) and use blueblockers in front of the screen ( they are very cheap for less than 10 bucks on amazon)
and you can buy a long cord (also cheap) for internet and use the computer in the garden as you tan and ground!

> It will happen eventually!
> I was outside in the sun for 30 minutes when you responded to my post!
> I started yesterday and will do it every morning along with incorporating the rebounder and qi gong outside on the grass barefoot.
>
> I have been craving the sun and being outside so this is perfect timing.

Nice   great start

> I am going to get enough food for 3 days to start which will include fish, eggs, sweet potatoes, salad fixins and a few veggies
> and then read more and go from there.
>
> I am a little concerned about eating meat with the Diverticulitis being so bad right now but willing to eat the fish
> as much as possible.
>
> I've been praying for a remedy and this seems so right. I am so happy to have found you!

I do not know what to eat for diverticulitis but I suppose when you eat natural foods and use sun and avoid nnEMF it sure will heal eventually  If you are scared of meat just use seafood, it is fine! But I do not think meat is any dangerous.... just try to get a good source, grassfed or game like moose, or lamb/sheep is mostly grassfed too.

I often eat pollock when I am out of money. It is very cheap and I love it! I buy it frozen. That is the cheapest fish here, and it is wildcaught. I eat it raw and cooked. Raw I marinate it in white balsamic vinegar, onions, grated fresh ginger and add some curcumin, black pepper and olive oil. Delicious!  Or cook it in a little water with onions, and add lots of butter and curcumin and black pepper and salt.

Herring and mackerel are so great too if you can get them! Fresh is best but frozen is fine if you cant get any other or budget is too tight. Remember light is the most important thing to get right

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-18T22:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207061>

What makes us fat faster than anything? Fake blue light day and night. You can use blue light flicker to see if it changes your stress response and your BUN and creatine ratio and makes you fatter. See what effects it has on your eyes in your mito-hacks. You might be shocked at what you find. You’ll see less dermal pooling of blood, massive effects on melanopsin regeneration, and centralization of blood plasma and a rise in Blood Pressure and a drop in vitaminD3 levels even when you are naked in Florida sun. You can use your computer screens dimming mechanism to bio-hack the effect on yourself for very little money. [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/artificial-blue-light-dehydrates-your-cells-jack-kruse?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/artificial-blue-light-dehydrates-your-cells-jack-kruse?published=t)

### JanSz — 2017-01-18T22:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-st-petersburg-florida.18960/post-207063>

\[qu ote="Tammy Rogers, post: 207054, member: 19702"\]I recently thought about using the Pedialyte and got 4 bottles of it yesterday as I want to use it for the electrolytes.
I am going to drink it in **between meals** **until I feel I can not eat** and be calm because when I don't eat, I get really dizzy and shaky.

We are talking electrolytes here. That is:
sodium
potassium
magnesium

This is not a food, you do not need a lots of it.
Definitely you do not want drink tons of water, even if some of it contain electrolytes.
Be ready for a fight that theoretically last 2 days, but 4 days is not out of the question.
It should get better latter.
All mostly psychological.
If you are religious, that will buy you a lot.
You fight with your self.
Do not feel guilty if you "fail", just pick up pieces and continue.
Tell us about it, we will root for you.
**If you are using insulin or blood pressure medications.**
**You must monitor dosing.**
Required dosing will change, sometime drastically.
Eventually, there is a good chance that you will wean out of those.
Unless you are type 1 (diabetic).

I drink enormous amounts of water with sea salt in it and it's doing me no good.

**Got a grounding cord yesterday** and am going to get some seafood today or tomorrow and start getting r done. lol

In short.
**Please do not use grounding cord.**
I posted recently my experience with grounding I had in my house.
Nothing pretty.
But, bare feet walking, laying on a ground, specially wet ground, grass or sand next to skin, far away from electric power lines, is ok.

I work on the computer all day and night for the past 17 years so I am turning my modem and phone off as much as possible
and everything off when I sleep.
**I am wearing the grounding cord when I sleep** because I live in a garage apt with a window air conditioner and the owner's electrical
EVERYTHING JUST ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE WALL.
(She now thinks I am COMPLETELY crazy fyi lol)
I am sitting in the sun every morning for 30 minutes with my feet in the ground but don't think that is going to be enough.\[/quote\]

\----------------------
**Do your best with grounding, nnEMF etc,**
**but remember sequence of importance of your goals.**
**You want to burn (your own) fat is a priority #1.**
/

I installed F.LUX and it has made a great difference so far. Dimmed my phone and do not use any lights at all unless absolutely necessary.

I am so overwhelmed by all of this. I used to be what I call "highly intelligent" but now, I get no sleep and can barely function.
I was just reading this from your Michael jackson post:
When we lose the fundamentals of neural chemistry we begin to de-evolve and see more primitive neurologic state of function.
I honestly feel I am at the point he was at-no joke. It is scaring me big time!

It all seems like a maze to me and trying to find what I need to do from first thing in the am until I go to sleep is deluding me.

I DON'T SLEEP AT ALL yet, all I want to do is sleep!
My head rings constantly and I feel like I am on the verge of an anxiety attack every time I try to concentrate (I have never had anxiety problems before) so the panic is real now.

I got the eggs, good meat and some veggies and started today when I woke up but I don't get up until 11 or 12 BECAUSE I CANNOT SLEEP so
I am not sure how to fix that.
I ordered the NOW Thyroid Energy to take in the am for energy.
I have the Vitamin C, apple cider vinegar and sea salt and am taking it every morning.
I got the grounding cord and am turning off all wifi at night and even wearing the grounding cord to bed.
Only candle light and a salt lamp at night.
Grounding in the sun as soon as I eat in the am for 30 minutes.

What else can I do to
1\. GET SOME SLEEP
2\. STOP THE RINGING
2\. HAVE MORE ENERGY?

a contributing factor.

I moved here and only know a few people and NONE OF THEM LIVE A HEALTHY LIFESTYLE
so truly looking for some new intelligent friends who want to have fun getting healthier.

I've actually sat and cried all day trying to figure this site out!

I keep saying tomorrow I will get it but 3 days in, I am still confused. My brain doesn't work the way it used to for sure!

I want to feel better right now and just need to accept that it's going to take some time and focus.

**Subforum:** The Cave

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-17T00:22:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206963>

Just listened to Jason Pralls's new podcast with DR Cowan. They get into Gerald pollocks work and how it shatters the standard heart as a pump model. Im 3 qaurters through the fourth phase so It was deffinetly good timing for me. Emjoy!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T03:27:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206974>

The problem with these "discoveries" is that they become systematized and programmed by paradigms.

The public needs to realize that "systems and programs" are for computers.... and they are not designed for living things like us. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to the sun and fully connected to Earth to work properly in nature to remain well.

We simply need to connect with nature and feel the zeitgebers..........and Mother Nature will take care of us. The Rx is simple but our brain's wants desirtes and nedds of society and culture obscure that perspective. [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/joim.12251/full](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/joim.12251/full)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T03:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206975>

> [Sun Disciple said:](/goto/post?id=206963)
>
> Just listened to Jason Pralls's new podcast with DR Cowan. They get into Gerald pollocks work and how it shatters the standard heart as a pump model. Im 3 qaurters through the fourth phase so It was deffinetly good timing for me. Emjoy!
>

One problem........Cowan model and Pollacks model requires that water's volume shrinks as it is heated for this model to work. Jason Prall rejects that. So is he airing episodes based upon science he rejects? Is that incongruent? Is is righteous. You have to decide. I already have.

### Lahelada — 2017-01-17T14:43:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206997>

Will you be elucidating (more..) on the function of the heart ?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T14:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-206998>

I think I already have.......the heart is not just a pump. The webinars have been clear that it is an electromechanical coupler of solitons phonons and excitons.

### Lahelada — 2017-01-17T14:56:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207000>

Thank you for the succinct summary. Not having been part of the webinars I had been wondering how much of the story I have. More than I trusted myself to have, but less than I should. Good to know.

### Alper — 2017-01-17T17:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207002>

I haven´t head this recent podcast with Thomas Cowan but another one he did with Ben Greenfield. I´m also familiar with the literature (Rudolf Steiner) that he is referring to mainly I think.
Jack, on the one hand you´re absolutely right - he (T.C.) is not putting human beings in contact with nature so his conclusions seem incomplete/incorrect. On the other hand he is referring to the other site of the coin - Rudolf Steiners unterstanding of medicine or rather of mankind in complex with the universe regards this from the arts of humanitis (Geisteswissenschaft in german - I think its more clear than the englisch term) and does not regard it from your view, the area of natural science (Naturwissenschaft).
Mankind evolved the same way minerals, plants and animals did. There are certain aspects that differ men from animal although we are mammals. But mankind is the first beeing to be able to think and reflect its own nature - with is nature itself. Animals can´t do that. They experience the state of consciousness (instincts/impulses) but the do not experience the state of self-consciousness (seperating the individual as "I" from the surrounding or the group they are living in).
I´m often confused when you are saying "mother nature will take care, if we couple with nature" or something like that (don´t be too picky on my quote - I think you know what I´am talking about). We are the embodyment of mother nature. Mother nature is a spiritual term (not meaning religious). Without mankind there will not be a term mother nature. This is a human realisation of its own. Nature holds the principles, not laws (laws only exist in disciplines like physics), that build our surrounding as well as us. So their is no real difference between nature and us (check the term "emergence").
It was kind of hard to couch these mental ideas in terms. I hope you get a feeling.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-17T17:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207003>

You are correct.......When I listened to this podcast it was clear to me the Dr. and the interviewer where passing through life completely blind to what really matters and focused in on what does not. This will confuse the person who is learning. It is also why I try to limit my exposure to people with half vision who develop and propagate half truths.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-19T01:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-thomas-cowan-secrets-of-the-human-heart.18961/post-207073>

Arnt half truths just dirty puzzle peices removed from the jig saw? only a single component but when cleaned and dusted off they fit nicely into the big picture.
I enjoy getting different perspectives and Im still learning and finding my way around. You half to test the boundries to know where the middle lies I suppose.
Im not really up to date with where J Pralll stands on everything. I started listening resently after I heard his interview With Jack. There seems to be more guests on his show that share commonalities with this community than not. Jack has connected important dots and synthesized his discoveries with the work of other brilliant minds who all brought to the table their own puzzle piece.

# Thread: CT and Circadian Rhythm Question

**Posts:** 3

### John G — 2017-01-18T18:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-circadian-rhythm-question.18963/post-207047>

I'm clarifying a comment I found on this forum post:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/so-you-think-ct-and-fake-light-is-bunk-huh.9780/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/so-you-think-ct-and-fake-light-is-bunk-huh.9780/)

\---------------------
Christos writes: "Jack from CT 6 and I quote '...activation of eNOS by cold actually blocks the SCN from reacting to photo stimuli to entrain circadian cycles'. Does this mean that artificial light would no longer affect melatonin signaling if one is "cold adapted'?"

Jack replies: it would...
\----------------------
I just want a clarification on this process of entraining my circadian cycle to the cold. If I do two CT sessions a week at 45 minutes...will my SCN still react to photo stimuli? Or, do I need to CT every morning to boost my eNOS in lieu of the morning sunlight stimulus? Ultimately, how would I know my SCN no longer reacts to photo stimuli and eNOS is dominating?

Thanks,

John

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-18T19:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-circadian-rhythm-question.18963/post-207052>

eNOS is thermoplastic.

Whoaaaaaa!!! Awesome. She explains this very well.

# Thread: DHA in Fillets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cap — 2017-01-19T03:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207084>

I Realize that Ideally, we want to each the whole fish, sea animal etc....

If one eats a lot of Salmon, Tuna, Sardines etc...but only has access to the Fillets (most stores and canned products are just the Fillets), are they still getting a significant amount of DHA from those sources?

### JoeT — 2017-01-19T16:30:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207095>

The only way to know is if the manufacturer discloses it, and only the higher end ones do For instance, I eat about 6 cans of Wild Planet Sardines per week. They disclose that each can contains 313mg EPA and 688mg DHA. You'll pay more for these, but you can also be much more sure as to purity.

There may be studies out there on the cheaper brands; I don't recall seeing one though.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-19T18:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207109>

Fresh fish is the key........

### cap — 2017-01-24T07:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-in-fillets.18964/post-207267>

Of course Fresh Fish is Key, along with "whole fish" is key. I was referring to less than ideal scenarios. Some Brands I use list that there are Omega 3's but don't give a breakdown at all. Others (supposed higher quality brands) from Spain may come in glass or canned, and don't say anything other than that they are fillets.

My question is more regarding is there enough DHA in fillets without skin? whether canned or not is irrelevant... Stores usually sell Steaks/Fillets and remove the skin, as if its a good thing . But can you still reap the benefits of plentiful DHA so long as you consume alot of these sources?

### Tammy Rogers — 2017-01-19T03:47:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tammy-rogers.18965/post-207086>

**I copied and pasted my info from my signature as I will delete it from my signature soon.

Age:** 53 **Sex:** F **Joined JackKruse.com:** Jan 2017

**Location: St. Petersburg Fl,** USA

**Dx:** Diverticulitis | Morbidly Obese **|** Sleep Apnea | Thyroid Disease | Chronic Fatigue | Fibromyalgia | High Blood Pressure | Extensive Scar Tissue from back injury in 1989 (4 herniated discs-they have been healed but the scar tissue pain is awful!)

**Surgeries:** 1982, Appendix Removed | 1987 C-Section | 1992 Gall Bladder Removed| 1995 C-Section |2006 Hysterectomy for Endometriosis

**Current Rx: Metoprolol |**

**Current Therapies / Protocols:
**Vitamin C, sea salt and ACV every morning**
****Soon-the NOW Thyroid Energy that is recommended on this site**
**Epi-Paleo Diet****
Sun every morning so far 30 minutes a day and increasing daily
[Leptin Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/) Having a hard time with this since I DO NOT SLEEP MUCH AT ALL.
[Cold Thermogenesis Rx](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/) starting tomorrow
**

I don't sleep much at all and do not fall asleep until I am completely exhausted or between 4-7 am (whichever comes first) so the brain fog is horrendous.
I have -0- energy level and am at the point of not being able to even walk around the grocery store.
I went to a Holistic Dentist who finally looked at me and told me it was my Thyroid so I have been using a Rebounder for 5 minutes at a time and doing Yoga every other day and they help but the soreness and swelling in my body isn't budging. I just ordered the NOW Thyroid Energy.
I only take the blood pressure medicine because I feel so sick sometimes that it's the only thing that calms my nerves. I am a calm person and do not want to take it.
I do not have insurance and am at the point of no return now.
I really need help and am praying this is what will help me.

I truly want to be able to wake up and have energy again.
I want to eat to live and change my work so that I am not on the computer all day and get all of this excess weight off of me.
I do not drink, smoke or do any drugs and want more than anything to have my clear thinking mind back again!
I am in constant pain all the time.
I have headaches all day every day. My mind is a foggy mess and for someone who likes to be clear-minded this is torture!
I hurt all over my body and feel the beginnings of carpel tunnel syndrome creeping up.

I am reading as much as I can daily and the success stories are giving me hope of feeling good again.

I hope I am not too far gone to fix this as I feel like I m slipping away mentally and physically.
My Dad, dog and brother all died in the last year so the grief is too much when the pain gets bad.

I can honestly say that I believe this is my last hope.

I have spent thousands upon thousands of dollars trying to get well and now that I have no insurance and am broke living in a garage apartment, this protocol is all I have left.

I have so much respect for Dr. Kruse.
He is so knowledgeable and I am so grateful for this platform.

Any help you can offer will be explored as I am willing to do anything at this point.

### Dimitri — 2017-01-20T05:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-for-tammy-rogers.18965/post-207133>

Hi Tammy, I was someone who had my fair share of sleeping issues with nights where I would not be able to fall asleep until 4AM. Those restless nights would only happen on an occasion for me but it could still take me an hour or more to fall asleep on many nights. I started to fall asleep faster when I began blocking the blue night at night with blue blockers but this did not fully solve my issue. What gave me consistently good sleep is when I heard Dr. K mention how you need UV light to build up your ocular melatonin, and I started making an effort to really spend much more time outside. That was when my sleep dramatically improved, especially when I would take vacations to the beach where I would fall asleep like a baby on those nights.

I see you spend 30 mins outside in the morning and increasing it which is great. I would suggest to really push the amount of time your outside and try to spend most of the day out there if you can. All in all from my experience the blue blocking only solved maybe 1/3 of my sleep problem, but the sun ultimately was the real deal.

I'm not sure what kind of thyroid disease you have but I had hyperthyroidism for a while which eventually I fixed with an increased selenium and iodine intake. Problem with me was that I supplemented with these instead of increasing my seafood intake because I was following food guru advice, but if you keep eating epi paleo you will do great.

And finally I will say that with time the weight will start melting off if you stick to the leptin rx. I lost fifteen pounds in 3 weeks and I wasn't even overweight to begin with. This was following a standard paleo diet back while knowing nothing about circadian biology before I heard about Dr. K, and I'm sure you will see great success because the leptin rx takes things to a new level.

# Thread: HIIT ideas, plans and discussion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-01-19T21:33:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-207115>

Hello ladies and gentlemen,

this shall be a thread where we can collect ideas for different HIIT workouts and also how you can vary them and progress optimally.

I begin with a simple training idea based on the 40 yard sprints which are mentioned in the Leptin FAQ.
I often have not much time to go out for that and also in winter I dont want to do that on frozen, slippy ground.
So I used kettlebell swings for the same program idea that will look as following:

10 Swings x up to 20 times / break in between as long as you need to be able to talk

\-start with 2 hands and aim for maximum power output on every rep, hardstyle

### FNJORD — 2017-01-19T23:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-207118>

Tabata squats or burpees = 20 seconds exercise as many as you can do + 10 seconds rest repeat 8 times.
There are youtube videos that have timers in them, which is useful.

### Chris James — 2017-04-28T17:13:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hiit-ideas-plans-and-discussion.18968/post-213046>

I have found that a circuit training type system works great for me. All exercises I do to failure or for 30 seconds, then rest for 10-20 seconds.

I start with pullups, then swings, dips, goblet squats, pushups on the dip bars and then kb deadlifts. I repeat this circuit 3 or 4 times.

it takes about a month to harden skin.
After that no amount of sun is too much.
Watch hydration.
Observe animals, they know when they got too much sun.

It takes a lots of effort to get the desirable levels.

Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)

But at least 1.5x above is also acceptable.

I have never met Dr Zach, but after viewing some of his podcasts/seminars online I am very impressed with the depth and breadth of his scientific knowledge, specifically of gut microbiome. He opened my eyes wide to the overwhelming importance of a healthy microbiome to a healthy body. They are literally inseparable.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-11-20T18:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restore-gut-junections-with-zach-bush.18973/post-282563>

Another study shows why docs like Zach Bush have zero understanding of how sunlight sculpts the microbiome. If he did he would never try to sell any human restore.......he's sell them on vacations to the sun. This is how Vitamin D is created in the body by UVB LIGHT exposure on the skin and NOT by oral supplementation. This ultimately leads to big improvements in gut bacteria populations. Looks like another win for Kruse Longevity Center in stomping out marketers for the public.
Skin Exposure to Narrow Band Ultraviolet (UVB) Light Modulates the Human Intestinal Microbiome
Microbiol., 24 October 2019
Participants who took vitaminD supplements and use the narrowband had no improvement in microbiome diversity.
Participants who used narrowband uvb light only had significant improvements.
Bacteria from several families were enriched in the group that did not take prior vitamin D supplements.
This is the first study to show changes in intestinal microbiome in response to Narrowband-UVB skin exposure.
This evidence suggests the existence of a novel skin-gut axis that could be used to promote intestinal homeostasis and health.
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full)

### DLO — 2017-01-23T00:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adding-wheat-back-into-diet.18974/post-207229>

Good interview with the guy who wrote Perlmutter of grainbrain fame

In the original article I also like
**The Importance of Seasonal Eating**
In his book "The 3-Season Diet: Eat the Way Nature Intended," Douillard delves into other fascinating research suggesting there may be biological imperatives to eating foods in accordance to season. Different microbes will be present in soil and plants during different seasons, and by eating certain foods at certain times of the year, you may be able to radically optimize your [gut microbiome](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2015/07/13/importance-gut-microbiome.aspx).

For example, in the fall and winter, enzymes like amylase are increased in grains. During summer and spring, amylase is decreased, and this enzyme specifically helps your body digest foods such as grains. So it may not be a fluke that grains are harvested in the fall and winter, when amylase levels are at their highest.

_"When you think about \[it\], maybe we are supposed to eat these grains at the right time of the year, as opposed to eating everything all day long three times a day in a processed form, which we can't digest,"_ Douillard suggests. _"We're part of the circadian rhythm of nature. We completely lost that … Birds fly south, whales migrate. Our survival also depends on us being connected to those rhythms of nature. Part of that is what we eat._

Yet they still say that winter is protein/fats.... doesn't the higher amylase mean we can tolerate some in the winter too?

I also eat blue corn tortilla chips pretty much every time I eat a can of oysters - it's the only way I can get them down - and have zero problems with it - I delude myself into thinking that because blue corn tortilla chips have lithium in them, it's not so bad for the ol' redox... plus, they still help me sleep so I can only assume that it's not a big deal - before you blow a gasket about the oil in the chips, I have no delusions about the oil in the oysters - that probably isn't olive oil even though they say it is...

### JanSz — 2017-03-09T16:14:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-optimal-journal.18977/post-209953>

You posted
insulin 68 pmol/L and translated to
11.33μIU/ml

[http://endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Insulin.php](http://endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Insulin.php)
translated
68 pmol/L= 9.721µIU/mL

my lab, on my reports gives insulin in uU/mL sometime and uIU/mL other times,
but at the web site it shows uIU/mL
so I assume that
uIU/mL is the same as µIU/mL

so, if your insulin= 9.721µIU/mL
it is too high
aim at 2-3

to get there you should have
over 24hr day period
long times where you insulin have a chance to fall

Intermediate fasting 24-->16/8
is one attempt at that goal
one would eat only within 8hr window, and not eat during the orther 16 hrs

Over time the 8hrs may shrink to less with a goal of eating just once a day.

In the mean time, you may want on beginning of the 8hr period eating only fats.
Fats do not raise insulin (or glucose) significantly.

\----------------------
You posted
Hemoglobin=9.4mmol/L
and translated it to 605.90g/L
that cannot be right.

I have found:
Hemoglobin leveles (which are roughly 12 to 17 g/dl)
1 g/dl = 1000 mg/dl = 1000/18 mmol/l = 55.5555555 mmol/l that does not give me much help.

Work on it until you get hemoglobin to within (12-17).

\=======================================

### JanSz — 2017-01-24T15:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cdc-study-shows-city-living-is-healthier.18978/post-207278>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=207276)
>
> CDC makes 5 billion dollars per year on vaccines. Most are done on city dwellers. Of course they have a vested interest in saying being in the city is safer.........that means more people will come there and get vaccinated by rules and laws they set up.

**Not everybody who helps you is your friend.**

Not everyone who shits on you is your enemy

A little bird was flying south for the Winter. It was so cold the bird froze and fell to the ground into a large field.

While he was lying there, a cow came by and dropped some dung on him.
As the frozen bird lay there in the pile of cow dung, he began to realize how warm he was.

The dung was actually thawing him out!
He lay there all warm and happy, and soon began to sing for joy.

A passing cat heard the bird singing and came to investigate. Following the sound, the cat discovered the bird under the pile of cow dung, and promptly dug him out and ate him.

Morals of the story:
(1) Not everyone who shits on you is your enemy.
(2) Not everyone who gets you out of shit is your friend.
(3) And when you're in deep shit, it's best to keep your mouth shut!

When your body knows it cannot produce heat well, or that it is in a poor energy state, those fears are justified.
When food is the only tool (crutch) you know, you'll find that most problems can be remedied or at least soothed by it.

### JanSz — 2017-01-24T19:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cdc-study-shows-city-living-is-healthier.18978/post-207287>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=207283)
>
> And I've found the fears of hunger and cold can disappear while outside barefoot.

Walking barefoot when we step on a small stone, there is a reflex where we can lessen the load on that area to lessen possible injury.
I could use a help.
I have a problem getting used to walking barefoot over frozen grass (sharp rigid pointy blades) or snow with ice on top.
Basically I am not able to stop worrying that I may get cut.
Specially after a while on cold snow or ice, I feel my feet less, any reflexes are delayed, and I am afraid of injury.

### nonchalant — 2017-01-24T21:22:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cdc-study-shows-city-living-is-healthier.18978/post-207294>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=207287)
>
> Walking barefoot when we step on a small stone, there is a reflex where we can lessen the load on that area to lessen possible injury.
> I could use a help.
> I have a problem getting used to walking barefoot over frozen grass (sharp rigid pointy blades) or snow with ice on top.
> Basically I am not able to stop worrying that I may get cut.
> Specially after a while on cold snow or ice, I feel my feet less, any reflexes are delayed, and I am afraid of injury.

I don't worry about frozen grass (probably because the sunlight is already softening it, this far south), but I just can't get used to the cold of snow under my feet, though I've tried it during our infrequent snow days for several years. I've been wondering if the problem is that snow insulates my feet from grounding. If I scrape the snow away, I can stand in that spot ok. Since I'm away from the city now, I haven't seen much ice on the ground. Perhaps you could put a tarp on the ground to protect an area? Or would it just be buried in deep snow and be impossible to remove for grounding?

### tola — 2017-01-24T23:12:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207299>

59 y/o male with no complaints other than bowel movements x5 in a 7 day period. No surgical history, medical history has me on doxycycline for 10 days about 20 yrs ago. Also, idiopathic a-fib x2 in 2014. electro-converted back to sinus. lights out on etomidate was actually kinda cool, like a lens that closes in on itself and then you wake up. That's when I started magnesium 400mg daily. palpatations gone. Now down to 100mg day maintenance dose. expecting RBC mg labs. vision still pretty good, no glasses needed. sleep is good, though I do wake around 6am to pee.

Ok. Haven't got my labs back yet, but I just looked at Scompy's journal and read a few pages of EMF stuff. I may have semi-biohacked my smartmeter by putting an aluminum roaster pan around the smart meter. It's like a parabolic dish so that at least it's broadcasting outward. Better than nothing and cost nothing. Also doesn't piss off the electric company tech.
In anticipation of having a low DHEA level, I'm not sure what I'll do about it. I don't have a problem taking 10mg/ day but I can't find any long term studies on it, especially higher doses....
Organic Acids Testing sounds interesting, too. Just starting to research it.

### tola — 2017-01-27T21:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207450>

Here I am on the continuum...so what is my optimal? Being high normal on lab results? Just feeling good and damn the doctors? Slamming Sudoku? My optimal will be integration of body-mind-spirit. Seeing the pieces come together is the fun part. Reading about the dc current along the spine and the collagen matrix and then thinking about the governor and conception channels and how they flow...grounding is another...the ancients had you use your mind to supercharge your electron vacuum when grounding. not only is it pleasurable but you get a wink from nature while you're drilling down.

anyway, got my Quest labs in-
dhea-s 259 mcg/dl (38-313 wnl) I supplement about 6mos out of the year 10mg day
RBC Mg 5.2 mg/dl (4-6.4 wnl) Daily supplementation 100-400mg depending. Makes me wonder if I have absorption issues. The rest of the labs may clarify?

Question for y'all- what is the guideline here for using medical shorthand? seems like a lot of you are up on it and i see it sprinkled it here and there. it does save a lot of time....

### tola — 2017-01-31T20:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-207672>

More labs…please comment, but keep it clean, there could be ancestral health lurkers about.

**The Good-**

HS-crp 0.51 mg/L

HDL- 59 mg/dl

Triglycerides 58mg/dl (total cholesterol 256, total/HDL ratio 4.3. meh...)

**The Bad-**

Vit D, 25 hydroxy 35 ng/dl. As an experiment, I stopped supplementing D last summer to see if 2+ hrs of sun daily would ride me through the winter. Historical levels were high 50’s with 3-5000iu daily. Sun exposure for the last 2 mos. has been about 30 min to 1 hr. shirtless 5x week.

Fasting BG- 101 mg/dl

A1c- 5.7%

Same fasting BG as when tested 5yrs. Ago...still on the pre-diabetes fence. Eating winter diet of hi-fat, low-ish carb, try to avoid sugar, but avg. 1 oz. dark chocolate daily. I may look into Berberine for supplementation. Krusian tx is getting more sunshine? I can do that.

**The Ugly-**

Serum Ferritin 412ng/ml incoming!!!

I gave blood yesterday. I’ll give blood x3 and retest next year. If still high, I’ll test for hemochromatosis? (serum iron 136 ug/dl, normal range 38-169)..or not, since the tx is the same either way. 500ml blood contains approx. 225mg iron. So it would take 8x donations to get to minimum value. At least the blood bank gives out movie tickets for frequent flyers.

**The Unknown-**(enlighten me!)

Homocysteine 9.9 umol/L
<8 would be better? Is this a folate/MTHFR thing?

BUN/creatinine ratio- 19 (14/0.72) cellular dehydration from nnEMF? Do I need a beer transfusion?

DHEA-s 259 mcg/dl (38-313 normal) sleep is good, gut motility could be better, but clears up when I eat flaxseed with every meal. I’m wary of taking a large dose of hormones until I find long term studies. I’m fine with continuing 10mg 5-7x wk for the time being... started a few months ago.

B12 454 pg/ml (211-946 range) I eat a lot of meat. Thought it would be higher. Absorption?

TSH- 1.65uIU/ml

Free T4- 1.24 ng/dl

Free T3 2.9 pg/ml

I do supplement with Lugol’s, 3mg twice a week, with 100mcg selenium daily. Use non-iodized pink/grey salt. Try and eat lots of salmon. Should I pulse my thyroid with red light for a kickstart? I don’t feel sluggish but I’m not gonna play Aussie rules football anytime soon, either.

**The others**\-

CBC wnl

WBC with diff is wnl, midrange-ish, no signs of infection

liver enzymes wnl

UA clean, ph 7, s.g. 1.008

RBC magnesium- 5.2 mg/dl. I really thought I’d be high-normal with daily mg intake, but I’m

lo-midrange. Absorption factors? Poor supplements? Mg test hacked by Putin?

**Supplement**s- daily unless otherwise noted

100mg ubiquinone

1gm C time-release

100mg alpha-lipoic acid

100mcg selenium

100-300mg magnesium, malate or citrate

90mcg K2 mk7

3mg Lugols iodine 2x week

5000iu vit d, 3x week (restarting)

5ml cod liver oil 5x week

10mg DHEA 5x week

digestive enzymes if I pig out (who, me?)

¼ tsp apple cider vinegar if I pig out on bbq ribs

85% dark chocolate for medicinal purposes only

**Lifestyle-**

Moved out of Oakland. Now I’m in (s)wine country. Short walk to the micro-brewery.

I don’t miss the hood, the crime, bad light, fluoridated water and nnEMF fog

Eat high-ish fat, low-ish carb 100g day +-50g

blue blockers with red light, blacklite at nite, skin covered

no wifi at home nearest neighbor 75 yds. away

lots of gardening, no job, no worries (maybe worried about the new administration!)

RO water, exposed to sunlight when not too lazy. Should I rest the stainless steel water dispenser on magnets?

### LinD — 2017-05-20T04:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-214318>

> [tola said:](/goto/post?id=209571)
>
> This bloodbank has the ability to do double RBC removal, recycling your plasma, which I think I'll try. I've just read that at 20% hematocrit, a skin flap will actually perfuse/oxygenate better than at normal range, so I guess I won't worry about losing all those iron-laden RBC's.

Oooh, Tola. I just saw this. Double-Red & Whole Blood donation notes below:
\~~~
Whole blood donations contain red blood cells, platelets, plasma and white blood cells.

**Whole blood donations can be performed every 56 days.**

\- BUT -

**_Double red cell donation can only be done once every 112 days (16 weeks) or more._** Double red cell donation is ideal for donors who want to minimize their trips to the blood center and who are blood type O.

**How Is a Double Red Cell Donation Different?**

During your double red cell donation, blood is drawn from one arm and drawn through a sterile, single-use needle set to a machine. The machine separates and collects two units of red cells and then safely returns the remaining blood components, along with some saline, back to you through the same arm.

Comment about double red donation:
'...double red blood donation is hard on the body. You lose A LOT of nutrients at once.'
\~~~
I wouldn't do double red, but whole blood only and go more frequently, but that's just me.

### tola — 2017-08-06T04:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-221017>

my latest iron panel came back.
TIBC- 297 ug/dl
UIBC- 201 ug/dl
Serum iron 96 ug/dl. last lab 6 mos. ago was 136
Iron saturation- 32%
serum Ferritin- 67, last lab 412

I calculated it would take 8 blood donations to get my ferritin down to 30ng, but I'm calling it a day at 5 donations and 67ng. I'll still give blood once or twice a year depending on labs.
I'm assuming the apolactoferrin 285mg consumed daily for the first 3 weeks and then, only consumed during my weekly red meat fest made some of the difference, though in hindsight I could have done without it. Apparently the lactoferrin keeps one from absorbing the normal 1-2 mg of iron that gets past our sm. intestine after and iron-rich meal.
Interestingly, I read that in the intestinal epithelium lies d-cytochrome-b reductase which takes oxidized and insoluble ferric iron and reduces it to soluble ferrous iron. The b-cytochrome is a 1 electron transfer agent with a peak absorption spectra of around 520-560nm. The color green hangs out at 495-570nm. In the taoist system, green is the color of the liver, and there's lots of iron in the liver....hmmmmn.

### tola — 2017-09-11T21:07:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-223389>

I haven’t had a run of A-fib for 3 years, but last month, it bit me. My guess is that I’d been eating a lot of celtic grey salt in the previous weeks, trying to see if I could hold onto water better and if that would combat my orthostatic hypotension/dizziness when standing. The dizziness seems worse in hot weather, so I got the idea I was getting dehydrated too easily, maybe exacerbated by drinking RO water. Funny that I never get dizzy when getting up to go to the bathroom at 6am...

Also, I’ve gotten sloppy with electrolyte monitoring. I wasn’t compensating for all that extra sodium with potassium, and I’d been slacking off on magnesium, too, which really extinguished palpitations.

I took 225mg of flecainide to convert to NSR in a couple of hours, after trying everything but carotid massage to convert. First time I’ve used the “pill in a pocket” approach. Beats cardioversion with 200 joules any day! When I looked up the mechanism of action on this drug, it came up as a sodium channel blocker. I stopped the ad lib salt munching and started back on 300mg magnesium, 500mg taurine, and 1-400 mg potassium daily depending on my salt intake.

I recently saw an interview on Mercola.com with someone quoting a german Dr. Hamer (sp?) saying that “territorial loss” was at the core of heart attacks. Bold statement, gotta hand it to Mercola for putting that on his site. I found it interesting that he said because of a heart rythym controller in the brain next to the coronary artery relay, you may be able to convert to NSR by putting an ice pack above your right ear. Worth a try. On a similar limb to walk out on, I’m wondering if my previous high ferritin of 400 meant that iron was depositing in my heart, thereby increasing my sensitivity to EMF.

### tola — 2017-09-15T20:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tolas-jernal.18982/post-223745>

Hail Einkorn! Ever since I went paleo, etc. a few years ago, my GI has slowed considerably. I tried lots of fruit, psyllium, flax, fibre, etc. to minimal effect. After reading "Eat Wheat," I decided to give einkorn flour a try. It's molecularly very simple, and tastes the business, but I also have increased energy, and the GI issues have disappeared, I mean, textbook poops!
Do I have some gliadin guilt over this? Yea, maybe. I'm going to do more research on this, but right now the whole grain honeymoon is in full swing.

# Thread: Look back and face forward
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Just Breathe — 2017-01-25T00:00:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-207300>

I deleted my old journal because it needed to be rewritten.

37 yr old Female, 250 lbs. I currently live at 44° 56' 39" N / 93° 5' 35" W. All my life I have been very heavy, losing 100 lbs about ten years ago. That's all come back now. Mom grew up on a farm, after engaging in SAD diet and modern life, thyroid issues and recent gallbladder removal.
When I moved to my current location, the health challenges really began.

After about 5 years at this location, I moved into a really pretty old Victorian house. It was gorgeous inside, perfect location! One friend mentioned that there might be mold in the basement, and that the bedroom and living room wiring was ungrounded. I didn't listen to that concern, but I should have. The year before I moved in, a smartmeter had been installed on the bedroom wall.

Within a year of living in the house, things that had been on the edge, crumbled. Major GI upset after every meal, vomiting, diarrhea, at least 3x a day. Extreme fatigue. Stress. With a stressful job and difficult group dynamics in my hobby, I was stressed all the time. I went paleo with the Whole 30. It didn't fix anything. Diagnosis with a smart practitioner of adrenal fatigue. 2 years of following the protocols, and I still had brain fog and fatigue.

Summer of 2015 I began following JK lifestyle modifications, including hacking the environment and CT April of 2015 I began the Leptin Reset. It all worked incredibly well, giving me better results faster than I thought possible! I was cured! I was shrinking and my brain fog was gone!
Fall of 2015 something was turned on in the neighborhood. The wires in my home in the ungrounded areas actually hummed. Tinnitus, headaches, eye twitching, chestpains, crazy scary stuff!
I moved out of the area..when I return to that area I noticed that there was a cell phone tower within a half mile of the home. Whenever I return to the neighborhood, I get the same symptoms.

I moved out of that neighborhood into a new area. This has much more population density and is a worse location. But you do what you have to do. When I moved in, I knew something wasn't right, but it was an improvement over my last location so I tried to bear with it. Over time, my ears started ringing when I was in the home only. Symptoms increased in intensity such that they now ring all the time. I got a crazy case of food poisoning and potentially cardiac arrest when something was turned on in my area, likely the smart grid or 5G. The food poisoning took 5 months to cure. I was left with symptoms of EHS.

I invested in an environmental consultant, who tested body voltage, dirty electricity, RF, geopathic stress, everything. As a result of their testing, we discovered 2 cell phone towers beaming directly in the windows. I invested in RF film for the windows, a Naturell bed canopy, and greenwave filters for the outlets. We moved my bed and they put in a few copper things to "break up" the geopathic stress lines.

Thanks to Jansz comments in another journal, I realized that I was really losing out on potassium, with the struggles to take a full breath and rapidly declining body composition. Regular consumption of prunes and coconut water really helped with that. That year I probably gained another 40 lbs! (With all that crazy, I had abandoned paleo eating, except I still was doing the BAB.)

After the RF film was up, blocking the two towers, my relief and dramatic loss of symptoms is palpable!
Whew!!! Constant sugar and chocolate cravings are gone! After a year which felt like a significant setback, I feel like I'm back on track again. Interestingly, while all that insanity was going on, there was still some healing from the AF that went on inside. I feel like my bones, muscles and organs still did some great healing last year and I am in a better spot today. I am frustrated at my huge size, of course. Once the films were up, (2 weeks ago) I stopped the almost constant weight gain. I feel like I've lost the leptin potential sensitivity I had, when I was losing lots and changing shape over night. I now have drippy sweaty head when I workout, which had disappeared with the leptin reset.

I know I'm still in a bad location and have plans to move. The market is a bit hesitant to hire right now, so I hope for some movement on that this year. I'm concerned about bias in hiring based on the way I look, but I just have to do my best and remind myself that there is a company out there that is just waiting for me!

Bad location equals vigilance:
Vitamin D & K
Turmeric
Probiotic
Vitamin C & 50 mg zinc.
Biocidin drops once a week to stave off anything and help my poor gut win again!

20 G magnetico within a naturell bed canopy.
Planning to dump the cell phone and smart TV.

Grounding and being outside for the sun are hard in this location, its a concrete jungle and the parks are not very safe. I do look out the window in the mornings. The windows have the RF film, which drops a lot of the UV coming, but its better than nothing.

I dance once a week and have been attending barre classes twice a week. Rest is key for muscle building for me. After three years no exercise allowed from AF, it is SO wonderful to workout again! I take it slow, do CT, take magnesium, rest. A few days later when I try again, my muscles feel strong and improved, no soreness at all!!

Yes to all spring water, all blue light sources in the home and workplace have been blocked. Nothing in the bedroom except a white noise machine. I turn the bedroom breaker off at night. The food poisoning was from seafood, so it took me about 6 months to returning to adding seafood to my diet again. I did do a bit of fiddling with iodine and selenium last fall and this winter. What I learned is GO WAY Slow. Once or twice a week dosing is all I needed with one drop to feel that things were on the move. I had the weird acne on my face and scalp so I was like, yeah let's be cautious with this.

Healing is a non-linear path, alright. While I thought all last year was a huge setback, there were some gains forward too! I learned a lot and figured out I can't always be in charge and its ok to just be.

Snoring is still an issue, likely because of my size. I had a sleep study done and I had mild sleep apnea during a very short time in the evening. Sleep tech was not concerned and thought a CPAP wasn't necessary. The sleep doc gave the choice to CPAP or sleep on my side and lose weight. After the same doc was talking to me about calories to lose weight, I elected to focus on weight loss.

Going forward: Leptin reset principles. I was exhausted with all that and struggled to cook, so a lot of grains and sugar were in my diet. Back to cooking and thinking clearly again! yay!
Issues to work on: weight loss, tinnitus, EHS, snoring.
Cheers to my fellow biohackers!

### Just Breathe — 2017-01-26T22:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-back-and-face-forward.18983/post-207402>

I thought of a few more updates & clarifications that may be helpful.

**Plantar fasciitis
**
My case of PF was multi-factorial. 30 years of being on releve made for shortened achilles, tendons and calf muscles. Physical therapy, acupuncture and boots that stretch the heels out were helpful, but did not do the trick at all. I believe this condition was excessively aggravated by a poor nnEMF environment. Often, PF is electron loss. Following the approaches to regain electrons, seafood, water, grounding, made a huge difference! The last factor is still in progress. I had a massage therapist ask if I ever used a treadmill, stair stepper or elliptical. I said that yes I had. She said that regular use of these non-native movement patterns can put your spinal cord into a twist (stair steppers/ellipticals) or a jam (running on a treadmill) that can be difficult to undo. She turned me on to the Egoscue method. It consists of a series of exercises that take a half hour. They put your spine into realignment. To maintain and keep this realignment you need to be walking (outside is best of course!) for 30 min, 3x a week. A poor nnEMF environment slowed the success of this, tightening and stressing my muscles. I highly recommend this method for anyone who has tried everything...It also helped with a knee ache.

**Iodine/Seafood**
It's often recommended that iodine protocol be matched with selenium in the form of brazil nuts. I'm not a fan of the brazil nut approach, as you can't tell what amount of selenium is in them. I did 200 mg of selenium, b complex and one drop every few days. Vitamin C comes in powder form and can be dumped into an emergen-c shot to have a heavy dose that's not in those huge pills. After researching Vitamin C, I tested in a separate hack what my level was. I read that you can go up as high as you need to, until loose stools.. that is your clue for maximum dose. vitamin c worked better when taken at a different time than the iodine. Wild-caught seafood is best, as is adding sea vegetables to a meal, but hard and expensive for some people to do. I only used the iodine protocol when my tummy was so upset from seafood food poisoning. I think the food poisoning took 5 months to cure because of a not so good environment.

**Candida**
Candida is a real thing and can be incredibly difficult to fight back. I now know that a poor nnEMF environment contributes to candida...candida is a mold and light destroys mold...nnEMF encourages it. I used Candex and a strict no sugar, no fruit, no alcohol, no grain diet. I added drops of Biocidin to an Emergen-C shot to mask taste. Biocidin plus Candex plus the diet is a significant knockout for candida. I supplemented maybe once a week with a half teaspoon of baking soda in water, whenever I felt I was losing the battle. I've got to re-do this protocol, based on last year.

**MSG poisoning**
I got a huge overdose of presumably MSG from a restaurant a few weeks ago. Everyone who ate at that restaurant had searing headaches immediately after and for a week after!!
I tried the cream of tartar hack, putting 1/2 teaspoon in water. Not super helpful. The Vielight in the evening did a much better job.

(might you notice that I've received subpar quality food from restaurants in the past year? I sure have. Blue-lit workers don't care about food quality or service here. I've been served cold food, & when I complained, they remade it, but did not apologize nor comp my meal. I've now been poisoned twice, vibrio and MSG. I'm now cooking at home much more often & carefully vetting a restaurant. One more nail in the coffin towards this so-called 'great' city life.)

**Tanning**
I get a spot of seborrheic dermatitis that arises every year now after the fall solstice and disappears at the spring solstice. Literally within a day or two of the solstice! I go tanning and use red light therapy to treat it. Winter can be polar vortex type weather for months here, so anytime I can get outside and walk, I will. If I can't, I'll consider tanning. I use the expensive beds, based on their percentage of light. Cheap beds just burn you and take your money. If I go in the morning, I'll do 12 min of red light therapy and a 100% UVA session. If I go at noon, a UVA/UVB 50/50 mix bed. If I go in the evening, UVA session and then red light. I try to only go in the day time, when it would be natural for me to receive this light. This, plus turmeric, has been really helpful in the winter. Once the spring solstice returns, or any temperature above 20F, I'm outside again!

### Just Breathe — 2017-03-27T21:02:06+0300

I went to see a seasoned acupuncturist for sinusitis and tinnitus. I have been going twice a week for close to 8 weeks and have made HUGE improvements!

She noted that I had a very sensitive system and nothing was talking to anything else. Each system was angry and fighting for dominance! Ha, no surprise there.

After a few sessions focusing on all systems talking to each other, we started adding in points for any and all points of the digestive system. Due to hitting those points, something crazy was flushed out of me. The detox/flu from that took about a month to recover, with joint aches, severe headaches, major EMF sensitivity and exhaustion. I was taking activated charcoal just to help me get through it all. I'm curious what others think what might have been flushed out? I had a gallbladder attack during that time and things have since calmed down. I added in MSM and DMSO to help flush things out. Having such a sensitive gut system, I can literally "feel" in the evenings when bad guys are being taken from one spot to be flushed out of my system. It's been a wild ride with this acupuncture thing, but it has been SOO WORTH IT. Sinuses are opening up and now more receptive to the vielight. Skin is more receptive to the sun and redlight/tanning treatments. We finished outfitting the house with RF Film on all windows, so now with the acupuncture, tinnitus is GREATLY reduced. EMF sensitivity is incredibly reduced as well.

During this time, I've also re-focused on Leptin Rx principles. I've been imperfect with food selections but perfect with timing. I'm now having the incredible hot flashes after I eat, or right when I am supposed to eat. I started my re-focusing in early February. This is GREAT NEWS! The last time I tried the Leptin RX, it took 4 months to get the heat after eating. (All last year I made sure to have a BAB within an hour of waking, so still chipping away, even though that was all I could do.)

I specifically selected a community acupuncture business that offers sliding scale and community style. You can go more often, inexpensively and get the treatment you really need. This one also keeps good records and everything is hard-wired in her practice space. I previously went to one that had a ton of wifi and TV's with aquarium scenes and it felt like a wasted electron exchange where I was the loser. I've also tried it in the past, but had no success because my home environment was terrible.

This new place is A+++

It's been almost 1.5 years since I've had real forward progress on anything. I'm so happy to finally report it!

Morning light: I'm out there 3-7 days a week catching at least ten minutes. Ideally before sunrise, but always getting at least ten minutes before 9 am. Weekends its much more. RF films on the windows block a lot, but not all, of the UV.
Weekdays are spent with a few walks during the workday to get light outside, grounding and ideally the full lunch hour outside. With a book, of course.
Cell phone has a low blue light film on it and usually stays at home on airplane mode. Work computer has a privacy screen and f.lux. A full spectrum bulb on a lamp next to me. Fluorescent bulbs everywhere and a TON of wireless. Laptop is on a charging station by my feet with two defender pads between me and it. Mouse and keyboard are hardwired.
Weeknights have a total of 2.5 hours under flourescent for my dance classes. The rest of the evenings are at home, going on walks outside or using natural lighting in the home if I can. I'm in a condo in a city center, so it takes effort to get to greenery. After dance classes I'll be in a cold bath.
Salt lamps are on timers and are on from 7 am to 10pm in the home. Blacklights in the bathroom and a purple and red light in the living room. I'll get a red light treatment once or twice a week.

Weekends are camping or heading to a park for a walk or a book.
