# Thread: Dylan's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dylans-journal.19054/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dylan J — 2017-02-08T20:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dylans-journal.19054/post-208233>

Hello all! I am a 26 yr new to the whole Optimal and hoping for some guidance on where to jump in

I got tested a few months back before discovering the whole Dr. Kruse world. (Labs attached)

My understanding from looking at my body, cravings, etc. is I'm currently Leptin Sensitive but I'm not 100% sure. I have been eating a Bulletproof Diet for about 24 months before these labs (Attached). But that whole time included the "safe starch" theory on some nights.

A few notes from the labs/life:
\-The high LDL jumps out at me as a possible infection/leaky gut? (My functional med doc recommended Mag O7 and saccharomyces boulardii which has seemed to help a bit).
\-I still have pretty big reactions to eggs (acne/stomach aches)...autoimmune/leaky gut?
\-I felt terrible when I had 5 or so oysters for the first time last week...autoimmune/leaky gut?
\-These labs were taken about 4 months after moving from Maine(near a power line) to a Washington DC highrise so may have traded one poor EMF for another. Less ppl in maine though and cleaner water.
\-I still have acne
\-I am radically effected in a positive way if I supplement with Gaba and 5-HTP. Anxiety decreases and Mood increases...making me think maybe not as leptin sensitive as I think.

My question is what prescription to tackle first?

As of writing this, I am moving back to maine in a week or so and looking to take my first steps towards optimal.

Thank you!

\-Dylan

Fix gut, then you can eat anything.

Pampering results in low resistance.

Rye is getting extinct.

From my memory, back in Poland, 1940-1971, bread was a rye bread, nothing but rye.
Wheat was for deserts, pastry, donuts, cakes.

Where to get 100% rye bread?
The (pretty good) Lithuanian sourdough bread, that I can get in my German store (is made of wheat).

I know good 100% rye sourdough bread made from starter.
My father was excellent at that.
He had oven, like pizza oven, just for that purpose.
Practically home bread baking was just for fun. There was available rye bread in bakery in town.
I have newer made bread myself, but from what I have seen watching my father it was time consuming process.
Most of the time is spend waiting and watching the dough to grow (proofing).
Not sure if machine can replicate that, or if it can, I would have to somehow predict that time and tell machine how long to wait.
IIRC, there are two or three of those periods, each take different amount of time.
First make a dough, leave it, wait till it grows
then mix it, let it grow again
mix it, make loves, wait til it grows
bake it
Father knew what to look for by watching, touching, adjusting room temperature (no drafts), humidity (covered with cloth, water sprinkled).
I missed my chance, newer got involved in that process.
But the bread was insanely good.
When father made bread usually my mom would make butter.
Freshly baked bread, still hot from the oven, lots of new butter chased by buttermilk.
Is there anything better?
.

### WalterNL — 2017-02-09T08:05:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/auotimmune-reversal.19055/post-208284>

Michael Pollan predicted the gluten free dogma would come falling down eventually.

There is nothing wrong with gluten per se for a healthy person. But huge amounts of gluten for an already sick problem can exacerbate things. Of course the most processed breads are the worst. Traditional breads are fine depending on your location. And like JanSz says, making bread is time consuming, it's supposed to be.

### Phinx6236 — 2017-02-08T23:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mile-high-city-denver-colorado.19058/post-208246>

Hi everyone I am new here from Denver Colorado. I already bought myself a nir infRared light therapy lamp, far infrared therapy heating pad with jade stones, have the blue blocker glasses I use at work(county office) along with long sleeve shirts, I also been trying to track my dha. I get out into the sun as much as I can even waking up early to catch the sun rise. I take my family out more to get sun as well. I am looking to step my game up and do more. Any suggestions are appreciated and I am looking for a uv option to use indoors with my infrared light. Thank you>

### Rubicon — 2017-02-09T00:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mile-high-city-denver-colorado.19058/post-208250>

Hi Phinx, welcome.

Denver is at around 5000ft and it looks like it's a short drive to get to 8000ft. @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) has found there's a good bit of UV light in the winter at those elevations: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/scompys-journal.16641/page-40#post-206950](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/scompys-journal.16641/page-40#post-206950)
Note: that may be mainly UVA and not UVB based on my testing.
If I were you I would probably concentrate on trying to get UV naturally year round.

If you still want an indoor artificial UV light, some members have had good results with the Fuji Sperti Suntanning Lamp (the red one).
Personally, I've been using a [160W Powersun Reptile Light](https://www.amazon.ca/Zoo-Med-PowerSun-Mercury-Vapor/dp/B0002AQDJ0) this winter...though I've been meaning to try the Sperti.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) has a detailed recommendation for "Artificial Lighting for a Human Locked in a Small Room": [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738) It might have some ideas you can use to biohack using artificial lights

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-09T18:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mile-high-city-denver-colorado.19058/post-208320>

There is a downside to Denver:

Too high puts you in range of clouds like these:

This paper shows you why circumpolar flights and the "donut hole" over Australia are really bad news for the water cycle that feeds the clouds over Australia. Their is an hypothesis that has been proposed that pre-biotic bacterial cells and the first cells capable of growth/division did not require a cytoplasmic membrane to reproduce. This is important link to how mitochondrial function can be affected inside our cells when we fly because they have a bacterial origin. It is believed that a gel-like microscopic structure less than a cubic micrometer may have had a dual role as both an ancient pre-cytoplasm and a boundary layer to the higher-entropy external environment to help life manifest. The gel pre-cytoplasm exposed to radiant energy from the sun would have a massive effect depending upon the frequencies. For life to manifest we we need a pre-ponderance of light especially in the infrared (IR) region. 42% of sunlight is IR-A lighty. This light in the visible part of EM spectrum resulted in the production of an exclusion zone (EZ) with a charge differential (−100 to −200 mV) and boundary that may have been a possible location for the latter organization of the first cytoplasmic membrane. The charge differentials of cosmic radiation that is mentioned in this link would destroy life by ruining mitochondrial function. We believe that pre-biotic cells and then-living cells may have used hydrogen as the universal energy source because of its capability of acting photosynethcially. This implies that life has always been capable of thermosynthesis in their bioenergetic processes because of the unique properties of water. [http://www.popularmechanics.com/flight/a25086/radiation-clouds-in-upper-atmosphere/](http://www.popularmechanics.com/flight/a25086/radiation-clouds-in-upper-atmosphere/)

# Thread: Mitochondriac Scientists
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2017-02-09T08:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208283>

[https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2017/01/170126093255.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2017/01/170126093255.htm)

### WalterNL — 2017-02-09T10:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208290>

Not really sure what to think of this.

> Since this research been going on for well over a century, it's clear that mitochondria only give up their mysteries rather slowly.
>
> "For the past decade our focus has been on a particular 'back-up' system found in the mitochondria of lower organisms, but which has been lost during the evolution of complex animals such as humans or fruit flies. This back-up system kicks in when the regular energy-generating system of the mitochondria is overloaded, damaged or poisoned, protecting the cell against the harmful stresses of having a malfunctioning 'engine'. Indeed, mitochondria can be thought of rather like a car engine, **that burns fuel (food molecules)**, and recovers the energy in a useful form to drive the processes of life. A malfunctioning engine imparts less energy but also creates toxic by-products as a result of incomplete combustion. Mitochondria are very similar," Jacob clarifies.

And then of course come the medical applications:

> Jacob's team **has transplanted** the back-up or 'alternative' respiratory machinery from the mitochondria of lower organisms to human cells, showing that it can protect against pathological stresses, and even lethal poisons like cyanide, that target the mitochondria.
>
> "This could have medical applications even within the next decade. But part of our work is still focused on very basic processes inside mitochondria. And there are always new surprises, sometimes relating to topics that have been neglected or have been impossible to study until the right tools became available," says Jacobs

This 'alternative respiratory machinery' is further explained on his group's website:
[http://www.biocenter.helsinki.fi/bi/Jacobs/projects.htm](http://www.biocenter.helsinki.fi/bi/Jacobs/projects.htm)

> ****Alternative respiratory chain enzymes: a possible therapy for mitochondrial disorders?****
> The genomes of plants, fungi and many microbes, as well as some primitive animal phyla, contain genes for alternative mitochondrial respiratory chain enzymes, which buffer a host of redox and bioenergetic stresses similar to those encountered in humans under pathological conditions. The genes for these alternative enzymes are absent from humans and other complex animals. However, we reasoned that their introduction may alleviate many of the physiological defects associated with mitochondrial disease. We have transferred the relevant genes from the tunicate Ciona, as well as from fungi, into human cells and model organisms, creating proof of concept for such protection. Our current research focuses on ascertaining the potential of these enzymes in therapy, both by exploring what specific pathologies they can prevent, as well as testing out ways to safely introduce them in a therapeutically effective form to humans. In addition we are studying the natural biology of the alternative enzymes in Ciona, to understand better how they can be most effectively used in humans.
>
> -   The alternative oxidase (AOX) and NADH dehydrogenase (NDX) from Ciona can be expressed in model organisms in all tissues and throughout the life cycle without any significant deleterious effects.
> -   AOX and NDX confer resistance against respiratory poisons (e.g. cyanide, antimycin, rotenone) on both cells and whole organisms
> -   AOX protects cells and tissues form the effects of chronic exposure to mitochondrial toxins, such as found in cigarette smoke
> -   NDX and AOX can protect Drosophila from lethality and other severe phenotypes caused by genetic ablation of respiratory chain complexes I and IV
> -   AOX also protects against pathological insults connected to oxidative stress

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-09T17:05:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208311>

Waves are the pulse of the ocean and the spark the nature gives to life. These waves connect to the tides of the sea and connect to us in ways few observe. Tides bring food to creatures and take boats out to sea. An sailor has the impression that the ocean is made of waves than that it is made of water because of how he senses them. Heredity is about waves too. Most humans believe it is about genetic waves in the nuclear genome but guys like me think it is about the mitochondrial wave that are made by the light it liberates. It is general expression of the periodicity of organic life that is buried in our mitochondria. All generations belong to a continuous succession of waves of some sort, in which every single one resembles its predecessors and its followers yet changes because the world around forces that change.

### Scompy — 2017-02-11T09:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208393>

Some secondary mito mentions in this one on Fibromyalgia: [http://nationalpainreport.com/fibromyalgia-expert-says-misdiagnosis-is-rampant-8832803.html](http://nationalpainreport.com/fibromyalgia-expert-says-misdiagnosis-is-rampant-8832803.html)

"A second one is when you’re having trouble in the cells of your body making energy or the currency of energy called ATP, or adenosine triphosphate, you’ll be energy deficient. Your cells will be starving for energy, and this occurs in little organelles in the cell called mitochondria. The mitochondria are like the little energy plants of the cells. They can be really damaged by things like long courses of antibiotics. Certain types of antibiotics, like the fluoroquinolone class of antibiotics, like Cipro, for instance, are really poisonous to mitochondria, but many other medications are harmful to mitochondria, including cholesterol-lowering drugs called statin drugs. They can really do a lot of damage to the mitochondria and the biochemistry of the mitochondria because they can cause profound CoQ10 deficiency, which is really important in mitochondrial biochemistry for energy production.

\[Other causes for mitochondrial dysfunction include anemia and respiratory issues, like COPD.\]"

### Scompy — 2017-02-13T09:33:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208495>

"It can be concluded that the exposure to chronic hypoxia induces a shift from aerobic toward anaerobic catabolic metabolism. The hypoxia-induced increase in intracellular and mitochondrial ROS formation was not excessive and may be involved in endothelial signaling of hypoxic responses. Our results indicate an important role of succinate, complex II, and reverse electron transport in hypoxia-induced adjustments in endothelial cells."

[http://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00424-017-1935-9](http://link.springer.com/article/10.1007%2Fs00424-017-1935-9)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-16T18:42:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriac-scientists.19061/post-208721>

...
Brand New podcast and I think one of the better one's I've ever done. Max is an amazing guy from Germany I met in London in 2106. Before the truth can set you free, you need to identify which lie is holding you hostage. We talk about that here.
Once you eliminate epistemology and ignore obvious controls.…..you can 'scientifically prove' just about anything to keep the grant money flowing in. That is modern science in a nutshell and it is why nutrition and food studies are worthless to a mitochondriac.

# Thread: Chad's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/>

### Primed for your Life Chad — 2017-02-09T13:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208298>

I love everything about making my life revolve around light, water and magnetism. I am conscious to maximize all the good aspects and minimize the bad. I am grateful for Jack and all his staff and this group.

I am 48, fit and healthy. Australian adopted from New Zealand (Irish Mum) to Hong Kong, Singapaore, US, Canada and eventually Adelaide. Had a restaurant in Brisbane but now a Wellness Coach in the Philippines after being in the Hospitality industry.

5'9 / 64 kilos (141 pounds). Good skin, sleep, energy and body functioning.

Previously over-trained (4 Ironman events and many marathons) and fueled poorly in a bad environment.

Travelling towards optimal for last 3 years but especially since really getting addicted to learning from Dr. Jack mid-way last year, 2016.

I live in a bad quantum environment in Manila with high population density and wi-fri and cell towers. I use an inexpensive cell without connection to internet. I have an ipad.  Ipad will go and we will move eventually.

Current Labs show:

\- Vitamin D of 99 nmol/L ( I wear minimal clothing for maximum sun exposure on my northern European skin- it is Philippine winter here at the moment)

\- HDL of 136, Triglycerides of 44 so ratio of .32. Previous ratio of .37

\- Total cholesterol of 348 and LDL of 204.

\- HS-CRP is a high 3.92 but was .74 in Nov. 2016 and .83 in April 2016. I do not over-exercise any more!) Not sure why so high for this period? I had a 24 hour "flu" just starting so I think this might have affected this reading.

\- Bun / Creatinine ratio has gone from

15 in 2013
16 in 2014
13 in 2015
19 in 2016
22 in 2017

HbA1c

5.6 April 2016
5.0 Nov. 2016
5.7 Feb. 2017

I do not drink alcohol, maximize sunshine, eat a lot of seafood and quality fats and green veg. Love CT and minimize fake lite but not enough. I do run to all my client meetings so sometimes run 20km within a day but wear a quality pollution mask and never feel tired or even sore.

Thanks.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chads-optimal-journal.19062/post-208348>

Hello Dr. Jack Kruse, Any feedback on the above would be greatly appreciated.

Have read your book twice, listened to the podcasts up to 5 times each and study everyday on how to get to optimal.

Do CT, do not eat after sun down, feel I am a lot smarter, stronger and happier. I am a success story like others here but want to get to OPTIMAL too.

Thanks.

I am up to 45 minutes in the cold plunge (approximately 52 degrees) and love it. I still shiver even 10 minutes after in the sauna!

Definitely much cooler to get younger and happier.

I also had a doc want to give me a statin prescription after I ran a 3:30 marathon following a morning bike of 91km and with my HDL at 126 and Trigs at 26. Haha. I don't do those crazy events any more and prefer CT and running lightly with my daughter.

I even sent her a lot of information but the drug company holiday and cash is mightier than the facts!!

### Rocky — 2017-02-12T20:31:05+0200

Chad,

### Primed for your Life Chad — 2017-02-13T02:01:15+0200

>

Dear Rocky,

I started with face then feet in an ice bucket with my children and we are now loving the ice baths. It gets a lot easier as you go along.

I live nearby a hotel that also has a sauna and a cold plunge (as cold as an ice bath) side by side.

With my ice bath at hoe I listen to Jack Kruse youtube videos with the lights off and the hotel one I listen to Jack Kruse podcasts.

You need to concentrate on your breathing and I really love it. You sleep like a champion afterwards.

I sometimes am still shaking 20 minutes after but also enjoy this process too because I know it is making me younger!

Cheers mate and have a great week ahead.

Chad.

# Thread: Brent's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 44
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### brent-next — 2017-02-09T16:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-208310>

I was born in the tropics to American parents who were vegetarians and who didn't eat fish for religious reasons. They did eat eggs and milk. I'm guessing that my mother had gestational diabetes due to my 9 lb. 3 oz birth weight.

I lived near cloudy Chicago from when I was two until I went to boarding high school in Knoxville, Tennessee (even cloudier) then I went to college in Chattanooga Tennessee where it is also very cloudy from October to March. I would get very depressed in the winter when I lived in Tennessee.

At home and at school, we ate a lot of textured vegetable proteins (fake meats) which we were told was health food by our church. Beans were a staple of our diet.

My maternal grandfather lived to age 93 then died from a heart attack in Walla Walla Washington. My maternal grandmother had esophageal cancer in her late 70's and then died of starvation in Walla Walla Washington in her 80's after refusing to eat liquid meals (Ensure) due to chronic nausea after her cancer surgery.

My paternal grandfather lived to age 89 and then died of multiple mini strokes and a fever. This was confirmed by autopsy. My paternal grandmother lived into her 80's and also died of starvation after refusing to eat, only her reason was depression following becoming incontinent (Asheville, North Carolina).

My mother has had two psych hospitalizations for mania (near Chicago). My father has had one heart attack (near Chicago) and two strokes (one near Chicago, and one in Mesa, Arizona).

My health problems include inadequate blood sugar control. I don't seem to have any instant release insulin and then slower release insulin will drive my blood sugar uncomfortably low (75 mg/ dl or lower). Even tiny amounts of carbs in my diet, or a little bit too much protein will push my fasting blood sugars over 100 mg/dl.

For at least 10 years I also have had a problem with waking up hot, tired and wide awake at 2:00 a.m. and not being able to fall asleep until 4:30 a.m. Previous testing didn't show any easy to observe correlation between blood sugar changes and these wakefulness periods. Recently I have added blood ketone testing to my regimen of midnight tests and found that my periods of night time wakefulness are caused by either a decline in blood sugar, or a decline in blood ketones or both.

I have been able to end my long, hot wakeful periods by drinking water (which often washes food out of my stomach) or by very small carb snacks (like half a dried fig or 15 g. of cooked sweet potato and 20 g. of butter or a few oz of heavy whipping cream.

I'm interested in suggestions on how to maintain stable blood sugar and blood ketones over night.

I also have chronic knee and hip pain. I am trying to get strong and I like doing pushups, pullups and swimming. It is not easy for me to gain muscle.

I eat a low carb diet with all the fish I can get (often 4 or 5 times per week). I've been in Cyprus since the end of October. First Larnaca, then Paphos and now I'm in Girne. Paphos is somewhat sunnier and warmer than Girne, but my Republic of Cyprus visa expired so I came to the Turkish side. The latitude here is 35 deg. N.

I try to get the right amount of sun and just bought a Sun Friend UV monitor watch to try and help with that. My skin tone is naturally pasty white, and right now I have a light tan.

My noon shadow here as of February 9 are still longer than I am tall so I may still not be making any vitamin D. I'm still supplementing with vitamin D until my shadow is shorter than I am tall.

It hasn't been as sunny here as I would like, but it's better than Central Europe which is where I was last summer and last winter. Perhaps I should have gone further south.

The neighbors here have WiFi. I'm using a switch with cables strung everywhere. I mostly turn off my computers at dark. I'm not sure if the water here has fluoride in it or not.

I'm the happiest when my blood sugar is 83 mg/dl and my ketones are about 3 mmol/l.

My tools for biohacking include, blood sugar and ketone meters, a Hexoskin data shirt and a reasonable budget for labs.

Six months ago I tested positive for ascaris lumbricoides (round worms). I treated that and re-tested negative. Toxoplasma Gondii IgG was over 650 and IgM was .349. (intracellular parasites).

My diet is basically low carb paleo with as much fish as I can get.

I tried Ozone injections in my knees and hips for the pain. That hurt a lot and 100% did NOT help at all. Sunshine, even enough to get a light tan in the summer seems to not help my knee pain at all.

I'm now following a leg exercise program of various leg lifts, but lifts, and marching in place. This has significantly reduced my knee and hip pain.

Any suggestions on how to further reduce my knee and hip pain, control my blood sugar , gain muscle and live exuberantly would be welcome.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-09T18:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-208323>

Parasites like worms, virus, bacteria, mold and fungi all undergo explosive growth with nnEMF in the environment.

Lots of people will try to make you bulletproof by telling you mold is really bad and telling you to avoid it. Do you know the only way to really avoid it on a planet it evolved on? You have to avoid the fertilizer that makes it grow. What is that? nnEMF and non native electric and magnetic fields are the fertilizer that makes fungi and mold grow uncontrollably. That includes blue light too. You don't hear that from supplement makers because they cannot monitize it. Toxic mould in homes grows much faster under the load of high electromagnetic loads. Did you know that? Even NASA knows it but does not really talk much about those experiments done in space where nnEMF also exists. They grow much faster in a disturbed milieu. Mercury / heavy metals can also exacerbate the growth because mercury can under go a molecular resonace energy transfer coupled with blue light exposure. Did your supplement maker buddies ever tell you that? Nah.......it would stop those monthly checks coming in for the lastest crap coming to your house you do not need. We need to keep you controlled by keeping you from the data. Many people do not know that bacteria and mold and Archea undergo explosive growth in space. This was well known when an America astronaut, Dave Wolf spent 4 months in space on MIR for NASA before the USA planned on building the ISS. When Wolf opened up the hatch where all the water and wiring were found he found the are filled with microbial and moldly life. This shocked NASA and they knew this could mean trouble for the space program if they were going to try to colonize space.

Little did they know it was the calling card for what ails modern humans on Earth. Confused? Let me explain some evolutionary history to you.

The next trip into space, NASA made sure this experiment was fast tracked to prove that bacteria do undergo massive growth in space. They had to prepare the ISS for this issue for health issues for the astronauts. It was performed on the ISS quickly. They did the seminal experiment that showed when you put a bacteria into a place with no connection to Earth or to the sunlight bacteria undergo explosive growth. Why is this a critical piece of evidence in my Quilt document? What is our mitochondria, fundamentally and where did we get it? It is a bacteria we stole 600 million years ago at endosymbiosis. We controlled its growth on Earth by surrounding it with water and deleting most of its genes to 37 in number. Only 13 of its genes remain which code for the proteins that make the respiratory proteins which tunnel electrons from food, magnetic flux, and the sun. After the eukaryotic cells absorbed the bacteria it surrounded it with water and cell membranes loaded with DHA. Why did it do this? Bacteria are known to release 5000 times as much light as eukaryotic cells. Heat is a form of IR light. Heat and light do special things to water. Heat shrinks water when the water is confined in small spaces. This controls the size and shape of the mitochondria below. This is why bacteria in space grow like mad. When they swell this is a stimulus to divide. Since our mitochondria used to be a bacteria we control its size and shape to use it as a powerhouse of quantum energy production. All frequencies of light affect cell water but UV and IR light build water into a battery by making something called the exclusion zone of water. It has a net negative charge and excludes protons from it. This EZ become a perfect battery to run the ATPase of the mitochondria as a quantum torque engine. In space, we lose these effects in our own mitochondria and this is why astronauts get unusual medical conditions.

Today we live an indoor life, out of the sun and disconnected from the Earth magnetic field. A blue lit world that is microwaved simulates space from a mitochondrial perspective.

[http://it-takes-time.com/2015/07/10/microbial-growth-and-electromagnetic-radiation/](http://it-takes-time.com/2015/07/10/microbial-growth-and-electromagnetic-radiation/)

Metals interact with EMF and lower the cells redox by destroying glutathione levels.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-10T02:10:41+0200

### brent-next — 2017-02-10T17:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-208362>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=208342)
>
> And why is that? Maybe because those people stay inside and keep their windows closed and Air conditioning on?

So how much reduction in non native emf is enough? How much sunshine is enough?

I've changed a lot of things in my life to reduce non-native emf. I sold my Tesla model S electric car and my home converted electric truck. I'm no longer a ham radio operator and I don't fly my own airplanes any more. My computer is six feet away from me.

I have an ionizing radiation meter in my bag. The only time I have seen a high reading is while flying in a commercial airliner, where my meter says "Radiation levels dangerous" at 2.10 micro sieverts per hour. I don't fly much any more.

My microwave meter shows dangerous levels all the time and while I know that zero levels would be ideal, they are really hard to find. My meter that reads 50 or 60 hz signals often will show 1000 v/m if say a refrigerator isn't grounded.

Last winter at 50 deg. North in Europe, I got really sick. This winter in Cyprus at 35 deg North I have stayed fairly healthy, but I haven't made much progress on my road to optimal.

If mold toxins are increased by a factor of 600 due to typical microwave background radiation as the link above suggests, then places like Arizona might have strong mold problems because they don't have enough trees or water to soak up the microwaves.

Perhaps my road to optimal is to keep adding hours of sunshine exposure and keep reducing my microwave dose until I notice real progress?

### brent-next — 2017-02-17T13:32:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-208770>

Today was sunny day here in Cyprus, so I started sunbathing about 9:30 a.m. as soon as my Sun Friend UV watch was showing some UV (the second led was lit). About solar noon (with the 6'th led on the Sun Friend lit) I did 30 minutes of CT in a 50 deg F. shallow outdoor pool.

My blood sugar was 93 mg/dl and ketones were 1.1 mmol/l before CT. My blood sugar immediately after was 96 mg/dl and my ketones had dropped to 0.3 mmol/l. Before CT I ate about an ounce of butter and then ate another ounce of butter after CT along with some hot tea with milk to warm up.

I shivered for 30 minutes after getting out of the pool and drying off, in spite of lying wrapped in a blanket in the sun.

This was my longest CT so far. I tried to sit motionless in the pool. I did shiver some while in the pool, but didn't shiver intensely until I was out and had fully dried off.

After I warmed up and stopped shivering I felt very good.

Does anyone have any thoughts on the fact that my ketones declined but my blood sugar didn't or other comments or suggestions?

### brent-next — 2017-02-19T18:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-208899>

Could using a polarized computer monitor be a big problem that is slowing me down on my road to optimal?

I make my living on a computer and have known for some time how dangerous this is. For the last two years my laptop is usually 8 feet away from me connected to the internet with a cable. My monitor is 5 feet away from me and I am careful to use Flux and turn my computer screens off at dark. I use a remote keyboard and a mouse.

But my computer monitor is polarized, just like most computer monitors are. If you want to check this on your computers, hold either a polarizing filter (or a pair of polarized sunglasses) in front of your computer screen and rotate it. If the screen goes black at one point, then the monitor is polarized.

Most, but not all computer monitors are polarized. I have a projection monitor (unfortunately in another country right now) that isn't polarized and I have tested one flat panel TV and found that it also was not polarized.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/polarized-light.17680/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/polarized-light.17680/)

Natural sunshine is never polarized.

Even though I am careful to have plenty of natural light in the room where my computer is located, having a big source of polarized light that I am looking at, certainly seems like it could be a problem.

Comments? Suggestions?

### brent-next — 2017-03-09T13:17:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-209940>

Okay, I relocated to a sea front resort that is mostly empty (it's the off season) in the most sparsely populated part of Cyprus (the the panhandle of the island that points towards Syria). There are 25 cabins and I am staying that is out of range of wi-fi. A hill blocks strong signals from the many cell phone towers on the island although cell phones still work from my cabin. Mine is off 99% of the time.

Most nights, I'm the only guest.

My microwave 200 Mhz to 8 Ghz meter shows mostly clean at the location of my cabin. There are just a few chirps, that I haven't been able to identify. The sea is dark in front of my cabin and on moonless nights the sky is dark. I'm using a cable to connect to the internet to make this post.

I'm getting as much sun and time in cold water as the weather will allow.

On days with strong sun, I'm trying two to three drops of methylene blue (aquarium grade) in water with 0.5 mg. of nicotine gum (1/4 of a 2 mg. chicklet) tucked in between my lip and my jaw. This definitely makes me feel good while I am doing it. Most days have at least some strong sun. My shadow near noon is shorter than I am tall.

I'm hoping to make progress mostly on knee and hip health while I am here and I think I will stay here until it gets crowded, which should be about to the end of April.

### brent-next — 2017-03-25T09:19:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211044>

I just tested positive again for Ascaris Lumbricodes eggs a common roundworm parasite:
[https://web.stanford.edu/group/parasites/ParaSites2005/Ascaris/JLora\_ParaSite.htm](https://web.stanford.edu/group/parasites/ParaSites2005/Ascaris/JLora_ParaSite.htm)

Last year I also tested positive for this and took a three day treatment and then re-tested negative.

I did the re-test yesterday because a Dr. told me that the worms don't produce eggs at all times of their life (none at all if they are male). He recommended three different re-tests from at least two different labs.

There are some parasites that are arguably optimal for human health, but this is NOT one of them.

I just now took three 250 mg. Pirantel (Kontil) chewable tablets. I need to take at least one more dose and then re-test several times.

Anyone have problems with re-occuring Ascaris Lumbricodes? I don't know if this is the same infection or a new one. I'm guessing that it's the same one.

### brent-next — 2017-03-29T08:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211330>

This source lists treatments and calls for a 3 day dose of Pyrantel:

[https://www.unboundmedicine.com/5mi...t/14076/all/Ascaris\_Lumbricoides\_\_Ascariasis\_](https://www.unboundmedicine.com/5minute/view/Select-5-Minute-Pediatric-Consult/14076/all/Ascaris_Lumbricoides__Ascariasis_)

### brent-next — 2017-03-31T08:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211449>

My first dose of Kontil (three chewable pills) didn't seem to do much. I still had a very noisy abdomen (you could hear it rumble across the room) and discomfort / pain in my chest. I think the medicine wasn't effective because I drank too much water that day.

I took a second round of Kontil (again three chewable pills) three days later and again didn't notice much. But 24 hours after the second dose, I had four bouts of strange diarrhea like stools and then after that I felt fine. My bowel quieted down for the first time in a very long time.

I plan to take another dose of an anti helmenith medicine (either pyrantil or albendazol) probably in a few days. My guess is that I have has ascaris lumbricodes for a long time.

### brent-next — 2017-04-04T18:23:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211736>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=211463)
>
> Another tack at parasites. (snip)

Thanks JanSz. I've now taken two doses of albendazol and two of pyrantil. Pyrantil made me feel sick and albendazol did not. All of the worm medicines I have taken made my gut quiet for about a day and then the noise would come back.

I really don't think that I am over this particular parasite yet.

Here's another good video on Ascaris. Note that this Dr. who treats this parasite daily has a much different dosing recommendation than the WHO recommends.

He treated this patient with 100 mg. albendazol orally, twice daily for three days and repeated at two weeks. Note that this patient was only 45 kg. The WHO recommend 400 mg. as a one time dose for people 70 kg and up.

This patient also needed endoscopic extraction of just one worm to prevent obstruction of his stomach.

### JanSz — 2017-04-04T22:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211764>

### brent-next — 2017-04-05T11:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-211796>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=211764)
>
> @[brent-next](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19774/)
> why not getting gartroscopy, easy enough
>
> ....

Yes, gastroscopy is easy enough to get, but on Dr. Kruses advice I am living in the least populous part of the island and it's an hour and a half drive to the city where this would be available.

I crushed my last two albendazole pills (400 mg) mixed them in a big glass of water and took yesterdays dose like that. I've seen far too many pills go from mouth to anus without dissolving at all and if the worms were living in my stomach (likely) I want to deliver the medicine to my stomach active and ready to go, rather than needing to dissolve and having the worms try to get out of the way.

If my epigastric symptoms don't go away after this round of medicine, I will probably see if I can get an inspection with a scope.

### JanSz — 2017-04-05T16:55:56+0300

### brent-next — 2017-04-08T17:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-212020>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=211809)
>
> Gastroscopy is easy and it would take the worms out, so it would make your life (temporarily) easier,
> but anything that is spread thru your body would need medicine otherwise they will come back.
> ...

My guess is that Albendazole is good at killing worms that it comes into contact with for more than 20 minutes or so. The Dr. in the Blue and White striped shirt video above from Guatemala says that H. Pylori (the bacteria that causes ulcers) often occurs at the same time as Ascaris.
There is an easy stool test for the antibodies to H-Pylori that I plan to order next Saturday along with a re-test for parasites and occult blood.

The recommendation here for treatment is to take it again in two weeks, because some larvae may be in my lungs or somewhere else in my body, when I took my the first course of Albendazole.

The mechanism of action of albendazole includes being a mitochondrial poison for the worms. That's not comforting to someone who is trying to make healthy human mitochondria.

[https://www.drugbank.ca/drugs/DB00518](https://www.drugbank.ca/drugs/DB00518)

"Albendazole causes degenerative alterations in the tegument and intestinal cells of the worm by binding to the colchicine-sensitive site of tubulin, thus inhibiting its polymerization or assembly into microtubules. The loss of the cytoplasmic microtubules leads to impaired uptake of glucose by the larval and adult stages of the susceptible parasites, and depletes their glycogen stores. Degenerative changes in the endoplasmic reticulum, the mitochondria of the germinal layer, and the subsequent release of lysosomes result in decreased production of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), which is the energy required for the survival of the helminth. Due to diminished energy production, the parasite is immobilized and eventually dies."

I still have epigastric discomfort and sometimes pain, but my gut is much quieter than it was before the Albendazole.

So I would definitely NOT recommend anywhere in Cyprus.

### brent-next — 2017-06-06T16:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-215489>

I just remembered to check the field strength produced by my laptop computer setup at my current location. I was getting a 600 v/m field produced by the power supply of my laptop feeding off the 50 hz power here. I think the legal limit for living next door to a high tension power line is something like 100 v/m.

I plugged my computer into a grounded outlet and the measured field went to zero.

My testing shows that these very high fields are common in homes and are usually caused by grounds being disconnected.

Turn as much of the AC power off in your house as you can check the fields and re-check them every time you change your electrical setup.

### brent-next — 2017-06-12T14:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-215910>

Has anyone noticed that it is easier to stand up straight (have good posture) after either some intense sunshine or cold thermogenesis?

I seem to be able to effortlessly have good posture after about an hour in the noon time sun here in Ukraine (6 or 7 on my Sun Friend UV meter) or after about an hour in a 55 deg. F cold tub.

### brent-next — 2017-06-14T08:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-216137>

I just tested positive for a different parasite the pinworm Enterobius vermicularis. This was a stool test and according to Wikipedia finding these in the stool is rare so I guess I was lucky that these showed up in this particular stool sample.

Have you heard of the "scotch tape test" for parasites? If not read this link from Stanford.

[https://web.stanford.edu/class/humbio103/ParaSites2006/Enterobius/diagnostic tests.htm](https://web.stanford.edu/class/humbio103/ParaSites2006/Enterobius/diagnostic%20tests.htm)

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinworm\_infection](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinworm_infection)

So it looks like the advice I got to test and re-test with multiple different labs was excellent advice. This positive test followed two different negative tests at two different labs.

So people -- if you have had one negative stool test for parasites, it DOES NOT mean that you don't have them. You need to test and re-test with multiple labs on different days.

Oh, by the way, I don't have any of the common symptoms of Enterobius vermicularis like anal itching.

One of the main symptoms of pinworms is insomnia, which is a problem that I have had for many years.

This quote from the Wikipedia page:

"General symptoms are [insomnia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Insomnia) (i.e., persistent difficulties to sleep) and [restlessness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Psychomotor_agitation)."

The thing with parasites is that they vary from ones that you badly need to get rid of because they can kill you, like ascaris lumbroicoides, to ones that won't kill you like Enterobius vermicularis to ones that are arguably good for you like having just a few hookworms, to turn down your immune response.

Right now I would be delighted to have just a few hookworms, but the Enterobius vermicularis looks like one that would be better to not have. At least it's not in the likely to kill you category.

### brent-next — 2017-06-16T10:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-216325>

I had a very disappointing moment at the Dr. on Monday.

In March of 2016 and again in June of 2016 I had them do an ultrasound of my carotid arteries and my abdominal aorta. Both of of the 2016 tests showed a thickening of my left carotid artery at 1.5 mm thick but no restriction in flow. Both of the 2016 test showed a 2 mm thickening in my abdominal aorta but no restriction in flow.

The June of 2017 test showed a 1.6 mm left carotid artery wall with a 25% restriction in flow. My abdominal aorta now is 2.5 mm thick with a 20% restriction in flow.

So what did I do right between March and June of 2016 and what did I do wrong between June of 2016 and June of 2017?

Here are my theories. Between March and June of 2016 I took, 10 grams of vitamin C per day along with proline and lysine. After June when it was unchanged, I thought that the Vitamin C wasn't doing much so I cut back to about 2 g. per day and dropped the proline and lysine.

Over the recent winter I was in Northern Cyprus for three months where everyone smokes like a chimney and I'm sure the second hand smoke didn't help. I also drank a lot of cream in a failed attempt to control my epigastric pain and I ate a fair amount of cheese. I'm sure the cream and the cheese were bad ideas. Also my carbs and protein were higher than I would have liked.

I also was not as careful about dental hygene and avoiding tooth infections as I should have been. I didn't use my water pik and didn't brush with baking soda every day. I did use dental floss a lot.

Over the winter I did get enough sun to maintain a tan and keep from getting the flue. I also took anti worm medicines (albendazol for ascaris). This apparently got rid of my chronic constipation problem.

I did a modest amount of swimming in 16 deg. C water.

Any comments or suggestions?

### brent-next — 2017-06-18T09:38:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-216474>

Yesterday, I bought tickets to fly to Costa Rica. Costa Rica is at 10 degrees north. I will be there on Thursday.

If anyone has any ideas on how to minimize or eliminate health risks like dengue fever in Costa Rica, I would certainly appreciate it.

My guess is that these risks are fairly low, but as a first time visitor to Costa Rica there are probably some details that I need to learn.

### brent-next — 2017-07-05T23:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-218037>

I've been in Costa Rica for two weeks and here are some things I have noticed.

1\. My "Sun Friend" UV meter is not something that should be trusted to keep me from being burned in the tropics. One day I spent about half of the morning in the sun and was careful to keep my UV exposure meter readings below what I knew was safe from Ukraine (5 and under on my Sun Friend watch) and still managed to get a sunburn. I had a mid brown tan before this so it wasn't a bad burn. But I need to learn more about how to interpret light meters at 10 degrees north compared to 50 degrees north.

Takeaway: Don't count on a meter reading that you know is safe at northern latitudes being safe in the tropics.

2\. My Thrifty Toyota Rav4 rent a car emits four 10 second high power burts of microwaves per minute. I suppose it's a tracking system that contacts the cell phone network. That needs to be fixed.

3\. Costa Rica is swimming in microwaves. Often you can see 3 or 4 cell phone towers very close to each other and signs advertising high speed wireless networks are everywhere. You can find dead zones, but it is difficult and time consuming.

### brent-next — 2017-08-22T00:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-222015>

I've now been in Costa Rica for two months and most days I have slept terribly most days. By sleeping terribly I mean, that I wake up at 11:00 p.m. to 2:00 a.m. and then can't sleep for several hours. This has consistently happened even when I have gotten plenty of sun in the morning.

But, if I get some good solid morning sun, some sun close to noon, and if we get lucky and there is some afternoon sun (and I get my bare skin out in it) then I sleep well. My conclusion is that I need doses of intermittent sun, spaced over the day in order to sleep well.

Morning sun may be more important, but unless I get something that approaches all day intermittent sun, my sleep will still be terrible.

So if you are looking to find your place in the sun, look for somewhere with good all day sun.

### brent-next — 2017-09-03T01:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-222773>

Okay I'm in the tropics (Costa Rica) trying to get to optimal.

Sometimes, when I go out in the strong sun, I will feel good for the first 10 minutes, then I feel less good, then I start to feel bad, so I go back to my hotel room, turn on the air conditioning and slowly feel better.

So how can strong sunshine help me get to optimal when it often makes me feel bad? I've checked and I'm definitely not getting sunburned when this happens.

Here's my current theory.

Dr. Kruse has written that strong UV light shrinks the respiratory proteins in mitochondira:

[https://www.jackkruse.com/time-12-adrenal-fatigue/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-12-adrenal-fatigue/)

My understanding of this article is that strong UV light will make you able to make ATP from glucose more easily. The more glucose you can easily use, the lower your blood glucose will tend to go.

My guess is that I am often becoming hypoglycemic in the strong sunshine, even when I am sitting still in a beach chair. This was a surprise to me, because I hadn't expected strong sunshine (in the absence of any exercise) to lower my blood sugar.

There is ample anecdotical and clinical experience that strong sunshine can make you become hypoglycemic.

[http://theblondeabroad.com/2017/05/31/how-to-manage-your-blood-sugar-in-hot-destinations/](http://theblondeabroad.com/2017/05/31/how-to-manage-your-blood-sugar-in-hot-destinations/)

Right now I need to buy more blood sugar strips so I can test my theory.

### brent-next — 2017-10-13T02:22:11+0300

I just ordered a wildly overpriced computer monitor that uses e-ink. In other words it doesn't shine any light into your eyes at all.

This can be used outdoors in bright sunshine, which is the probably the best place to use a computer if you have to use a computer.

[https://www.amazon.com/Dasung-Ink-P...srs=10072153011&ie=UTF8&qid=1507837135&sr=8-1](https://www.amazon.com/Dasung-Ink-Paperlike-13-3-Monitor/dp/B075FL8DKV/ref=lp_10072153011_1_1?srs=10072153011&ie=UTF8&qid=1507837135&sr=8-1)

It certainly is not a perfect monitor. It doesn't display video well has a slow refresh rate. But the fact that it doesn't shine visible light in your eyes at all, should help.

It still of course emits some RF, like all computers. But I am thinking that eliminating non native visible light EMF from my work day should help me.

### brent-next — 2017-12-23T21:35:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/post-230959>

What common mitochondrial poison is very difficult to diagnose, but still results in over 20,000 provable trips to the emergency room and over 400 provable deaths per year in the USA? (1).

Surprisingly, your body makes tiny amounts of this chemical and uses it to regulate your circadian rhythms. (2) (8)

You probably have between five and ten machines that you own, that make this mitochondrial poison in relatively huge amounts. These machines have to be used and maintained 100% perfectly from the time when you first buy them, until they are retired at their end of life in order to not chronically or acutely poison people nearby.

The half life of this poison in your body (when you are breathing air or oxygen) varies considerably between published reports. When breathing air, the half life is quoted as between 3 and 6 hours, so yesterday's accidental dosing and the day before yesterday's add to todays accidental dose.

There may be some people who have much longer half lives of this poison in their body. (7)

This poison mimics the symptoms of many other common illnesses and since it is a mitochondrial poison it can actually cause many very different diseases if I am understanding Doug Wallace's research correctly. (9)

It provably can cause heart disease (3) even after a person has appeared to recover from the acute poisoning phase.

Allow me to humbly introduce to you, everyone's favorite mitochondrial poison, that we all love to make, it's name is carbon monoxide.

Since carbon monoxide poisoning is so difficult to diagnose, would you care to guess how many people really seek medical care or die due to carbon monoxide poisoning? It's likely that there are a lot more than just the provable ones.

You probably know that carbon monoxide meters and alarms exist, you might even have one in your home. But did you know that there are meters that can shine a light through your fingertip and measure the carbon monoxide in your blood? (5) There are also breath testers that can measure how much carbon monoxide you exhale after holding your breath for 20 seconds. (6)

Carbon monoxide is a chronic and acute poison is literally every place where there people live.

To imply that it might be a bigger problem than people typically think is as unacceptable in this society as saying "Fire is no damn good" would have been to our ancestors in caves.

Perhaps my road to optimal health should include just a little bit more study about how this popular poison affects my life. Perhaps yours should too.

Cites:

1\. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbon\_monoxide\_poisoning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbon_monoxide_poisoning)

2\. [https://www.nature.com/articles/nsmb.3331](https://www.nature.com/articles/nsmb.3331)

3\. [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2008/01/080129125412.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2008/01/080129125412.htm)

4\. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10713010](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10713010)

5\. [http://www.masimo.com/home/rainbow-pulse-co-oximetry/rainbow-measurements/spco/](http://www.masimo.com/home/rainbow-pulse-co-oximetry/rainbow-measurements/spco/)

6\. [https://www.bedfont.com/file/2053-2014 - Tseona - Carbon Monoxide Monitoring.pdf](https://www.bedfont.com/file/2053-2014%20-%20Tseona%20-%20Carbon%20Monoxide%20Monitoring.pdf)

7\. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10912868](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10912868)

8\. [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-3-wake-september-2016-ends/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-3-wake-september-2016-ends/)

9.

# Thread: Let me show you something awful
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let-me-show-you-something-awful.19064/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthias — 2017-02-10T20:03:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let-me-show-you-something-awful.19064/post-208365>

Today, I'd like to show you the (metabolically) worst thing in my entire life by far. It's this:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This an induction heater.

_**A 3kW induction heater**_

And I hate it.

We use it to heat up parts at the company I work for. We do a form of laser welding (use big lasers to apply powdered metal to a piece), and sometimes it requires getting the part hot ahead of depositing the additive material.

But this is what we used to do it. And I hate it. I can hear the frequency it operates at, too. And I know that it's wicked powerful, wicked toxic. Let me show you:

You ever known 70mG at 50Hz to be good? And that's just 12 inches away. It's literally off the readout right next to it.

Often I have to be right up next to this thing because that Dremel on the left side of the picture needs to be adjusted during the process.

It doesn't even get much better 12 feet away!

That 3mG is a low number; usually it hovers at 7-8 mG. Guy on the right is my coworker Oleg. Oleg is a real G, and soldiers on through this process. I'm trying to educate him about the problems with non-native EMFs. He answers his cell phone constantly, too. (I can't entirely blame him; he has a wife and two young kids).

\~~~~

Even with my Magnetico and with my (soon incoming) Quantlet, I don't know how much I can hold back the tide on this one.

I don't have to use the induction heater every day, but I take a hit on each day that I do:

The parts we use it on are ~50-60 lbs, and have a weird geometry that make it hard to hold. (Especially when some of it has just been welded and is wicked hot). Anyway, in lifting a bunch of them across the past two weeks, I've found that my forearms, on the ulna side, really really really hurt.

And they really hurt whenever I have to lift these parts. Even after ~a week of rest.

When I chipped my tooth, I remarked that Jack and you guys have all taught me that metabolic coincidences just don't happen. And I don't think that the failure of my arms to recover as a result of their relatively increased exposure are an exception.

I don't know if I want to continue working there if I keep having to do stuff like this.

# Thread: Biophotons redshifted from animals (bullfrog, mouse, chicken, pig, and monkey) to humans
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biophotons-redshifted-from-animals-bullfrog-mouse-chicken-pig-and-monkey-to-humans.19068/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2017-02-11T05:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biophotons-redshifted-from-animals-bullfrog-mouse-chicken-pig-and-monkey-to-humans.19068/post-208385>

This [article](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/biophotons-human-body-emits-communicates-and-made-light) was posted in the Quantum Health facebook page which got me searching pubmed for more recent papers on biophotons. I found this one super interesting "**[Human high intelligence is involved in spectral redshift of biophotonic activities in the brain](http://www.pnas.org/content/113/31/8753.full)"**
> Based on recent knowledge of the level of intelligence, which is related anatomically to the number of cortical neurons and physiologically to the speed of conductivity of neural pathways, the spectral redshift means that the human brain may use lower energy biophotons (longer spectra) to carry out neural signal communications between neurons to be more effective and economical.

I found it interesting because of what Jack has said in the past and in his Time series particularly [Time 2](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-2-how-is-time-built/) which talks about gravitational lensing and redshift.

> Jack said:
>
> Red light slows time. Cold slows time. Blue light speeds up time and your life is lived like a blue straggler star. Quick and bright. Over supplemented comes from being undereducated about light. Light has weight. As it falls to anything with gravity it gets heavier because light has momentum. Light moving away from mass allows light to "get lighter" because it loses its purple power and shifts to red. Gravitational lensing is a redshift effect.

> The 2016 Biophoton paper said:
>
> **Significance**
> It is still unclear why human beings hold higher intelligence than other animals on Earth and which brain properties might explain the differences. The recent studies have demonstrated that biophotons may play a key role in neural information processing and encoding and that biophotons may be involved in quantum brain mechanism; however, the importance of biophotons in relation to animal intelligence, including that of human beings, is not clear. Here, we have provided experimental evidence that glutamate-induced biophotonic activities and transmission in brain slices present a spectral redshift feature from animals (bullfrog, mouse, chicken, pig, and monkey) to humans, which may be a key biophysical basis for explaining why human beings hold higher intelligence than that of other animals.
> ****Abstract****
> Human beings hold higher intelligence than other animals on Earth; however, it is still unclear which brain properties might explain the underlying mechanisms. The brain is a major energy-consuming organ compared with other organs. Neural signal communications and information processing in neural circuits play an important role in the realization of various neural functions, whereas improvement in cognitive function is driven by the need for more effective communication that requires less energy. Combining the ultraweak biophoton imaging system (UBIS) with the biophoton spectral analysis device (BSAD), we found that glutamate-induced biophotonic activities and transmission in the brain, which has recently been demonstrated as a novel neural signal communication mechanism, present a spectral redshift from animals (in order of bullfrog, mouse, chicken, pig, and monkey) to humans, even up to a near-infrared wavelength (∼865 nm) in the human brain. This brain property may be a key biophysical basis for explaining high intelligence in humans because biophoton spectral redshift could be a more economical and effective measure of biophotonic signal communications and information processing in the human brain.

### Alex V — 2017-02-11T06:07:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radar.19069/post-208388>

**According to sources I have consulted online and personal communication with experts, most radar systems operate between 1 and 40 GHz and can sometimes operates at higher frequencies. Radar can thus operate at frequencies well beyond what most RF/MW meters can detect. E.g., I own a TES 593 meter with a frequency range of 10 MHz - 8 GHz, and I currently live a couple miles east of LAX in an area where planes pass overhead nearly every two minutes. Since I am EHS, I can feel the radar from the planes; yet my meter doesn’t seem to pick up the radar.

I was wondering if anyone knew if there was any way for me to measure radar frequencies--in particular, airport radar frequencies? I was also wondering if anyone knew about how the higher radar frequencies behave? For example, can they penetrate through hills much more easily than, say, frequencies used by cellphones? Or when one would be at a safe distance from airport radar?**

### Scompy — 2017-02-11T09:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radar.19069/post-208392>

Try the sound-mode in your RF EMF meters as an idea. You're right...many of the meters I've seen only get up to about 10 or 12 GHz. Some car radar detectors get up into those ranges, but I think they are for specific bands. I previously read that planes have newer radar systems for landing procedures. So in addition to the normal radar, I think they have additional radar systems that kick in as they approach for landing. This would make a lot of sense for setting off EHS.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-11T15:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radar.19069/post-208403>

Here is many cities biggest silent killer: Your new news radar tracking device. The one we have in NOLA is deadly. [http://www.nbcnewyork.com/news/loca...ost-Powerful-Radar-in-New-York-405036715.html](http://www.nbcnewyork.com/news/local/StormTracker-4-The-Most-Powerful-Radar-in-New-York-405036715.html)

Most people do not realize, many local TV broadcasters (such as NBC) have been converting their weather radar systems from C-Band (4.8 Ghz) to S-Band (2.4 Ghz), with a coverage radius of 185+ miles. and the power to slice through everything including us to track bad weather.

StormTracker 4 is a high-frequency S-band radar, of a kind previously reserved for the military and the government. It is the only one of its kind in the tri-state area, which gives NBC 4 greater resolution and clarity than other radar products in the region. (Published Monday, Jan. 30, 2017)

### Optimalbound — 2017-02-11T15:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radar.19069/post-208406>

What makes the S-band radars worse than the C-band ones, despite operating at a lower frequency? Is it the dual polarity or because they have a higher power output?

Thanks for your feedback. Hmm, interesting, that you read that planes have newer radar systems for the landing procedures. But the range of detection would still be too narrow to detect the full range of radar frequencies. But I think sound mode could be very helpful for understanding frequencies within the detection range of the meter.

### Marko Pollo — 2022-05-11T18:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radar.19069/post-312912>

I would proffer that some houses are going pending in an hour. All except cash buyers need an appraisal, which takes time right now. Pre-approval means nada. There are some strategies buyers are using to get their offer accepted over others such as quick or no inspection, quick close, large earnest money, fawning letters to the sellers ect. After acceptance, they try to bicker and stall based on an inspection. The selling agent doesn't want to deal to collapse and will encourage seller to accept lower offer. No one loses earnest money these days. This is an emotional time. While selling our house last summer, this happened to us. The buyer had an inspector question integrity of roof and demanded a $70k price reduction. My selling agent tried to push me into accepting. I said I would relist with an other agent right away, gave the buyer 24 hours to accept original price or move on. They took it. Also, many buyers are making multiple offers and waiting to pick best one. The lack of homes make it seem like millions of buyers, theres not.
During the last housing crash I was buying homes at foreclosure auctions. There could be 100 investors competing in frenzied bidding. I would not partake. By waiting a week or two, at some point the investors and hard money lenders would shoot their wad and there was no one bidding. Scary if you need confirmation the property was a good value, but awesome to get house for $1 over minimum bid.
You two are also retirement age so dont need great schools or prosperous local economy. Looking in a more rural local may be good. If youre seeing lots of out of state buyers, look elsewhere if they are pricing you out. Many only want places with lots of shopping, great connectivity, and other garbage stuff.
Keep your eyes open, trust only yourselves and remember it is your hard earned dough. Good luck.

One of the most wise statements I have seen recently was made about food today. Karen P said, " "Kempner’s Rice Diet program began at Duke University in Durham, North Carolina in 1939. The treatment was a simple therapy of white rice, fruit, juice, and sugar, and was reserved for only the most seriously ill patients. Although low-tech, the benefits of the Rice Diet far exceed those of any drug or surgery ever prescribed for chronic conditions, including coronary artery disease, heart and kidney failure, hypertension, diabetes, arthritis, and obesity."
The truth is, the if we continue to demonize carbohydrates as we once demonized fats, we will effectively be in no better condition to understand the vast nuance of dietary intervention for metabolic diseases as we were in the days of Ancel Keys. History will continuously repeat itself until we pay close attention to information that doesn't fit our current ideology, and instead of fitting information to adapt to our ideology, we fit our ideology to adapt to the information." The key that food gurus miss that it is not the fuel we put into our Ferrari that matters..........it is the engine in that Ferrari that really matters and the engine is CALLED A MITOCHONDRIA.

Fung is more about fasting than any particular diet. He also tells his patients to use whatever type of fasting works best for them - be it intermittent, short term, or long term fasting. Same with diet. He has found that most of his patients do best on a HFLC diet, but he doesn't insist that all his patients use it.

I don't think he ever made a claim that fasting is the answer for perfect health, but that it has been successful at curing type 2 diabetes in many of his patients.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-06T15:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jimmy-moore-january-2017.19071/post-218078>

BLUE LIGHT DRIVES DOPAMINE LEVELS IN THE EYE AND BRAIN AND LEADS TO HOW WE MAKE CHOICES, DECIDE, AND ACT. Jimmy has been blue light toxic for a decade because his business plan does not mitigate the risk so his choroid in his eye is now I bet quite large and leading to his defective central retinal pathways as I discussed in Vermont 2017.
The brain and memory are making predictions based on patterns stored in memory. For example, if you are driving to the store and the traffic light turns yellow, you automatically expect it to soon turn red. OK, now the light is red and you wait and wait and wait and it is still red. Again, your brain knows roughly how long a red light usually lasts and it starts signalling you when it is about to turn green. If it doesn't, you suddenly start paying attention to the light. So based on previous information (pattern) stored in your memory, the brain is making a prediction of what is likely to happen next.

If your past history is to eat ice cream when you are sad, the next time you are sad, your brain reminds you that you usually eat ice cream when sad. Your brain takes all the little sensory information that comes in, stores it in a hierarchical fashion, and tries to make sense of it by creating "beliefs".

I find that just realizing that my beliefs are my brain's attempt to make sense out of the input I have given it over my lifetime, makes it easy for me to see that my beliefs may not be completely accurate and, in fact, may be wrong. So when your brain tells you something is true (or false), realize that it is just making a prediction based on previous patterns it has stored and organized in your memory.

In computer programming, there is a saying "garbage in, garbage out". The same applies to your memory. Your brain makes no value judgements about the information it stores in memory. It just tries to identify patterns and organize it in a "meaningful" way. So maybe it is as simple as just telling your brain that your "belief" has proved to be false and that know you believe xyz. Just keep repeating this until eventually it is a stronger memory than the one you want to get rid of. Do everything sensually you can do to drive home your new belief with sunlight and dopamine and melatonin will fix you.

### DavidL — 2017-07-06T16:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jimmy-moore-january-2017.19071/post-218083>

So how does my brain work now that I've bought in and done what you've said doc? The patterns, habits, and judgements I make on a daily basis has forged a new life, a new identity, I can "see" now. I didn't really know in 2014 where you were going with all this but I decided to stick around even though these new ideas would challenge and scare the shit out of me. The old belief systems I built consciously and unconsciously were once my truths, but as time when on, it appeared I had only had half truths.

Guru's failed me. I put my faith in them that they would get me well and empower me to help others make better choices. Half truths led to whole lies. I no longer follow these gurus, nature is my guru. It wasn't my low carb diet, not my raw vegan diet, not my keto diet, not my blood type diet, it wasn't being vegetarian, drinking coffee, or building my physique to the point where I couldn't swing a golf club. The ambition I once had for being 6% body fat is gone.

Longevity now? What I wouldn't give to get back the $1000-1500 a month I spent on superfoods every month...perhaps it was working shift work, the 3 days a week without melatonin, and sleeping until noon?

I remember when I first stumbled upon this website. I couldn't understand a damn thing. It was horrible and I was horrified by horrible my brain worked. But I went with my gut, rolled the dice and over the last 3 years, it began to click.

What are the patterns now?
1\. relentlessly pursue sunrises.
2\. Eat seafood nearly everyday.
3\. Get grounded outside as much as possible.
3\. Constantly find better ways to improve lighting in the house.
4\. Zero TV.
5\. Never read the food gurus webpages.
6\. Don't buy diet books.
7\. Read the blogs and forum every spare minute I have.
8\. I've learned to keep my head high when people tell me I'm crazy, but maybe I am.
9\. Pursue sunsets.
10\. Sleep when it's dark.
11\. Never work out in a gym under artificial lights.
12\. Smile, laugh, and have fun.
13\. Remain authentic.
14\. Swim in cold water.

Seasonal Ketosis is not nutritional ketosis. Seasonal ketosis is what mammals employ with their fat mass and is an epigenetic tool to deplete deuterium and it is not a human panacea as the LCHF guys believe. Remember food you can live without for 30 days but you cannot live without water for 7. 75% of our body is water that has two hydrogens. This means getting your water deuterium depleted is way more important than we think. The metabolic machinations of the hydrogens in TCA intermediates and glycerol and glucose is the real job of metabolism and that is all they count for. Counterintuitive quantum biology perspective you get when you understand mitochondria well. Most bio-chemists don't.

In fact, ketosis is a circadian fine tuning tool cells use as the solar spectrum varies in habitats we choose to live to get rid of deuterium…….and we don’t realize it in the research world yet. When you know better, you simply do better...........the sad part is few of your experts today know better. Nothing in cell biology makes sense except under the solar spectrum of evolution. ### Scompy — 2017-02-11T21:57:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-208428>

I have this instinct: Someone "high up" made a calculated choice of Austin, TX for their first 5G trials. Why? To confuse the epidemiology on purpose. Austin is a place where a TON of people have been fleeing to from California, so the population is harder to pin. Next, Austin has good sunlight. That means those that use the sun or accidentally use the sun will help to mitigate a lot of the 5G tech they are putting in. In other words, it will be very hard to make sense of the data, and also to ALTER the data to show 5G is benign when people first look at Austin to track if 5G looks like a health hazard.

SmartEpidemiologists(TM) need to put on their thinking caps. They should monitor personal behaviors in SmartCities to know if they are actively sunbathing, wearing sunglasses and getting outside for at least part of the day... maybe looking at roofers that are forced to be on buildings in the sun vs. office workers that miss most of the morning sun, while also under 5G. If the roofer end up getting more diseases as well, it will be very, very telling.
