### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-12T16:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-208452>

This 5G story and vaccines are going collide.
Not too long ago, lethal infections were feared in the Western world. Since that time, many countries have undergone a transformation from disease cesspools to much safer, healthier habitats. Starting in the mid-1800s, there was a steady drop in deaths from all infectious diseases, decreasing to relatively minor levels by the early 1900s. The history of that transformation involves famine, poverty, filth, lost cures, eugenicist doctrine, individual freedoms versus state might, protests and arrests over vaccine refusal, and much more. Today, we are told that medical interventions increased our lifespan and single-handedly prevented masses of deaths. But is this really true? Dissolving Illusions details facts and figures from long-overlooked medical journals, books, newspapers, and other sources. Using myth-shattering graphs, this book shows that vaccines, antibiotics, and other medical interventions are not responsible for the increase in lifespan and the decline in mortality from infectious diseases. If the medical profession could systematically misinterpret and ignore key historical information, the question must be asked, “What else is ignored and misinterpreted today?” Perhaps the best reason to know our history is so that the worst parts are never repeated. If you want to educate yourself on both parts of this story you'd be wise to start with Dr. Suzanne Humphries books on the subject. After my own experiences in the healthcare system, (Hepatitis B vaccine we were forced to take in dental and medical school) and thoroughly examining both sides of the vaccination debate, it became clear to me that the issue on this "science" was not ‘settled long ago, and laid to rest’ as most of the medical profession says it is.
The history of vaccination is more complicated than most people understand. The anti-vaccine movement is HUNDREDS of years old. It heated up in the 1800s, when parents in the UK became fed up with watching their healthy infants and children become ill or die shortly after the smallpox vaccinations (Edward Jenner) or get sick from smallpox anyway. Parents and doctors who refused smallpox vaccines, risked losing their homes, their furniture and their livelihoods if Judges ruled against them.
The smallpox vaccines were made from pus scraped off of diseased cows’ belly sores, contaminated with disease matter from a variety of animals and in some cases, humans. The smallpox vaccine history is probably not what you think it is.
Doctors and those administering vaccines are SUPPOSED to obtain “informed consent” before vaccinating. Informed consent is not possible because parents are not given all the information they would require in order to understand the most important issues.
I DON'T CONSIDER IT MY PLACE TO TELL ANYONE WHETHER TO VACCINATE OR NOT. I NEVER TELL OR ADVISE PEOPLE NOT TO VACCINATE.
As a physician I believe it is my place to understand as much as I can about vaccine science and history and give people a more complete understanding from which to make their choices. If you are interested in learning more about Dr. Humphries work you can email her too @ [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]). [https://amicrowavedplanet.com/a-deadly-cocktail-vaccines-and-electromagnetic-radiation/](https://amicrowavedplanet.com/a-deadly-cocktail-vaccines-and-electromagnetic-radiation/)

### WalterNL — 2017-02-13T18:11:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-208520>

But to point out the obvious: everyone in telecom (from mechanic to CEO to shareholders) is going to be affected too, their kids will be affected too, no amount of money can shield anyone from the stressors of modern life they voluntarily endure. (Have you seen Bill Gates? He ages like everyone, 85 billion USD doesn't appear to help.) Those small 5G cell towers will be on every house in their neighborhoods too. I agree that 5G will be implemented with enthusiasm by the telecom sector, but they too will suffer because of it. Whether they can connect their own health issues to technology remains to be seen, but I believe it will become clear. You deny reality for a while, especially when your paycheck depends on it, but not indefinitely.

And besides that, you shouldn't underestimate societal reactions. There is little the people won't destroy when they see their families suffer. You can already see small reactions like parents who violently oppose their kids having to use ipads at school from age 4. That's how it starts. There is no linear 'progress' (or however you want to call 5G), sometimes the system breaks down. So my optimistic take: the people will connect the dots, maybe around 6 or 7G.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T03:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-208619>

Scotty here is some more bad news I found out about and may link to the faked NOAA climate data before Paris: NOAA Next Generation Radar (NEXRAD) Level III Products This dataset consists of Level III weather radar products collected from Next-Generation Radar (NEXRAD) stations located in the contiguous United States, Alaska, Hawaii, U.S. territories and at military base sites. NEXRAD is a network of 160 high-resolution Doppler weather radars operated by the NOAA National Weather Service (NWS), the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), and the U.S. Air Force (USAF). Doppler radars detect atmospheric precipitation and winds, which allow scientists to track and anticipate weather events, such as rain, ice pellets, snow, hail, and tornadoes, as well as some non-weather objects like birds and insects. NEXRAD stations use the Weather Surveillance Radar - 1988, Doppler (WSR-88D) system. This is a 10 cm wavelength (S-Band) radar that operates at a frequency between 2,700 and 3,000 MHz.

RF devices wont be able to measure these windows. This means coastly communities have a new thing to worry about.

### Limpei — 2017-05-23T11:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-214462>

Hi guys,

I live in Singapore. Unfortunately the country I call home is also moving towards the 5G direction.

**Singapore waives frequency fees for 5G trials**[http://www.channelnewsasia.com/news/singapore/singapore-waives-frequency-fees-for-5g-trials-8873408](http://www.channelnewsasia.com/news/singapore/singapore-waives-frequency-fees-for-5g-trials-8873408)

Globally, it's adopt 5G or be left behind economically going forward.
This is a beast we all have to tackle.
The march of technological progress will not stop, and we best deal with it.
I've got 2 little girls, and it bothers the hell out of me that we are going to be bombarded with with even higher powered nnEMF.

This has got me reading through Dr K's stuff + other books (Overpowered, Martin Blank).
.

So far, I've not come across solutions on the forum (pls post links you think are helpful. Forum's a huge place).

From what I've read this is what I gather,
(1) Get more sun
(2) Reduce/turn off "G" options on phone (4G can be dipped to 3G, or turned off completely when required. No wifi)
(3) Non-flouridated water
(4) CT
(5) Decent protein from marine/high quality meats for DHA + collagen

CT + IR + Good water = Builds internal EZ.
EZ acts as an internal faraday cage for cells

(6) Faraday cages
Anyone have details on this?
I read on facebook that Dr Kruse has installed wire mesh around his place.
Minus that, no other proper practical info.

Found some answers
[https://www.quora.com/How-can-I-insulate-my-room-from-WiFi-signals](https://www.quora.com/How-can-I-insulate-my-room-from-WiFi-signals)

I'm gathering practical "To Do"s to hedge against a higher nnEMF future.

### Scompy — 2017-05-23T20:03:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-214515>

And everyone ~under~ omitting the sun will be looking to hijack the infrastructure that already exists, like this:

"Just had a major run around with Comcast. We recently switched from Frontier DSL to Comcast, thinking they would be wired internet instead of wireless. Comcast confirmed this, yet about a week after having their service, I noticed that my EMF Microalert meter was registering microwave radiation from the Comcast router. I called Comcast, and they insisted the wifi was turned off. I found out that Comcast uses private home routers as wifi hot spots for the public. They turned off the wifi hot spot, but there was still a hidden network. It took a few hours on the phone with Comcast to get them to agree to turn this off. It seems that the wifi hotspot is a 2.4 GHz and the hidden network is a 5.0 GHz (aka 5G). Basically, it seems to me like they are using our home routers as mini cell towers now, unbeknownst to most of us. I will now keep my MicroAlert EMF meter next to my computer in case they decide to secretly turn on these wifi networks again without my knowledge. I still may go back to Frontier DSL, as my trust has been eroded."

"What bothered me the most was how covert they were about it all. They said I have a dual bandwidth router-with both 2.4 Gigahertz and 5.0Gigahertz capabilities. They turned on the 2.4 Ghz as an "xfinity wifi hot spot." They turned on the 5.0 Ghz (5G technology emitting as a mini cell site from my router!!) and it listed as a "Hidden Network" on my computer when I opened the wifi status tab on my computer. It was only after talking with several people at Comcast that I figured out what was going on, and only because I have an EMF meter that I could confirm the wifi was not turned off. It just makes me wonder how many people think they have wifi off, when it is really still on."

The relation between high/low quantum yield, dopamine system and choices is very interesting. Intuitively people already know the answer for addiction; addiction counselors would say don't go back to your old environment (meaning old social circumstances). What they need to learn is the total story of the environment including sun, nnEMF and all we talk about.

I also believe we see this exact interaction (but less extreme) with people who live in northern America/Europe, go south for the holidays and turn into different people, then return home and lose the feeling/better outlook they had in the sun. Of course it's an N=1 story so it won't be the same for every individual. Perhaps some can break the cycle with a mere one week in a better environment, another would have to remain there for the rest of his/her life.

I can add a little more. The Comcast xfinity routers will show up on old client devices as only the 2.4Ghz. Most devices can see the 5Ghz band, listed as Whatever-5, the "-5" added to your network's name. The xfinity hotspot is currently on the 2.4Ghz band so as to be visible to all devices, old and new.

We probably want to disable the 2.5Ghz named Whatever, band, and go with only the 5Ghz named Whatever-5, band. We need to poke around and see if we have to call Comcast to turn off their "xfinity" hotspot. I don't think the "xfinity" hotspots are hidden. They offer a 24 hour free trial per MAC, and then an expensive subscription price for wifi-only service paid by credit card. Since the "xfinity" hotspots show up to any device, it doesn't take a meter to see if they're turned on. As for turning them off, maybe we can figure out how to turn off the 2.4Ghz band and thus halve our Wifi exposure and thereby turn off the xfinity hotspot, ourselves.

### BobDodds — 2017-12-23T02:09:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-230906>

At the same time, the cable and wifi providers are trying to blanket cover, like cell, everywhere with wifi hotspots. In residential areas at this point, when we see "xfinity", we may be seeing our own wifi router and not a box on a telephone pole. In commercial areas, I think there are some dedicated, standalone wifi hotspots not billing to Starbucks, Panera, McDonald's, and maybe they are mounted on telephone poles. In DC there is a spotty free wifi network with identifiable, understandable hotspot naming as such.

In addition to the dozen or more hotspots/routers we see from our neighbors, realize that every single one of those is set up to broadcast on both 2.4Ghz and 5Ghz, despite most devices being able to see and log on at 5Ghz now. If I turn off my 2.4Ghz, two neighbors are still going strong on either side on 2.4Ghz.

If we move to a rural location, it is economical for co-ops to install fiber. If a locality does not go for fiber, a contractor will probably come in with wifi from a hilltop or mountaintop or existing tower or put up a tower. "WISP". One more reason to co-op install fiber is to head off wifi pollution of a lovely sanctuary.

To their credit, wisp's put New Orleans and the Gulf Coast back online for voice-over-ip, internet, and even municipal police and emt communication, following Hurricane Katrina. They were jumping 25 to 50 miles dish-to-dish to reach the outside. There were over 500 airboat crews volunteering, with 10,000 police and emt volunteers from other states, all blocked by Bush-Cheney "fighting the last war" helicopter sales security, from showing the world the unfortunately thus still not real yet America.

Rural fiber DIY/Co-op-ing: [https://www.startpage.com/do/search?q=rural+fiber+to+the+home](https://www.startpage.com/do/search?q=rural+fiber+to+the+home)
WISP, WISPA: [http://www.wispa.org/](http://www.wispa.org/)

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-12-30T01:33:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-5g-article.19074/post-259210>

Photos show some pretty typical base station flat panel antennas. Could be transmitting 4G LTE signals-- not sure about 5G.

In other news, AT&T is crowing about **5G is now here in Dec 2018** (article in _SafeHaven_):

"The world’s largest telecom, **AT&T** (NYSE:T), had a self-imposed deadline to launch 5G services in 12 U.S. cities before the end of 2018. Well, the telecom giant says it has earned the bragging rights as the first mobile carrier to launch the next-generation mobile technology after rolling out the service in the following cities: Atlanta, Charlotte, Dallas, Houston, Indianapolis, Jacksonville, Louisville, Oklahoma City, New Orleans, Raleigh, San Antonio and Waco.

The service will become available on December 21 along with the first 5G-compatible device, **Netgear Inc.**’s (NASDAQ:NTGR) Nighthawk 5G Mobile Hotspot. Initial adopters will be rewarded with generous perks, including data usage at no cost for at least 90 days. In the spring, users will have to part with $499 upfront for Nighthawk and 15GB of data for $70 a month on a compatible plan.

AT&T plans to add seven more cities to the mix in the first half of 2019. These are: Orlando, Las Vegas, Nashville, Los Angeles, San Diego, San Francisco, and San Jose."

I've been practicing for 30 years and have seen just about everything a body can get and manifest - from A-Z. Out of everything, there are only 2 things I will not treat or even say I can help, solely by natural means (vitamins, herbs...). If anyone says they can, I call BS. The 2 are chronic nail fungus and infections of the teeth. 20 years ago, I could handle toenail fungus; not anymore. Infections needing a root canal/extraction need immediate attention and until handled, the body will not and can't begin to heal.

The increase in EMF over the last 20 years has made gram negative bacteria and their biofilm more resistant to treatment (H. pylori, Pseudomonas...) as well as fungal infections. Both can still be treated effectively and quickly as long appropriate natural and pharmacological interventions are done together, and before the issues become too chronic. Those here also know the other measures that need to be done to create a more effective healing environment within our body, cells, and mitochondria.

On a side note, one hack that I find very effective to help jump start a chronically ill patient (and I mean full body, mitochondria shut down) is based on the Marik Protocol for the treatment of sepsis/septic shock. I use 150mg's of benfotiamine BID, 500mg's of lemon bioflavonoids BID, 500mg's of ascorbic acid BID, and 2.5mg's of low dose naltrexone. Even though supplemental forms of these are not recommended by Jack, If I can't get a patient to consume seafood and/or they can't get Sun for whatever reason, I use in conjunction to the above 50,000IU's vitamin D3 per week and 1000mg's of DHA per day. I have pulled people out of some deep dark places with this. This is a TEMPORARY protocol until the Redox Rx and other measures (light/water/magnetism) begin to kick in.

"The more DHA and sulfated vitamin D3 you have in your tissues the more back-up power source you have to generate a larger electromotive force in your mitochondria to generate the DC electric current. If your battery cannot hold the charge from the Earth or sun, for any reason, you get ill." J.K., MD

# Thread: Ultrasound+MRI
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2017-02-12T00:22:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-208435>

Yeah, nothing to see here but a happy baby, until the seeing of the baby causes neurodegeneration:

"MRI scanning is usually only used by doctors when an [ultrasound](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/science/science-news/11489538/Unborn-baby-shown-grimacing-in-womb-as-mother-smokes.html) fails to provide images of sufficiently high quality to check the development of the baby. But this is the first time the two techniques have been combined."

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/...nborn-children-first-time-3d-virtual-reality/](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/2016/11/21/parents-can-meet-unborn-children-first-time-3d-virtual-reality/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-12T17:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-208456>

Im not a fan of either.........but if the diagnosis dictates imaging you have to give informed consent for both procedures.

### nonchalant — 2017-02-14T00:34:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-208536>

So glad my obstetrician wasn't in favor of an ultrasound just to check on my babies in the 1980's. Being hesitant to embrace the new technologies is a good thing.

### brent-next — 2017-09-03T02:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-222774>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=208435)
>
> Yeah, nothing to see here but a happy baby, until the seeing of the baby causes neurodegeneration:

I know the medical industry's view of ultrasound is that it is harmless and good. Putting aside the MRI issue for a minute, what do you think about prenatal ultrasounds which are done for reasons (like to know the sex of the baby)?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-09-04T10:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-222836>

Gah, I had three with both my babies...

### brent-next — 2017-09-04T21:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-222870>

My recently born son had six prenatal ultrasounds. That seems like too many to me, but I don't know what the specific side effects can be.

### CatherineRaye — 2018-09-15T02:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-251011>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=208536)
>
> So glad my obstetrician wasn't in favor of an ultrasound just to check on my babies in the 1980's. Being hesitant to embrace the new technologies is a good thing.

I haven't had an OB for any of my pregnancies, but I am grateful my mother didn't have one with me. Our family doctor asked my mother if she was going to abort me if they found anything wrong on the ultrasound. When she said no, he said don't get one, we don't know enough about what they do yet.

People have thought I am nuts not getting them, but even the American Pregnancy Association says not to get them unless they are "medically indicated", but since a lot of MDs have decided that pregnancy is a disease to be feared, I guess they have decided being pregnant is a medical indication for ultrasound.

"There is not a recommended number of ultrasounds that should be performed during routine prenatal care. Because ultrasound should only be used when medically indicated, many healthy pregnancies will not require an ultrasound." [http://americanpregnancy.org/prenatal-testing/ultrasound/](http://americanpregnancy.org/prenatal-testing/ultrasound/)

### isisandgranta — 2019-01-27T14:56:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-261536>

[https://www.amazon.com/Studies-Cond...=UTF8&qid=1548593727&sr=8-1&keywords=Jim+West](https://www.amazon.com/Studies-Conducted-Indicate-Prenatal-Ultrasound/dp/1941719031/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1548593727&sr=8-1&keywords=Jim+West)

### Anderson Joe — 2019-02-04T21:40:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound-mri.19075/post-262286>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=208536)

100% Agreed. It saved my mom's life.

# Thread: Gassy Baby at Night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gassy-baby-at-night.19078/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sam Richardson — 2017-02-12T19:38:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gassy-baby-at-night.19078/post-208466>

Our two week old baby has been having some rough cry sessions these past couple of nights. He's good throughout the day, actuallt sleeps through most of it, but once the night hits, oh boy. The only thing that really calms him down seems to be the sound of the dryer and the red light on his face that the dryer emits whike it is turned on (wonder if there is an explanation for this). I'm going to switch the lighting to amber bulbs in the house in hopes that they will help soothe him at night.

As for the bad gas, I assume that it is coming from an environmental mismatch since we live in Toronto, Canada and have not been getting out in the cold weather too much or getting sunlight. We aren't eating seasonally either, so I wonder if that it playing a role. How important is my wife's diet for the baby's breast milk? Our midwife told us that due to circadian biology, the mother's breast milk will become more fatty at night to facilitate sleep for the baby. Could it be the fattier milk that is causing the gas? Might my wife need to get out in the cold more, in particular, will it change the biochemistry of her milk.? How safe is it to expose a two week old baby to the cold as well?

### bionaut — 2017-02-12T20:06:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gassy-baby-at-night.19078/post-208473>

Your wife's diet can make the baby gassy. You'll have to widdle down what it may be. We used to live by a highway which would wake our twin babies often. So ambient noise is problematic, we did use a fan and had no issues but this can affect how children filter noise so use at your own risk.

Babies have a lot of brown fat, so they should be good in the cold, obviously use common sense and err on the side of caution. We would take them out for 5 min at a time to get some sun when it was the strongest in winter.

My newest one is 6 months at this point lights do affect his sleep. Change all the lights in your house for your own sake you will sleep better. Its typical for kids to cry at night, my advice to you would be to buy a pressure cooker, and make a shellfish broth, with beef bones, with cabbage, kelp, mushrooms. Add turmeric and pepper in yours, not hers, it can slow milk production. You want to beef up your glutathione production so you can deal better on less sleep. Also a 10 minute practice of mindfulness meditation each day goes a long way in keeping your cool when things get hard. Goodluck.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I've used NIR on my Forehead and Thyroid. Some say that they can feel cognitive benefits from forehead application, but I haven't found that to be the case. That doesn't mean it is not effective. # Thread: Cornet RF, EMF readings... Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### krw2586 — 2017-02-14T01:04:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208539>

I just got my Cornet 88T meter and was wondering if someone could help me understand the readings. I get WHAT is being measured, but I have no context for which values are high and what an ideal measurement would be. The meter is automatically programmed to associate readings with colors based on how safe it is: green, yellow, and red. Are those interpretations trustworthy?

I am surprised to find that the RF readings in my whole apartment are always green and range from about 0.01 to 0.3 mW/m2. The only time they look higher are if I'm within about a foot of my laptop. I live in an apartment that is above the garage of a single family home. The wifi router is in the main house, which is separated by a cement wall. My signal is a little weak, but the plus is that I'm not close to the router. Are my RF readings ideal?

I did test the Cornet standing near the wifi router and it lit up red, but I only had to move about 5 feet away before I was supposedly in the green safe zone again. Could that really be right? If that's all it takes to be safe then how are cell towers so dangerous since they're much father away?

Regarding the EMF/LF mode, most measurements are between 0.02 to 0.07 uT. I am using the LF30 mode, which measures from 0.1mG to 10mG and has a frequency range of 50Hz to 1KHZ.

I was noticing that where I sleep I often get body pains, edema, and sore lymph nodes and that happens to be where the levels are at 0.07uT. I believe there is a main electrical wire that comes into my wall right there. It feeds into the electrical box in the garage which is underneath me. But there are layers of cork below my floor, which I think might absorb EMFs?

Thank you so much for the help!

### Scompy — 2017-02-14T06:48:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208553>

If you have 0.3 mW/square meter on average when you sleep, it's a bit too high. Trying to keep it under 0.1 is advised, and even down to 0.01 is recommended. When you get closer to RF emitters, you'll get more density near them as a bit of a square-root falloff, but reflection and absorption can matter also.

For sleeping space, evaluate with your magnetic and electrical modes to be sure you're not in a field... typically under 1 milliGauss is good.

### Scompy — 2017-02-14T06:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208554>

FYI... if your circuit breaker box is below you in the garage, where is the smart meter? Closeby?

### krw2586 — 2017-02-14T16:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208567>

Thanks! This is very helpful. I'll try to track down the RF signals and see why it's going up to 0.3 mW/m squared. I have a Roku, which I think can create it's own wifi signal so maybe it is that.

For the magnetic and electrical modes, the readings on my bed are anywhere from 0.02 to 0.07 uT. In parentheses the meter says that is 0.3 to 0.7 mG, so hopefully that's ok. But I'm hypersensitive to EMFs so when it's at 0.7 mG, I have shooting pains and a very stiff, achy spine.

The strongest EMF field is definitely that wall behind my bed and I can even find the precise area on the wall where it goes up to about 1 mG. It's about a 2 foot band that runs along the length of the entire wall. You are right, I looked outside and the smart meter is on the same wall, but below me (it's attached to the outside of the garage and I'm above the garage). All the electrical cords must feed in from there, right?

I also realized that my HVAC unit is in the garage and in the corner below my bed, super close to both the smart meter and the electrical box. When the heat comes on, my EMF meter goes up...

I have been trying to capture the burst of RF from the smart meter and haven't been able to yet. I read they send signals about every 3 minutes, but I have stayed still with the RF mode for about 5 minutes and haven't seen any bursts. I'm wondering if my meter doesn't send wireless signals. There are still some meters in my area that use wired signaling. I also live somewhere that doesn't have a super close cell tower, which of course neighbors complain about, but I'm thankful for it. Of course there are still hundreds of cell towers near me so maybe "close" is relative.

### Scompy — 2017-02-14T19:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208577>

You probably need to consider moving to a new location in the future or moving the bed farthest away from the wall with the HVAC, meter and such if they spike when they turn on. But only 0.3 to 0.7 mG is pretty darn good. You also may need more grounding overall because you're sleeping elevated, if you know a good spot. My home setup in Seattle was a lot like yours and we had similar issues because of the rooms above the garage. I also had a bit of stray voltage coming into the bed itself. We had that because we had carpet and metal connecting to it, so when I measured my bed, I was surprised.

Depending on the smart meter, they can vary...some turn on at 3am for an hour. Some pulse like every 15 to 30 seconds, some do like you said. Some are still analog. It's a mess.

### JanSz — 2017-02-14T19:54:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208579>

> [krw2586 said:](/goto/post?id=208539)
>
> I was noticing that where I sleep I often get body pains, edema, and sore lymph nodes

We have a lymphatic system that must drain properly.
In large way drainage is under gravity and by muscle movements.
We need to move for the system to work.

There is lymphatic massage.
I think it is best if done by doctor not a masseuse.
Osteopath doctor. DO

/
Standing on a head ok.
Hanging of the feet ng.

//

### JoeT — 2017-02-14T21:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208583>

> [krw2586 said:](/goto/post?id=208539)
>
> I just got my Cornet 88T meter and was wondering if someone could help me understand the readings. I get WHAT is being measured, but I have no context for which values are high and what an ideal measurement would be. The meter is automatically programmed to associate readings with colors based on how safe it is: green, yellow, and red. Are those interpretations trustworthy?
>
> I am surprised to find that the RF readings in my whole apartment are always green and range from about 0.01 to 0.3 mW/m2. The only time they look higher are if I'm within about a foot of my laptop. I live in an apartment that is above the garage of a single family home. I believe there is a main electrical wire that comes into my wall right there. It feeds into the electrical box in the garage which is underneath me. But there are layers of cork below my floor, which I think might absorb EMFs?

I have that meter and can just make a few observational comments. You didn't mention if you were in a rural area or not, but you did mention an apartment. Those RF readings seems very fishy, especially since you have Wifi running. For instance, to get my readings not even as low as what you are seeing, i use this mesh over my wifi.

[https://www.lessemf.com/computer.html](https://www.lessemf.com/computer.html)

Second, you should see noticable spikes in the LF30 (magnetic) readings around the incoming electrical main on the other side of your bedroom (to at least a few Mg I think), and around most appliance etc.

### Penny — 2017-02-17T00:52:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-rf-emf-readings-help.19080/post-208749>

**RadioBio: DARPA To Explore Cell-to-Cell Communications**
**Investigating “Natural Antennas” Sending and Receiving Messages at kHz to THz Frequencies**

February 16, 2017
The Pentagon wants to know more about how cells use electromagnetic radiation to talk to each other.

The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, better known as [DARPA](http://www.darpa.mil/) or ARPA, is embarking on a new program, called RadioBio, to determine whether cells are able to exchange information with EM signals and, if so, what the cells are saying and how they do it.

DARPA is the high-risk, high-reward research arm of the Department of Defense. Created in 1958, after the Soviets launched Sputnik, the first man-made satellite, DARPA bills itself as “creating breakthrough technologies for national security.” Over the years, it has been instrumental in developing the Internet, weather satellites, GPS and drones among many other high-tech tools. Even Siri, Apple’s virtual assistant, has its roots in DARPA.

“DARPA develops the weapons of the future,” says [Sharon Weinberger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sharon_Weinberger), the author of _The Imagineers of War: The Untold Story of DARPA, the Pentagon Agency That Changed the World,_ which will be published next month by [Knopf](http://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/234382/the-imagineers-of-war-by-sharon-weinberger/9780385351799/). “It funds everything from basic science to advanced technology, though the ultimate goal is always to develop something that can be used by the military,” she told us in an interview.

“The RadioBio program will determine the validity of electromagnetic biosignaling claims and, where evidence exists, learn how the structure and function of … natural ‘antennas’ are capable of generating and receiving information in a noisy, cluttered electromagnetic environment,” DARPA states in its [announcement](https://www.fbo.gov/utils/view?id=6f3ed7fc42a93859559f3928b17ab26c) issued yesterday, February 15.

DARPA notes that some of these interactions are “well known,” citing as examples vision, photosynthesis, bioluminescence, neural networks and magnetic navigation.

Those awarded DARPA grants under RadioBio will propose “specific EM signaling mechanisms” and then experimentally test those hypotheses. The program will be divided into two phases, each lasting two years. The first will focus on the theoretical models and the second on testing their validity.

DARPA is open to investigating a broad range of frequencies from kHz to THz in the far infrared (1 THz = 1,000 GHz), and possibly as high as PHz (1 PHz = 1,000 THz). “We are not excluding any frequencies from consideration if a proposal has a good approach for definitely determining whether purposeful signaling occurs in a biosystem,” a DARPA spokesman told _Microwave News_ on behalf of Mike Fiddy, a program manager in DARPA's Defense Sciences Office.

When asked about the size of the RadioBio, the spokesman was non-committal, saying only that the budget will be determined, in part, by the number and quality of proposals received.

DARPA will host a [Proposer’s Day](https://www.fbo.gov/index?s=opportunity&mode=form&id=8d7980f7ad846f51c385f17f96cf93d2&tab=core&_cview=0) on February 21 to brief those interested in submitting proposals (advance registration required).

**Memories of Project Pandora**

Many of these ideas about cell-to-cell signaling are not new. Twenty-five years ago, [Ross Adey](http://microwavenews.com/news-center/ross-adey) described how cells “can whisper together across the barrier of cell membranes.” Such messages, he believed, could control complex biological processes. Further, Adey maintained that external EM radiation could also activate, overwhelm or muddle such processes. These are more commonly known as non-thermal effects.

Back in the 1960’s, Adey worked on a top-secret DARPA project, called Pandora, to investigate the effects of low levels of microwave radiation. The project was initiated after the U.S. government discovered that the Soviets were beaming microwaves at its embassy in Moscow.

In her new book, Weinberger calls Project Pandora, “One of the more bizarre episodes in the history of Cold War science.”

For more information on RadioBio, see the full [announcement](https://www.fbo.gov/index?s=opportunity&mode=form&id=a4a233d189fe4c2c6bc9e967dcfc804c&tab=core&_cview=0) and the [news release](http://www.darpa.mil/news-events/2017-02-07) from DARPA’s public affairs office. Those interested in seeking funds are on a tight schedule: Full proposals are due on April 12.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Set f.lux to 900k (no blue or green light)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2017-02-14T05:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/post-208551>

The current version of f.lux gives a user the ability to adjust the lowest color temperature of the screen to 1200K for the nighttime setting. At 1200k most blue is eliminated, but there is still a lot of green. It's possible to set the color temperature even lower, but that involves going into the registry and manually changing the values. [Here are some directions](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/#post-200138) on how to do that.

The above method is difficult and time consuming to implement. I wanted to be able to quickly change the settings on several computers so I made a vbscript that does it all in one click. The script will change the f.lux settings in the registry for both the day/night to 900k, and then restart f.lux to apply the new settings. It should work on any Windows PC

**Download a VBscript to change f.lux to 900k**
Download the script here: [tiny.cc/FluxScript](http://tiny.cc/FluxScript)

**Directions**

1.  Install f.lux on a Windows PC
2.  Double-click the script after downloading and it will automatically run. The registry settings will be changed to 900k, and you will see f.lux will restart to apply the new settings.
    _\*\*Warning: When f.lux restarts, the screen will suddenly change to full 6000K and then slowly transition down to 900K. You may want to sheild your eyes.\*\*_

If you want to see the code in the script that makes it all happen, right click on the script and select "Open with" notepad.

**Testing blue/green blocking**
To test how well blue/green are being blocked, I open this image and visually check how much blue/green I can see: [RGB.png](http://i.imgur.com/rwemyMX.png)
At 1200K a bit of blue and a lot of green can be seen. At 900K, all blue and green disappears from the RGB image:
[F.lux at 1200K](http://i.imgur.com/9pocr9u.jpg?2)
[F.lux at 900K](http://i.imgur.com/K4xFUKJ.jpg?2)

I really like having the screen at 900K anytime after sunset. Please let me know if you try the script and run into any issues.

### Rubicon — 2017-02-15T01:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/post-208599>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=208586)
>
> wouldn't something like 2000k be better for day time, lots more red than blue but a bit of blue to signal day time?

My understanding is that blue has damaging side-effects in the eye that are typically mitigated & balanced by IR & red light. Since a computer monitor has no IR, I'm not sure even a low level of blue light from a monitor is "better for daytime".

Currently I use 1200K during the day to give a bit of color definition, and only go higher for brief periods if I have a reason to have true color. In the evenings, I wear blue blocking glasses and set the monitor to 900K.
I get some natural light through the window all day and take breaks outside as much as possible to get full spectrum light.

Perhaps 2000K wouldn't really matter if one was using @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) 's setup as described here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### brent-next — 2017-03-13T12:31:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/post-210233>

> [Alper said:](/goto/post?id=209233)
>
> Any ideas for mac os x?

One work around for Macs is to re-set your time zone on your computer so that f.lux thinks it's your bedtime. This will allow you to set the color temperature as low as 1200 K.

If you have any timing applications that rely on your time zone being set correctly, this won't work, but for most people it should be just fine.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/post-216982>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=216805)
>
> Iris Pro goes down to 200K and has a ton of features such as Flicker illimination, rest breaks, multiple monitor support, custom fonts. Can't find black room mode though.

Can you teach me how to eliminate flicker on iris pro? not sure this is in the settings tab of Iris Pro. I think the flicker effect is built in the display itself, so unless you get a flicker free screen I don't think there is another workaround it.

Dark room mode go to settings - screen effects - simple inversion. and turn screen effects on.
Or you can go to color temperature settings tab and click invert colors.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/set-f-lux-to-900k-no-blue-or-green-light.19081/post-227920>

An easy way to test blue light coming from a screen -> open this image on your phone after your blue blocking app or setting is activated:

[RBG.png](http://i.imgur.com/rwemyMX.png)

If you can see the blue circle then it's a good indication that there's still an issue. Full green is not great either.

In my experience using Twilight on Android, the app is terribly ineffective! The Night Shift mode I've seen activated on iPhones is even worse.

# Thread: Does Winter Swimming = Cold Thermogenesis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### brent-next — 2017-02-14T09:20:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/post-208555>

Last year I discovered the Jack Kruse web site in early October and learned about Cold Thermogenesis (CT). Since I was in Ukraine where there is a long tradition of winter swimming, I thought I would try winter swimming and hopefully I would stay healthy in the winter.

Boy was I wrong! I swam in the river every day and tried to adapt myself to the cold as much as possible by walking around town lightly dressed with my big winter coat under my arm in case I really needed it. I did this from October until the middle of January, when I got the worst flue of my life. I also developed unexplained leg swelling which persisted until I was able to start sunbathing (in a house with less microwaves) in April.

When I got sick, I was eating lots of fish, drinking lots of home made bone broth, exercising regularly and using UV sun lamps regularly. On the down side, there was very little natural sunshine, my apartment had too much wi-fi in it (although I turned mine off) my apartment didn't have a tub for CT, and the only way to get my apartment cool was to open the window.

I swam like the locals did which was typically a 15 to 30 second swim in the river every day. The video below explains what it's like.

So why did I get so terribly sick in spite of doing many things right? Here are my guesses.

1\. It's very had to beat the averages. Millions of people in Ukraine got this flue and 350 people died from it. This was considered a fairly normal year for flue deaths. Note to self. Never spend another winter where people commonly die from the flue. This winter I've stayed healthy (but haven't made much progress on my road to optimal) in Cyprus.

2\. Perhaps the heat and microwaves in my apartment were a bigger detriment than the cold was a benefit? I did turn my Wi-Fi off completely and shut off my power at night and only used dim candles in my apartment after sunset.

3\. Sunlamps can't replace strong natural sunshine. In fact the ozone created by my uv lamps probably contributed to my lung irritation.

4\. I should have been very careful about warming the air I breathed with a scarf over my nose when I was on the street.

5\. Eating a high fat ketogenic diet resulted in more belching and this probably contributed to my lung irritation and sickness. First you belch up a little bit of acid -- then you inhale that acid and irritate your lungs. I should have reduced my fat intake slightly until my digestion improved.

My guess is that 15 to 30 seconds of winter swimming, European style, really doesn't help you that much compared to one hour of CT in a cold bathtub. The winter swimming does trigger the good feelings, but it wasn't enough to keep me healthy in the winter.

In my understanding of CT, we are trying to shrink the respiratory proteins in mitochondria to make them more efficient and make more ATP. Right?

It looks to me like sitting motionless in a 50 deg. F. tub of water for up to an hour would be much better than a few seconds of swimming in 35 deg. F water.

Comments? Clarifications? Suggestions?

### shah78 — 2017-02-14T10:39:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/post-208558>

Fine analysis of your Ukraine experience. NOW FIGURE OUT WHY YOU'VE WASTED YOUR TIME IN CYPRUS.  Why no "progress"?

### brent-next — 2017-02-14T18:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/post-208574>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=208558)
>
> NOW FIGURE OUT WHY YOU'VE WASTED YOUR TIME IN CYPRUS.  Why no "progress"?

It's difficult to weigh factors against each other without knowing if they are big effects or small effects. Sunshine is clearly a big effect. Was 35 degrees north far enough south to go? Perhaps not. Here in Cyprus, I have gotten enough sun to keep from getting worse, but not enough to get better.

On the other hand, I know that I do very poorly in hot climates, which is part of why I didn't go to a Red Sea resort in Egypt.

How much does living in a typical apartment building (which I have done here in Cyprus) hurt your mitochondria from too much wi-fi? I don't know.

Winter swimming seems to be something that will make you feel good for an hour, but not do any significant healing. Doing CT for an hour in your tub might help a lot, who knows.

My guess is that I have lousy mitochondria. I did 22 minutes of CT yesterday and seven minutes today. CT may help, but it will take time to show any effects.

I have methylene blue in my bag, perhaps thats the next thing I should try.

### brent-next — 2017-04-05T11:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/post-211795>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=208558)
>
> Fine analysis of your Ukraine experience. NOW FIGURE OUT WHY YOU'VE WASTED YOUR TIME IN CYPRUS.  Why no "progress"?

Hi Shaw78,

This is an insightful and helpful question. Thank you for asking it. Part of my answer is the parasite ascaris lumbricodes, which I write about in my optimal journal: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/brents-optimal-journal.19063/)

I tested positive for this naughty little worm again a couple of weeks ago after testing positive last year and then taking the prescribed medication and then re-testing negative. Doctors in parasite rich parts of the world (like Cyprus or Ukraine) say that a positive test for this worm means you have it, but a negative doesn't mean that you don't have it. Multiple re-tests from multiple different labs are recommended to establish a higher likelihood that you really don't have it. One worm is all that it takes to seriously mess you up and if you have one male worm, you will NEVER see eggs in your stool.

I did this re-test on the advice I had gotten from a sleep Dr. last October, but hadn't had a chance to poop and take it to a lab until now. Big mistake.

Two recent tests for my BUN / Creatine ratio were 15 and a few days later 35. Obviously I need more water and less microwaves. My vitamin D was low in spite of getting enough sun to leave me quite tanned. My Sun Friend UV meter watch was showing a 6 to 8 much of the time, my skin is tanning and I was taking about 2,000 IU of vitamin D per day. Even with all this my 25 OH Vitamin D levels were in the bottom 1/3 of this labs range. My current assumption is that my skin doesn't make a significant amount of vitamin D in my current environment.

The parasite ascaris lumbricodes (the worlds most common round worm parasite) is clearly a big effect.

Just keep evolving... Was I supposed to say swimming????

The first place I used CT was a 30 degree day with a river about 2 miles from my house. The water was so cold it shocked my whole body into a numbness, that within 10 minutes, was gone and replaced with boundless energy and a sense of well being. 30 minutes later I'm doing as many push ups as I humanly possible could at the time, maybe 50 straight? I was so cold I thought I was dying before I got home, that was the day I truly lived... Just keep evolving bud, you know what feels right, is the light.... Anytime you feel good remember light is sending that message...........

We aren't trying to make ATP in the cold. We are holding onto your life source and attempting to accelerate it. The more accelerated your life force = the more you can catch the power of light. I suggest you do not swim however and just stay still in a pose. I couldn't swim until weeks after adaption. Now Tahoe swims are nothing.... I love cold lakes

### brent-next — 2017-04-08T16:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/post-212019>

> [CTforlife said:](/goto/post?id=211825)
>
> I suggest you do not swim however and just stay still in a pose.

I think that's good advice. For the last almost two months I have been living in a remote beach resort in northern Cyprus. I had hoped to be in the cold sea for a couple of hours per day. Unfortunately many days including today have been far too windy or had waves too big for me to be in the sea.

I think I would have been a lot better off to get a stock tank set up so I could reliably get in it, rather than waiting for calm enough sea conditions.

I've ended up being in the 62 deg. F sea about three times per week for about half an hour each time. Unfortunately that's not cold enough water and not enough time in it to make any noticeable progress.

> JK mentioned using a Protein shake with raw eggs for breakfast not long ago.
>
> We would use a protein shake in addition to real food of course.
>
> Just trying to optimise everything ......
>
> Any thoughts anyone????

Ben Greenfield uses it with I think glutamine after working out (working out at that level generates a lot of inflammation) - also, the alternative health community uses it to "retrain" the gut - like your mother's gut flora did at birth - basically, their motto is bug bomb, rebalance the gut flora, and retrain the gut flora so it can recognize a pathogen - if you get crappy flora at birth, you are unable to recognize a pathogen because pathogens are the norm... for your friend, I would skip the colostrum and concentrate on collagen, electrons, red light and cold - have him drink bone broths with lime /lemon juice (vit c=electrons)(bone broths improve collagen which improves electron flow) and since it's summer there, if you're by the beach, have him jump into the ocean and just hang out - preferably in the morning - epsom salts with IR therapy might be a great move also - try to make him as least inflamed as possible - I think colostrum also has beta glucans - which I think mushrooms also have - for immunity improvements - not really your friend's problem -

This reminds me my life on my parents farm, 1945-1953.
5 yo till 13yo

mixed with little sugar,
covered with warm milk, straight from the cow (ok from a tit).
That was my before-breakfast.

I must be doing something right, _optimally speaking_, because people at work are dropping like flies. Shingles, bronchitis, strep and flu. On the other hand, it leaves me as the sole survivor in my area today so I will be very busy. I'll gladly take this manifestation!

Slimmer is a state of mind . I may or may not see a size 8 again but this post-menopausal woman is happy with herself at this time.

Finding myself thankful for the all of people in my life and stability of my job.

I'm at a crossroads after losing Mum last month ..... I still feel completely peaceful about that, but it makes it difficult to judge whether changes (positive and negative) that are happening are as a result of having that situation change, or of what I'm trying out. Probably a bit of both, but it makes judging mitohacks difficult. I have pressed the pause button and am treading water until another pathway reveals itself .... I could retire or go back to the business life I had before I drastically cut my hours down as Mum's condition worsened, but I'm pausing and leaving myself open to a few quantum possibilities...

Things that are driving me nuts? Getting drawn into something that interests me but I can't connect the dots ....(currently Niels Finsen's red light therapy for smallpox . What's the mechanism for daylight cause scarring.....) I don't like to think about how much time I've spent trying to work stuff out, but its giving me something to do while I wait for the quantum possibilities.

No dancing naked, but sphinx moon bathing anyone?

### Inger — 2017-02-20T13:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-208930>

I am dancing naked....
I am doing great! It is the toughest months of winter here but I have not yet been sick... lets see  at work they have the flu now!

I have dry brushed my body almost every morning/day now for many weeks, in front of my reptile light and infrared lamp.
I have to say, it is awesome!!! Very energizing, and my skin sure have become even softer and nicer! It is doing some real good things I feel   so glad I started doing it  I can only recommend it for everyone to try
I have two brushes now and use them both, one in each hand.

Partner and I are reading Osho when I am at his place or he is at mine.... or he reads for me in candle light and I listen
It is a great stuff!
Anyways, whatever happens, I feel that life is just magic and a beautiful gift. Soon spring will arrive

Started to drink seaweed water again as my body somehow craves it now. I have not been eating much seaweed at all lately. I forgot! I think I need iodine! The seaweed water just tastes so awesome to me.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-208977>

Little things from here. I decided today that I am well enough to get into exercisisng Movenat or variation on teh theme. Enjoyed it so much I did 90 minutes.

I also got quite cocky this summer ,which was easy when you have a blister from sandals and walked barefoot in the city centre, shoes dangling from my arm. I passed hundreds of people. I repeated the exercise several days running and out of teh hundreds maybe 10 noticed... Smombies abound..

I am having fun planning a biohack in Patagonia. I found a place that has no cell reception, no electricity and 25 miles from twon. It is in latitude 40 though so I need to climb quite high to get UV . Ct can be done in the lake in front of the cabin. Still looking for ways to get tests before and after. The doc I thought was onboard really was not, so still looking for someone to prescribe a very unusual (for here) combination of tests.

I made really great friends this Xmas and New Year . lots of fun and enlightening conversation. "2017 started as 2016 finished - on a high C .

Oooh ,edit ,edit I do dance . Every morning on my patio. Clothes are dependent on mood.
Sexier ,hmm I feel sexier. ..

The skin is a huge organ that helps detox stuff. So I bet a increased redox and ability to detox is part of the well-being feeling too

### caroline — 2017-02-23T00:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-209080>

Can you get canned sardines? wild caught salmon? cans of mackerel? frozen seafood is a really good alternative - it is flash frozen on fishing trawler so it isn't hanging around too long in someone's display counter.

JK mentioned, on a recent podcast, that lamb is a reasonable source of DHA

What hobbies are you doing? I love how you say you are returning to "creator" instead of just being a consumer.

What outdoor stuff are you doing? Sorry ....I can't remember where you are located?

Remember ....Jack says it is not about food.

### Just Breathe — 2017-02-23T22:51:41+0200

### Samy Colddigger — 2017-03-07T06:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-209809>

I sure as hell am looking forward to a nice vacation in Yucatan! It has been a mighty cold and dark winter! It wouldn't even be that bad, would it not be for my current job where they have Wifi Boosters and blue lights everywhere. Have been getting outside for sunbathing whenever it is Sunny, even in -20 C weather. I lay in the forest with my huge down Parka and the black interior lining soaks up the sun and keeps me warm enough to stay out for hours. But those sunny days that I get off are few and far between. So it means I have been really grateful for my good sleep set.....maybe as good as it gets for living on the grid without being able to shut the breaker off due to the pipes freezing! I have a 10 G magnetico and we sleep in a dark, cold basement with very low RF readings. Still AC magnetic fields though..... but Having lived in Vancouver last year, getting maybe a little more light but way more EMF, I can safely say I have made progress on this front. However, I have always loved the sun and I am giving all my thoughts these days to living in the place where I can get sun and grow my own food. I am no longer so down with the idea of living and working in a dark/blue light/emfed frozen hell hole buying food that comes from 1000s of kilometers away, starting a family so my kids can go to school and get their mito trashed!. We can get frozen seafood which is good....but I need to make the step up on the LIght Water and Magnetism front! Esp after finding out my Haplotype of k1 (ashkanazi, kurds, etc)..... think I am definatley not yoked for north of 60 degrees! So hopefully going to figure something out where I can work somewhere in the sun a build a life there. Any thoughts? I would be down for Mexico, I have been there before and always feel awesome there. Best I have ever felt really....maybe save for a few hot Summers in the Yukon. It boggles my mind to think how long I have lived with low charge. So this is something I would really like to bio hack......getting my Vit D into optimal range. Just getting charged up for a couple months, not a couple weeks. So thinking of heading back Yucatan in the summer after our little spring break visit.

JUST gotta hack the north until then! Thanks for the tips on the scrub brush.....I do the heat lamp and cold showers but never though about the brush. Another Hack that is awesome is just go for a run outside (very cod weather) with long johns and just the compression shirt on top. Add in some running on snow with just socks on for awesome flow!

Was wondering about the Paternal influence on energy status....or is there much influence at all? I have the k1 mito (I knew I always loved Spain and Portugal) but my paternal haplogroup is Scandinavian.....so im pretty much the worst thing to happen for the those Nordic supremacists.......funny how I studied this issue a lot in history of science and always detested the idea......and growing up with Yukon Natives I always knew that fitness was very much about the environmental influences!

With my life being forever effected by the death of my sister(died at age for Feb 29th on a leap year, 1993), this time of year has always been hard. But I see it in a new light now thanks to Jack. My sister Tesse died from "something" called hemi-encephalepathy. I recently found out my mom used a microwave throughout the pregnancy.!! A rare disease that they wanted her brain for....looking for all the wrong things!?. But I know now that it was simply heteroplasmy. Both me and my brother are perfectly healthy......Tesse was the youngest and experienced different conditions it seems.

Thanks Jack for shedding light on some of the toughest questions I have ever asked.

Sam

### johnsonmd — 2017-04-15T14:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-is-everyone-doing.19089/post-212346>

I have been following more and more of Jacks protocol for about 2 years. I got a magnetico about a year ago. I notice this week, that my fingernails are getting really strong and I am feeling better and sleeping better!
Also, in August, I fell off a ladder and broke my tailbone. I remembered the story about the football player who broke his neck and was back on the field in 3 weeks. I decided to do CT in the horse water trough with ice for 40 minutes each morning and then lay out in the sun for 40 minutes in my bikini. 10 days later, my Dr said it didn't look broken! He wanted to know what did I do? I showed him Jacks book.
I eat lots of seafood and veggies and drink my spring water from the local Indian reservation. I pray and meditate... and sing and dance! I watch for the sun and go outside and get sunshine whenever possible!

I live about 200 miles above San Francisco at 1200 ft. I am outside everyday from 1-6 hours sometimes working in the garden planting or weeding. if the sun is out Im in shorts and maybe no shirt even at 50\*. I often do bates method eye exercises sunning and palming outside while grounded. my home is on avg 50\*. I turn off all power in my apt while I sleep.
I use blueblockers at night when in the community kitchen( I live in community)or if on the computer with red LEDs for light. I often wear blue blockers when shopping day or night.
I think the tests look pretty good.
had labs done 2/2/2017

### JanSz — 2017-02-17T17:46:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/markus56-journal.19091/post-208776>

HEMOGLOBIN 14.4 1.2-17.1 g/dL 15 0r 15.5 would be nicer, eat liver
VITAMIN D,25-OH,TOTAL,IA 36 I think that is low
T3, FREE 2.8 2.3-4.2 pg/mL UL looks low, and yout TSH is asking for more
IGF 1, LC/MS 158 50-317 ng/mL that is to low but really important
TESTOSTERONE, TOTAL,
LC/MS/MS 804 250-1100 ng/dL------------------------this part is good
FREE TESTOSTERONE 57.7 35.0-155.0 pg/mL -----------this is not, likely due to high SHGB

//
Many items above can be improved by good vit D and good circadian rhythm
Try to get this numbers:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
/
Get the shirt off and all else also off, when you can get off Speedos.
Best sun plus mins 2hrs around soler noon. Maximize your outside time around noon.

Red Sperti lamp may help, but nothing is better than natural sun.

/
DHEA SULFATE 223
this could be higher too, but not sure, because you have real nice TTestosterone.

Start with vit D and Circadian Rhythm.
That also means that you should minimize your time with your eye glasses.

//

### Markus56 — 2017-02-17T22:44:41+0200

### JanSz — 2017-02-17T23:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/markus56-journal.19091/post-208798>

SHBG - sex hormone binding globulin
Protein that absorbs some steroid hormones.
It does that for storage and transport.
Hormone that is attached to binding protein is not-active.
You likely have some excess of SHBG.
I am guessing that because you have very good Total Testosterone but free testosterone is lowish.
Most of reasons why SHBG is too big is not known (to me).
Most popularly discussed,
SHBG may raise when one have excess of estrogens.

The way you describe your daily activities, you spend lots of time on the sun with lots of skin exposed.
Make sure that you do not use sunscreens, eat sulfur containing food (for vit D), and carotene containing food (for vit A).
Sunscreen may be one reason why your vit D looks low.
There are other reasons, that I do not really understand, but dr @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned their existence.

You have a lots of "good" stuff.
HDL CHOLESTEROL 138
Someone who knows what to look for could likely figure something out of it.

You have done those labs as routine health maintenance or something feels not right?

//

# Thread: Classroom Ideas

**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John G — 2017-02-17T21:01:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/classroom-ideas.19092/post-208785>

Hi all,

I'm an educator of young adults, and I want to change the lighting environment of my classroom. The room is 9.5ft long x 9ft wide x 7ft high. The room has 8 squares on the ceiling that each have 3 long fluorescent tubes.

I'm thinking of getting a whole bunch of lamps that look something like this (maybe shorter ones too):
[https://www.walmart.com/ip/Mainstay...ll-Torchiere-Floor-Lamp-Black-Finish/54906105](https://www.walmart.com/ip/Mainstays-MS41-020-001-01-Mainstays-71-Scroll-Torchiere-Floor-Lamp-Black-Finish/54906105).

I'm thinking of plugging in something like this (might get several different types to test various lumens):
[https://www.bulbamerica.com/products/philips-exn-mr16-50w-12v-fl36-bulb-no-fg-1](https://www.bulbamerica.com/products/philips-exn-mr16-50w-12v-fl36-bulb-no-fg-1)

Would this be an effective way to light up a classroom while maintaining the guidelines set by this post? ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...or-you-in-purchasing-artificial-lights.17241/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-goal-for-you-in-purchasing-artificial-lights.17241/))

Do you think there might be a better way? The one issue I foresee is too many chords from the lamps tripping students....or not having enough lamps to be bright.

Thanks,

John G.

### fitness@home — 2017-02-17T21:08:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/classroom-ideas.19092/post-208786>

I take it this means you don't have enough natural light through windows?

What about replacing the fluorescents with something else? Not sure if these are any better but take a look.

[http://www.naturallighting.com/cart/store.php](http://www.naturallighting.com/cart/store.php)

### John G — 2017-02-17T22:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/classroom-ideas.19092/post-208792>

Correct. I do not have enough natural light coming through the windows.

The full-spectrum bulbs are an interesting option.
\-Isn't the 5000k above recommendations? They don't have any 12v?

# Thread: Light as medicine - great article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2017-02-17T21:37:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-208789>

[https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine](https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine)
[https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine](https://wisconsinacademy.org/magazine/light-medicine)
The article references Tilna Karu from the Russian Academy of Laser Sciences and here is a movie called: Mitochondrial Mechanisms of Photobiomodulation:

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-18T17:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-208840>

Interesting article.  It ties in with a book I've been reading, a 1901 translation of Niels Finsen's book phototherapy, which is papers from the 1890s. About using what he called the "chemical rays", (blue, violet and especially UV) for the treatment of lupus vulgaris skin lesions, and the need to avoid those rays for the treatment of smallpox. If the chemical rays were completely excluded and red light used before the smallpox spots became "suppurated" the patients recovered without scars. Often if caught early enough the suppuration didn't occur and "It is noted that during six to eight weeks the skin remains covered with hyperaemic or pigmented spots; at the end of this time, however, they finally disappear." I've been driving myself nuts trying to work out the mechanism, but also wondering, apart from the fillers, what does a smallpox vaccine do the melanin system and the ability to do the human equivalent of photosynthesis?

### Sue-UK — 2022-11-08T18:18:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-as-medicine-great-article.19093/post-317401>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=208840)
>
> Interesting article.  It ties in with a book I've been reading, a 1901 translation of Niels Finsen's book phototherapy, which is papers from the 1890s. About using what he called the "chemical rays", (blue, violet and especially UV) for the treatment of lupus vulgaris skin lesions, and the need to avoid those rays for the treatment of smallpox. If the chemical rays were completely excluded and red light used before the smallpox spots became "suppurated" the patients recovered without scars. Often if caught early enough the suppuration didn't occur and "It is noted that during six to eight weeks the skin remains covered with hyperaemic or pigmented spots; at the end of this time, however, they finally disappear." I've been driving myself nuts trying to work out the mechanism, but also wondering, apart from the fillers, what does a smallpox vaccine do the melanin system and the ability to do the human equivalent of photosynthesis?

A possible clue comes from this paper [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3180186/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3180186/)
**Hydrogen peroxide fuels aging, inflammation, cancer metabolism and metastasis

"In normal wound healing, the production of hydrogen peroxide is shut off."

"Similarly, during scar formation, keloid fibroblasts produce hydrogen peroxide, undergo aerobic glycolysis and secrete lactate. Thus, myofibroblasts also have the capacity to undergo the Warburg Effect (i.e., aerobic glycolysis), a process that was previously thought to be confined to cancer cells."

"**

# Thread: Radioactivity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-18T16:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radioactivity.19095/post-208826>

The ceramic is likely made with radioactive elements from China. Ordinary ceramics often contain elevated levels of naturally occurring radionuclides, e.g., K-40 and the various members of the uranium 238/235 series and thorium decay series. The use of uranium in ceramic glazes ceased during World War II and didn’t resume until 1959.In 1987, NCRP Report 95 indicated that no manufacturers were using uranium-glaze in dinnerware.
Ceramics can be particularly radioactive if some compound of uranium (e.g., uranium oxide, sodium urinate) has been used to impart color (e.g., orange-red, green, yellow, black) to the glaze. It is widely known that uranium was used in the glaze of orange-red Fiesta dinnerware, but uranium glazes have also been used other types of ceramics: wall and floor tiles, pottery, laboratory ceramics, etc. The glaze can serve two functions: it provides color, and it seals the ceramic.
Although the uranium in the glaze emits gamma rays, alpha particles, and beta particles, the gamma and alpha emissions are weak. The beta particles are the easiest to detect, and they are also responsible for the bulk of the radiation exposure to those handling ceramics that employ a uranium glaze.

NCRP Report 95 reported the following measurements for dinnerware employing uranium glazes: 0.2 to 20 mrad per hour on contact as measured using film badges like we use in neurosurgery for Xray exposure.

Measurements of the radioactivity of ceramics that are reported in mR/hr are difficult to interpret unless it is known whether or not the detector was responding to beta particles. The exposure rate, in mR/hr, is defined for x-rays or gamma rays. It is not defined for betas. If a measurement is reported in mR/hr, the detector should not be responding to beta particles. CITES:Harry McMaster.Earthenware Dishes and Glaze Therefor. U.S. Patent No. 1,890,297, December 6, 1932 .

# Thread: Lab tests to be done
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-tests-to-be-done.19097/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ayman — 2017-02-18T17:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-tests-to-be-done.19097/post-208837>

hello hope everyone is well, below are the tests mentioned in the Epi-paleo RX:

1\. VO2 Max assessment
2\. DEXA body composition scan
3\. Comprehensive blood chemistry panel (Chem-20)
4\. Liver functioning test
5\. Serum Iron, TIBC, Sesum Ferritin and Iron % Saturation
6\. Lipid Panel VAP
7\. Lipid Panel NMR
8\. Homocysteine
9\. Complete CBC with differential and platelet count
10\. Gender specific hormone panels, serum and salivary - (cortisol, total testosterone, free testosterone, Dihydrocortisone (DHT), Luteinizing Hormone (LH), follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), estradiol (E2), Dehydroepiandrosterone sulphate (DHEA-S), prostate-specific antigen (PSA), insulin-like growth factor (IGF-1), serum leptin level.
11\. Complete thyroid Panel with antibody screening (TPO Ab and Tg Ab)
12\. Highly Sensitive CRP (HS-CRP) -not a regular CRP
13\. Omega 6:3 Serum test (for severe findings consider tissue assay)
14\. Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
15\. Complete vitamin D3 Panel

Did I miss anything? I'm planning on going to the lab as soon as I get feedback from this forum. Thank you!

skiing - the sunlight reflecting from the snow causes the Albedo effect
Bright snow and ice reflect sunlight back into space; the albedo of snow and ice is between .6 to .9. But what happens when Earth's temperature warms? Snow and ice melt, less energy is reflected and more is absorbed by the ground and water, warming the Earth and causing more snow and ice to melt.

in Germany - your body is adapted to assimilate it if you allow it to happen - if you put undergarments/snow gear on, you lose the opportunity for UVB assimilation - sun block, no - not a fan of pills - like fish oil pills - quantum biology is about details - you have to understand within the eye and the brain, DHA gets put into it in a very specific way and it's called the SN-2 position - very simply - all fats in the body have 3 carbons (holds up arm) that's the glycerol (fat) backbone - SN-2 means the middle one - so for DHA to work optimally in the brain to get that pi electron cloud, DHA has to be in the SN-2 position - # Thread: Hello from Boulder, Colorado. How is my understanding of why sunlight is important?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-boulder-colorado-how-is-my-understanding-of-why-sunlight-is-important.19100/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mlamz — 2017-02-19T04:43:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-boulder-colorado-how-is-my-understanding-of-why-sunlight-is-important.19100/post-208881>

Hello!

I moved to Boulder, Colorado from the UK four and a half years ago because I realized that the lack of sunlight in the UK was detrimental to my health. I realized this because for seven years, I suffered from cholinergic urticaria, and the only thing that made it better was UVB phototherapy and when I went skiing in France and got a tan. _My health is now fantastic because I have made exposure to sunlight the number one priority in my life._

I have now recently discovered the work of Jack, Gerald Pollock, Stephanie Seneff, Nick Lane and Travis Christofferson. It has been thoroughly eye-opening. From their work, I have attempted to put together a paragraph summarizing the importance of sunlight, and would like to know if I am on the right track.

The paragraph is this:

\--------------------
Sunlight is comprised of infrared, visible and ultraviolet wavelengths of light. The near infrared wavelengths in sunlight penetrate the skin and structure the water within our cells and mitochondria into an “exclusion zone”. This exclusion zone in our cells and mitochondria causes a net negative charge, similar to how a battery functions. The ultraviolet wavelengths in sunlight then acts to supercharge this exclusion zone. It does this through the ability of the skin to create cholesterol sulfate from ultraviolet light. Cholesterol sulfate then attaches to the capillary walls and causes the size of the exclusion zone of our cells to increase. The net negative charge within our cells and mitochondria causes the electron transport chain within our mitochondria to become more efficient. A more efficient electron transport chain means better control of oxidative stress, which is the root cause of aging, chronic disease, and cancer, if we are to believe the oxidation theory of aging and the metabolic theory of cancer.
\--------------------

How does this sound?

Hi,

I listened to Jack's podcast in which he was interviewed by Luke Story today. I was searching for information on long term water storage. He mentions storing water underground in the dark and how to bring life back into the water. I highly recommend this podcast.

I wanted to ask if anyone knows if water should be treated for long term storage. I know that if you buy the PVC buckets, they recommend treating the water with chlorine. I thought it would be better to clean the buckets with ozonated water, and then store it. I am not sure for huge amounts if glass would be possible. I am thinking of storing water for my mom in Florida before the hurricane season and here at my resort in Phuket in case of emergency. Again, most websites are recommending chlorine treatment I just think all the masterminds on this website would have innovative, great solutions.

Thanks

Mel
