**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209066>

So, here is what I've found thus far: obviously being deep in nature is the most optimal. But for those of us who can't be during the work week, grounding in an area with .1 or .2 mg is probably safe. The first problem here is that most of us likely don't have access to a meter that is precise enough. (i.e: Cornett is only sensitive down to about .5 mg).

So, my plan is to rent a meter from Michael and determine if my backyard is more than .1 or .2 mg. If it is, then it is no bueno and I will need use Michael's meter to determine if there is a local spot that is suitable .

### Alper — 2017-02-24T16:57:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209232>

Just thought about grounding in the winter (in high latitude without catching a cold, which I caught last time I tried to ground at 4°C, wet underground ): Huging a tree should have the same effect as grounding with your feet, shouldn´t it?

### JoeT — 2017-02-26T00:12:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209288>

yes that works, however, probably not as well because the balls of your feet are the optimal contact point. But it's surely better than not grounding at all.

### JoeT — 2017-02-27T22:02:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209403>

Another great article on potential hazards while grounding, with a "geekish" paper attached.

In addition, I see that gold member "Health2015" posted in the "ask jack" section (which I don't have access to) about feeling "wired" after grounding. Grounding should have the precise opposite effect. There are some serious red flags there, and may indicate her grounding environment is leading to absorption of more nnEMF than native electrons.

[http://www.electricsense.com/7130/grounding-health-emf-protection/](http://www.electricsense.com/7130/grounding-health-emf-protection/)

### JoeT — 2017-02-28T00:26:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-article-that-got-me-thinking.19107/post-209410>

And one more thing just to finish this Rant.....I'm kind of pissed at Mercola for putting his name on grounding sheets. Grounding is obviously more complex than meets the eye, and depends heavily on context and environment. It appears he did not do is due diligence, and his credibility is damaged in my eyes.

### JanSz — 2017-08-03T20:56:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-are-a-cheap-solid-food-that-has-dha-too.19108/post-220856>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=208974)
>
> We would love to buy eggs from chickens that roam around freely but we hear so much crap about eggs not being cage free at all - even tho we pay so much more for them.
>
> We have a new butcher in our village - he is young and eager. I asked about organic chickens - he has actually gone to look. Thousands of chickens are packed in to their cage and there is a very small doorway for them to get out - but they are so packed in they don't move.

Organic or not
ask him where the chicken feed was stored before.
If it went thru being stored in silo it is deprived of butyric acid (C4:0)

Our food of animal origins most of the time (or 100% of the time) comes thru feeding the animals a food that goes thru storage in silo.

Human population see decreased gut flora.
Our gut flora feeds on butyric acid.
Butyric acid messes up stuff stored in silo.
Silo are managed by minimizing butyric acid.

///
That is why even natural food tends to last long (without preservatives), thanks to the absence of butyric acid.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-are-a-cheap-solid-food-that-has-dha-too.19108/post-269602>

Egg sandwich r

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=209293)
>
> Does anyone know if quail and duck eggs are less reactive than chicken eggs? My allergens go nuts over egg white and egg yolk.... Duck eggs seem to be more keto friendly but aside from heart rate testing prepost any other ideas?

I personally really like dove/pigeon eggs. You need about three for every one normal size chicken egg. If you keep chickens you cant go away for a holiday or something without somebody looking after them, but pigeons/doves look after themselve

The French go crazy over dove eggs, and pigeon meat is £150 for a meal at posh French dinners. I can do one of my specialties is a French style dove delicacy

I prefer to keep pigeons than chickens because they look after themselves.

A pole dovecote is an excellent idea, you can lower it every day to take the eggs out and maybe a squab or two, (baby dove) then raise I back up to keep hem safe from predators.

They feed themselves with grubs and worms Ect ect. And you can take a lot of eggs from just a few families of pigeons.

I have one pole dove vote but would like a few more. Also homing pigeons is a great hobby you can take one with you and send a message home on its leg, the kids love it it's so exciting

### Apteryx — 2022-03-02T01:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eggs-are-a-cheap-solid-food-that-has-dha-too.19108/post-310663>

Just my humble suggestion: prior to entertaining eggs as too primary of a source of certain minerals (specifically metal cations) ... may be worthwhile to give some deep consideration to the matter of the exact subtleties of egg composition

Not the least of reasons, phosvitin. Powerful chelator.

You can do a {Control + F} search to catch all mentions. The matter will quickly gain razor sharp clarity

[https://www.mdpi.com/books/pdfdownload/book/502](https://www.mdpi.com/books/pdfdownload/book/502)

Egg Consumption and Human Health Special Issue, ed. Maria Luz Fernandez, University of Connecticut USA / Editorial Office MDPI AG St. Alban-Anlage 66 Basel, Switzerland

Some interesting things arise: _"it acts as an important melanogenesis inhibitor to control excessive melanin synthesis in the melanocytes of animal and human skin \[21\]. It was suggested that egg-yolk phosvitin has the potential to be used as a natural bioactive compound as a hyper-pigmentation inhibitor for human skin \[...\]"_

Hope this piques your curiosity, if you are in a position for this to be of relevance to you (i. e. entertaining reliance on eggs as a significant nutritional source)

Apteryx.

### Debntx — 2017-02-20T19:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arcus-senilis.19109/post-208964>

is the condition related to light- either too little, the wrong type etc... - I did a search and nothing came up- unless I misspelled it.

The usual information indicates harmless- but now I wonder...

Has anyone even heard of this?

I have not been diagnosed with this but I have a definite line starting to circle around my iris - perhaps I have something else- and it's actually a cholesterol ring?

Thanks to anyone who has any insights?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-21T04:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arcus-senilis.19109/post-208988>

I think they are do to low NAD+ and pseudohypoxia in the eye and this leads to the healing response and up-regulation of cholesterol deposition at this border zone. To me is it a surface sign of stress of some sort to the eye. It is most commonly seen in those at highest risk.........children and the elderly

### Debntx — 2017-02-25T18:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arcus-senilis.19109/post-209277>

not sure when i started getting this - but i wonder if wearing contacts were a contributing factor - the contacts certainly hid from my view the fact i even had them - i stopped wearing contacts a couple of years ago to improve my vision - after stopping their use - my eyes hurt tremendously - stung - it was not good but i persisted because i believed at that time that my eyesight was going to return to normal just by doing that  - little did i realize what else i was missing - i will have to read on pseudohypoxia etc... and hopefully will be able to glean something practical to apply - as well as working on my light, water, magnetism

### Debntx — 2017-03-13T00:01:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/arcus-senilis.19109/post-210215>

my eyesight has actually improved by removing my glasses and contacts and my 'night blindness" is just a memory now - just saying

### caroline — 2017-03-13T05:06:58+0200

### Debntx — 2017-03-13T15:58:55+0200

i started it.... didn't finish it - but, if it addresses this condition by name I will definitely go back to read - i understand, especially now the importance of getting the sun's ray - full spectrum - all that and am trying to apply what i have relearned since i had buried the sun without realizing it - i am not sure that it will lead me to a cure - but anything that cements the importance of the sun in my mind has to be of value - making me more determined to add it back to my daily life as I can and will do.

# Thread: Biohacking Shingles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-shingles.19115/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benedick Howard — 2017-02-21T05:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-shingles.19115/post-208991>

About 14 months ago I went to the doctor for a resent outbreak of a mostly dormant long standing issue with asthma. I was put on Symbicort 80 2x daily.
I have been doing Ashtanga yoga for decades and was prepping to shift some of that time into running.
Last year I got back into running for the first time in decades 3x weekly average. I took it slow.
After 14 months I was up to 8 miles on Saturdays and 2 -3 mile runs during the week.
I slowly increased my speed averaging 12 minute miles and high 9s best.

Nothing hurt and I could breath again!
But during the year my left eye vision got blurrier. Some days noticeable worse than others. I thought that it was due to a 2 year old percussive injury when an overstretched bungi cord unleashed sending it directly to the open eyeball. It knocked me out for a moment and I collapsed to the ground in pain.
Medical examination showed no damage.

So I got through last year's running, felt so relieved to be running again and then in December (a few months after coming across Dr Jack's work) I started taking DHA. I had been doing coconut oils for years because of it feeding the cell membranes, but I was unaware that DHA formed the mitochondria membrane. I have been eating really healthy, living off grid, very small nnEMF, great sunlight and clean air. Hey its Hawaii!

I began eating more seafood.

I noticed energy surges, brain clarity surges and felt stronger, although my run times were worsening, I just did not have the energy.

THEN BAM.

Three weeks ago in Parsvakonasana a standing side twist I reached fuller in to the pose than usual. I did not think about it until that night the pain began in between my ribs and over the following days exploded into shingles with twitching muscles and bolts of pain shooting up the ribs towards the spine.

But the shingles was not yet diagnosed. So I went back to my usual routines of MMS (sodium chlorite mixed with citric acid) and microcurrent stimulation.

Gassing the chlorine directly to the tissues was astounding how quickly the pain left. But each day it spread more and the pain just got worse. Using up to 6 caps 500 gm extra strength tylenol.
Taking the MMS internally 10 drops every hour or two helped a lot.

If I had known more about shingles pain earlier I would have done more MMS application, I was not adapting fast enough to the situation. I think it was containing, but the pain was so much I was having awful troubles just getting out of bed, sleeping etc.

About day 4 (they are all a blurr) I went to the doctors thinking I was low on potassium. They diagnosed me with Shingles. I could not believe it. After a second opinion and day later I got going with 1000mg Valacylovir 3 x daily. I have been taking potassium chloride about 500mg daily, B12, B complex, l-lysine, hours and hours of sunlight, homemade fermented veges, bananas, salads, chicken, ahi, potatoes, greens, kambosha, eggs, lots of butter, goats cheese, coconut and good virgin olive oil, no sugars, some carbs, no coffee.

At day 4 with the anti viral. The swelling has gone down immensely, very miminal seepage. I drained all the puss sacks 2 days ago. The doctor also prescribed 10 mg 3x Prednisone. I took it once that first night. No pain. BUT migraines, dryness and tortured dreams and such, as the research I could do on the internet (WIFI hurts the fascia around the ribs).

After I started on the Valacyclovair I stopped taking MMS internally, just gassing the sores with MMS. By then I was in a ritual of dressing the sores, gassing, some minimal exercise and LOTS of the early margining sunlight.

I took "Clear" magnesium citrate (day3) and it made relaxation way faster and easier to get comfortable in bed or hammock during the day.

The tissue under and around the sores are very sensitive. Wind sensitive, people energy sensitive, cold sensitive and WIFI sensitive. I don't have wifi on unless I am connected to the internet to read Dr Jack's latest posts!

At the end of the Valacyclovir the sores had stopped oozing. Never needed the inhaler with the antiviral.

But today, 3 days later, one or two spots in the sores started to minimally ooze, so I went back to the doctor for another course in Valacyclovir 1gm 3x daily for 7 days, 50 mg tremadol hcl for backup when the tylenol does not work.

I am using the phone (when I use it) on a selfy stick for maybe 10 minute sessions and mostly I leave it in airplane mode and connect when the phone is away from my body.

Its definitely not an option to stay in Kalispell, MT and any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Im concerned about:

-   High humidity- have re-occuring sinus issues and several small thyroid nodules. Grew up in Des Moines, IA and 2 yrs n MT. For the 1st time in my life, I can finally breath deeply, due to:
    -   Eliminated diary and gluten.

    -   Icing entire neck b4 bed

    -   Switching to ice cold CT in my nose with a grossman water-pick, eliminated the smell of infection. IR sauna. magnetico
    -   Seafood is fairly priced from Alaska and often is on sale.
-   All my CT is done on the ski mountain (thin long top/ bottom) for 5 hours each weekend (10-38 degrees) & no jacket everywhere. Ice baths had made my husband wonder if Im going crazy and he's upset that I eliminated wifi and havent provided double-blind studies on EMF.. so I try to stay in under the radar.

-   Air-Conditioning, most people dont have it and its the only excuse I have to open the windows at night.

-   No elevation
-   Unknown about what the deep south really holds and what new symptoms will manifest.

My health is ok, but I have 35 lbs to loose and was unsuccessful at Keto for 9 months (eliminated hypoglecimic) and Leptin Reset for 3 mos- bloating finally reduced timed with my sunglo and Nicotine gum. My son is underweight (gaining 5 lbs on Leptin Reset and fat).

Any info is great .. Im feeling a bit overwhelmed at the depth of options for future hacks, I appreciate all the efforts and posts that have been made to help change lives on this forum!

Think fat.
Think insulin.
Certain low insulin level.
When you are above that level (you are likely eating or ate recently)
Whatever energy from that food you have not used it is stored as a body fat.
When your insulin is below that level, you ate some time ago.
If body needs energy it will take it from your body fat.

If you are determined to loose weight, you have two choices.
1\. stop eating, until you have reached your goal weight. Just drink plenty of water.

2.Divide the 24hr in the day into 2 parts.
8hrs during which you may eat
16hrs during which you do not eat
You can drink clean unadulterated water, spring water, any time.

Another opinion I heard ht you get less emf from modern non-smartphones with buttons than old models..sorry. feel confused and I feel i need to addres it asap, along with a terrible recent habbit of reading on the smartphone too much, which does me harm, no matter how useful is th einfo i read.My 7 y old daughter is never on the tablet, and does not know how to use the phone. ### Skylife — 2019-03-06T07:12:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/airplane-mode-is-a-simple-hack.19118/post-264561>

> [Elena Zvon said:](/goto/post?id=254278)
>
> So is it better to have a smartphone but often switched to Airplane mode, with twiglight and blueblockers or and old model like Nokia? should i check for the SAR, or all old phones might be emmiting more EMF than mordern smartphones? I tried to google it at least in my language anf find only information of safer or less safe smartphones. Another opinion I heard ht you get less emf from modern non-smartphones with buttons than old models..sorry. feel confused and I feel i need to addres it asap, along with a terrible recent habbit of reading on the smartphone too much, which does me harm, no matter how useful is th einfo i read.My 7 y old daughter is never on the tablet, and does not know how to use the phone. I screwed my health \_lyme and advanced sarcoma, i can not do it to my daughter..I am so sick that I can not get her to school, so with the help of my mum we do homeschooling that saves her from emf and desire to have a phone.

Elena, its better to have a smartphone in airplane with twilight and blue blockers at night. My cell is pretty much always in airplane now unless i need to make a call or expecting an urgent call. I also have a hack that i do when i am expecting a call by turning off cellular on my iPhone. when you turn cellular off you will still get reception and calls go through but your phone is not constantly emitting radiation pinging cell towers every 15 seconds or so while in standby. This hack is verified by others and me too with 2 meters. when cellular is off you get calls but no texts or emails. I just turn it on several times a day to check them. thats all. The safest use of your cell when in standby. I also used to read tons on iPad and phone and still do but i mostly load an article or whatever and turn it to airplane (check also that bluetooth and wifi are also off). this way you can read on your phone without radiation. hope this helps. Natalia

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molybdenum.19122/post-209060>

I take one teaspoon of MSM twice per day. I am CBS upregulated and BHMT upregulated.... both waste sulfur products up to 10 times the normal rate each... from what I have read.... Is molybdenum needed to use the sulfate in MSM?? I know that molybdenum changes the toxic sulfite to sulfate. I take 500 micrograms of molybdenum a.m. and p.m. I also supplement NAC, creatine, glutamine and glycine, zinc, selenium and phosphytidal serine. I guess I should put this under success stories because now my hair does not fall out by the hundreds!! I have listened and read Dr. Stephanie Seneff and she does not mention taking extra molybdenum with MSM. Any thoughts from you cellular bio majors? I also take some extra copper because molybdenum can decrease copper.

### Sujoe123 — 2017-02-22T19:44:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molybdenum.19122/post-209063>

Even doing all these things with grounding and sunlight... I can only keep my malonylaldehyde test clear of cellular damage for about three-4 hour sections of the day following these supplements with PPQ, CoQ10, VitC, VitE, R-Lipoic and astaxanthin, and DHA, and EPA. Also, sleep on the super Magnetico. Also large total dose of intermittent Folinic, methly folate, and methyl B12 with P5P and R5P and B2 due to other homozygous SNPs in B12 and folic acid pathways. I stay away from added folic acid to breadstuffs grains and cereals because these synthetic folics just take up the receptor sites for the methylated forms that I need. This is so difficult to manage with slow and fast SNPs in fighting each other creating roadblocks in some places and dumping valuable nutrients to waste in the sulfur glutathione pathway, also the positive and negative feedback considerations!!!! My health is considerably better than years ago but this has been a tremendous expensive struggle to learn this much.... especially with the memory damage that all this has caused. Even though I am a nurse practitioner of anesthesia I had to study about 7 times longer than my classmates and my retention is poor. Thank God for multiple choice test questions!! My doctor put me on disability for memory and physical deterioration about 6 years ago and I have read like crazy to understand what happened and why it got so much worse. With the 14-hour days in the operating room my health just crashed!!! Thank you Dr. Jack for helping me understand part of the equation why this happened! EMF HELL HOLE!!! No sunlight or grounding!

# Thread: Tired of Feeling Geriatric
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 87
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chaostheory — 2017-02-22T22:11:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-209072>

Hi everyone. I'm a 24 year old who has dabbled in paleo here and there but only recently has gotten the motivation to really change things.
Most of the issues I want to change are brain-related. I've been diagnosed with depression and anxiety, suspected of having mild aspergers (mainly, I like to worry/overthink), and have a good deal of inefficient, circular thinking. I feel like I'm 80 years old. I don't enjoy anything, basically, except for eating but in that case it's become an addiction. I have sexual dysfunction that keeps getting brushed off as psychosomatic. My memory is kind of faulty (short term especially, but I can also take an extra second or so to remember things in a conversation) and I have constant brain fog. I could go on but will leave it there.
I'm not sure to what extent I will improve since my mind and body have felt crappy as long as I can remember, but I've decided I'm going to try and feel the best I can.

I do see a good integrative doctor, but I decided I needed a community of people who felt the same way I do. I've always had this intuition that if I lived out in nature I wouldn't have developed these problems, and that it could improve me. Right now I'm not functioning well enough to go anywhere and I'm living with my parents, but there's a lot I can do still. I'm still able to work, but it's hard currently.

Any ideas on what I should work on first? I just recently added iodine (as well as drinking non-fluoridated water) to my large collection of supplements and I can swear I'm already thinking better. I'm not sure how to eat, because the brain fog is so bad for me that it's hard to tell how my body reacts to different foods. I want to try Epi-paleo rx, but am scared of the effect it will have on histamine levels in my brain (my doctor thinks that might be an issue for me). Can a green smoothie (no fruit) be allowed on epi-paleo in order to protect against histamine.

The other concern is that according to my doctor, my mitochondrial function was decreasing. He seemed to attribute that to diet, but from what I understand it's more light-related.

I also went to an emotional release therapist who says she works with energy, and have noticed some improvements.

Oh! Really important to add, I have hashimoto's and also systemic antibodies against muscle and connective tissue. So some kind of systemic autoimmune condition. I'm not sure what the best thing to do for this other than what I'm already doing. I've been doing a right brain-left brain brainwave optimization machine to get my sympathetic under control, but without all the basics (like proper social connection) I think it's having a hard time calming down. The light in Michigan is terrible. I have some amber glasses that I hate wearing...would wearing them every night significantly help? Only thing is I'm embarrassed to go out anywhere with them. If I were to only purchase a red bulb for my room at night or a uvb bulb to get some real light in the day time...which would you choose?

I have a chronic sinus infection that's been with me I'm guessing for at least a few years, and I'm going to tackle it naturally (antibiotics will just mess with my brain more)....I'm not sure how though: Chris Kresser has an article about it where he recommended Xlear nasal spray, johnson's baby soap in a neti pot, as well as InterFase Plus...any thoughts? Right now I'm just generally supporting my immune system through diet/supplements. InterFase Plus has some edta in it to break up biofilms plus enzymes, so am holding some hope that that could help me.

Basically, I know primarily I need to change my lifestyle. Going to be early, eating epi paleo, getting sun/circadian rhythm figured out to help with mitochondrial issues, hormones balanced. I'm hoping to find a community of others who believe in getting as close to nature as possible. Is this the place?

I also was looking at the products restore4life and asea, particularly asea because in my case my brain issues continue to get worse and I don't have the mental power to overcome them in my current state (and I read on cancertutor asea can bide time). Something to bide me some time so I don't get totally disabled and can figure this out more efficiently would be nice (although I know it's not what nature would intend).

### caroline — 2017-02-23T00:39:06+0200

### WalterNL — 2017-02-23T10:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-209109>

This is totally the place. Be prepared to change your perspective on a lot of things, and also keep in mind that your personal context might change things up a bit. @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) always says to focus on the good things, instead of trying to combat the bad things. In other words if you do a lot of things right, many of your problems might go away. Human biology is complex, and any of your health issues can have a myriad of causes. For instance nasal spray is a very specific 'treatment' of a specific problem. You might be better of to focus on improving your overall health instead of fighting symptoms.

If you want to get practical, check out every Rx on the blog: [https://www.google.nl/webhp?&ion=1&espv=2&ie=UTF-8#q=site:jackkruse.com+rx](https://www.google.nl/webhp?&ion=1&espv=2&ie=UTF-8#q=site:jackkruse.com+rx)
But it's better to understand why it works because then you'll make better choices intuitively. It's a cumulative process, after many months/years of better choices you end up with a much better life.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-02-23T15:53:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-209119>

From another Michigander, welcome!  Sleep is a time to regenerate and since you currently live in the suburbs, pay attention to just how dark your sleeping quarters are at night. If your room is not pitch black, then you might want to get some blue blocking curtains. Target now sells a nice line of these curtains and they are very effective and reasonably priced too. I hung some in my 83 year old Mother's bedroom for her and she just loves them. It's so important to block out all blue light at night.

Caroline mentioned the face dunks, and those are great. Since you are in Michigan, walking barefoot on the cold ground, and especially if it is wet or there is frost on it, also has a positive effect on the mitochondria respiratory proteins. As does standing or walking in the cold water of a lake. I just did this in lake MI yesterday and the water was 36 degrees. I'm cold adapted now and I wanted to actually go in and do some quick CT, but there was still too much ice to maneuver and strong waves.

Here is my family health history from my Mom:
Paternal Grandmother: Good health at 88. One year ago had open heart surgery for a valve and is doing very well.
Paternal Grandfather: Died at 83. High blood pressure, moderate obesity. Mild dementia in last years after car accident.
Maternal Grandmother: Diet at 67. Colon cancer (her father had colon cancer too).
Maternal Grandfather: Died at 73. Pulmonary fibrosis.
Father: High blood pressure. Recently had a heart procedure to stop AFIB. Successful so far.
Mother: Good health. Some arthritis.
Father's sister (aunt): Died of multiple sclerosis at age 36.
Other father's sister and her daughter (aunt and cousin): Thyroid issues.
Father's paternal aunt: thyroid issues, had surgery.
Father's First Cousins, male: died of multiple sclerosis type disease in his 30s.
Daughter of different Father's First Cousin: in her 30s, has Multiple Sclerosis.
Mothers's First Cousins: Two male first cousins had learning difficulties, dropped out of high school. One was maybe ASD or mental illness as he was a semi-hermit for much of his life.
Maternal Grandmother's First Cousin: Died of thyroid issue in her 30s. Was very anxious.

Everyone else is fine.

### Chaostheory — 2017-03-10T03:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-210002>

So my disease is probably mitochondrial in nature. I have testing that shows it, mito hacks make me feel stronger mentally...but I'm stuck on what to do to get more resilient so I can continue to heal versus fall back into my old habits.

The other day and yesterday I went and looked at the sunrise, and went on a walk. I stood barefoot outside and waded through a local lake. I also spent $5 to be in an infared tanning bed for 30 mins (this felt really good).

Most of my lab tests are fairly normal, but they were done after receiving fecal transplants, which I think may have normalized a lot of things in my body. However, I do still have gut inflammation, adrenal burnout, and mitochondrial issues.

I have a lot of signs of choline deficiency. I'm going to try duck eggs (intolerant to regular eggs) as well as a phosphatidylcholine supplement. Phosphatidylserine from soy has helped a little in the past. I'll also supplement uridine. I've also been taking resistant starch for the butyrate (from potato starch). Before going to a doctor, I was trying some crazy things like nsi-189 which didn't work as well as I'd hoped.

I've been occasionally taking acetyl-l-carnitine and alpha-lipoic-acid (is also in one of my blood sugar supplements).

Some hacks I've been thinking of trying: restore4life to help arrest the gut issue, creatine for overall body energy/strength, and I really need something to help with my food cravings/addiction and I was thinking forskolin might be able to help me with that as well as help with thyroid (feel free to tell me other ways to do this).

The infared made me feel better and more resilient to the cold lake, but is being in a tanning bed healthy? I'm not sure. I'll probably just buy an infared reptile lamp anyway so I don't have to go to the tanning salon except maybe for their uva bed.

One question: the only big things I've changed recently are pqq versus coq10 for the last week (ran out of coq10 & had some pqq lying around), infrared bed for 30 mins one day plus some outside sun last few days, and wading through a local lake the last few days. I'm starting to feel a little more alive, or aware, really. I'm not entirely sure, but I think the lake is what did the most for me. What do you think?

The three things I'm adding into my RX: reconnecting with busy friends I've been avoiding, tai chi or qui gong to move things through my body, and maybe babysitting or some kind of social work so I can feel good helping others. Also I printed out the Change RX and skimmed over that.

Oh, another thing I've been doing based off of what I watched on a youtube video: Primal Play. It's specific physical exercises that are good for your brain, I found it through the terry wahl's website.

Would just a regular infrared reptile lamp from a petstore be good enough as far as infrared goes?

I also have been taking magcalm at night and doing some epsom salt baths, and think I may be low on potassium since i’m getting muscle twitches. How much potassium should i take and what brand? Should I also be supplementing boron? I’m taking 37.5 mg iodine (was low in it since i live in michigan), and some selenium.

My final idea for my brain is to put LLLT on my thyroid and brain to help for myelination purposes. Would that be better than the infrared?

Friends have thought I might be getting mildly paranoid. Is this from too much emf?

I'm thinking I'll try a bit of melatonin before bed tonight to see if that helps, also go to bed earlier.

I'm talking on the phone tomorrow to the doctor with my mom. I'm worried he'll subtly accuse me again of being too dramatic about my illness. I'm starting to think even alternative medicine has little to offer me with this. He felt bad for me and gave me an assignment to write about the use of pregnenolone for autistic adults because I said back in the day I was a good student, but I haven't done it yet. I feel like he's trying to show me I don't have it so bad. He said he'd pay me so I might as well, even though I'm frustrated (and need to stop going on the computer so much). Also, testing by my doctor showed that my body's making a normal amount of phospholipids, but maybe I need more?

### Chaostheory — 2017-04-15T23:18:07+0300

So I typed the last two posts after talking to my doctor on the phone. We're at an impasse. He wants to treat my from the bottom up, and I'd like to do the top down or outside-in approach. I went on a 4 hour journey through a forest park I found that was 10 minutes from my home...and I'm feeling good so far today. I really think this is the answer.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) His adrenal product is all adaptogens, so I'm not sure I should take it. Since Bionaut and Jack suggested I stop all exogenous antioxidants, I have, and my thinking is clearer. Tomorrow is easter and I'll be at my grandma's but I've decided that afterwards I'm basically going to live outside as I've done today...and then I can keep up my side jobs of babysitting and possibly math tutoring on care.com.

Even though alternative/bio medicine doesn't seem to have the right approach either, they were somewhat helpful in taking the pressure off of me. They also have given me a book for free about learning to release old feelings, and although I don't think that I'd need to do that if I were healthy, it seems like something I need right now.

### bionaut — 2017-04-16T01:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-212373>

Thumbs up on the nature hikes. Getting into natural settings is good stuff.

There was one point where I was taking about 10 supps. Was a waste of money in my case but results vary. A pressure cooker and a shellfish broth with turmeric and cow bones, however was not a waste of money, I encourage you to try it.

Release of negative emotions can have positive impacts on your heart and brain. Finding a meditation practice was extremely useful For mental optimization

### Remo — 2017-04-17T14:02:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-212438>

Hey! your doing awesome, keep at it and you will get more results.

I completely agree @[bionaut](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6076/) regarding supplement usage. My improvements did not happen until I quited my supplements! Instead of supplements I just use a trace mineral tincture once in a while and focus on using good water. I really should get back at bonebroths because they are a lifesaver when it comes to guthealth.

Iodine is very important but I would not supplement with it as I personally found that iodine needs co-minerals and enzymes from fresh fish to be used correctly. Taking too much supplemental iodine can really wreak havoc in your body. If you insist on using it then I would take Nascent Iodine and not Lugol's (which is much stronger and harder to regulate).

Thanks everybody! I'm really trying to keep my spirits up while I trust that nature will calm things down for me.

I'm coming around to the idea of quitting all supplements, except for the forskoline. Nothing seems to really help or be that magic bullet, if there even is one. Pregnenolone, I'm not sure whether it helps or not...I think I'm in stage 2 adrenal fatigue, so not at the worst point and perhaps I don't need it. I've had my hormones drawn a couple years ago and I remember them saying my pregnenolone wasn't that bad, so maybe I don't need extra. But then again he thought I had too many xenoestrogens, so maybe it's good to keep til I can get more leptin sensitivity and then my hormones will balance out.

The sodium bicarbonate seems to calm down my thoughts/ocd, which is kind of weird when it goes away I feel like I'm thinking about nothing.

I had eight servings of fish today. One was raw and my dad caught me eating it raw and freaked out. He also is freaked about the sungazing, so I guess I'll just have to keep that on the DL.

@[Remo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19832/) I actually have a plastic sauna, and I used it today after you mentioned it helping you to sweat intensely. The results were that I felt an increase in mood, but more brain fog/tiredness later at night. I actually had a pretty productive day for me, and I started it with youtube meditation.

I actually **think I'm the type of person that could get into ketosis still eating carbs** {based on blood tests showing I was in ketosis at times when I was eating more fat/protein), so I wouldn't have to totally give them up. **Or do you think it's a bad idea? Maybe intermittent fasting or modified ketosis would be better for us for now?**
>
> I can't see the sun from where I'm located during the sunrise, so tomorrow Im driving to a higher latitude (would be downtown, though) to actually be able to "make like the sphinx". So we'll see how that goes.

Some reliable carb sources:
\- Cauliflower cookies (egg yolk + salt and raw cauliflower.. stir and fry them for some innocant carbs).
\- Sweet potato, if you can get the purple one... so tasty! and full of anti-oxidants, vitamines, minerals, potassium, magnesium etc. Dont eat too much though.
\- I make my own vegetable "pots" where I just dump allot of vegetables + herbs in a pan and cook it. Seriously I am not noticing any downsides to eating vegetable carbs. Most veggies we think contain quite some carbs actually contain very little (Broccoli, brussel sprouts, cauliflower, zuccini.. but even higher carb ones are fine imo).
truthfully I think it is more important to eat allot of fresh fish (DHA/Iodine/selenium) then focusing if you are in keto 100% of the time.

Intermittant fasting is actually a great idea! you can even eat a diet based on carbs and still be in ketosis for a certain time if you set your time frame right.
What I do is this:
\- Eat your first meal whenever your hungry (unless your doing leptin reset etc. then I would follow Jack's protocol). btw, Having a bowel movement before breakfast is a good sign and cleans your digestive tract nicely.
\- skip lunch unless you are hungry or combine your lunch and dinner into 1 (could eat your dinner around 4PM for instance).
\- ideally you wouldn't snack anything after dinner but the main rule is to stop eating before 20:00. This way you ensure that you go to bed on an empty stomach which allows the transition to ketosis during sleep.
\- i usually do a timeframe of 14+- hours, noticing that 16 is really the most benificial for most gains (but its hard to get adjusted too).

Currently im not even in ketosis myself but this is mainly since im living with my parents atm. When I move out it becomes a bit easier to micromanage my meals.

**There appear to be three types of responses to methylfolate:**

FIRST: A person who can jump on methylfolate and feel absolutely wonderful. The only down side they experience is why didn’t they know about methylfolate before?!

SECOND: A person starts methylfolate has an amazingly incredible week where they are happy, interacting and alert. Then the second week comes and they switch to wanting to hide in a room by themselves or literally throw dishes across the room out of anger. Or they may become bed ridden from muscle aches, intense headaches or joint pain.

THIRD: A person takes a small amount of methylfolate and feels all the methylfolate side effects right out the gate.

****Methylfolate Side Effects:****

-   irritability
-   insomnia
-   sore muscles
-   achy joints
-   acne
-   rash

-   palpitations

### Chaostheory — 2017-05-11T02:02:05+0300

Anyway, I've been able to ride my bike lately...it feels like too much effort if I go far, but I always liked it growing up so it's good to get back into it.
I was trying to ride to where I thought was the nearest Burger King that had computers, and it turned out it's gone which just goes to show how much I've been missing these last few months that I never noticed that. :-/ Anyway, next to where it used to be was a new indian place and I unwisely decided to spend my money there. I had some gluten via some breaded chicken and a little dessert and some naan bread, as well as some dairy in the dishes...this was coming off of a few days off of the antibiotics. The results were that my stomach swelled up and got rock hard and my sinuses became more clogged up. I also felt like not much was going on in my head, so I won't repeat...lesson learned. The food tasted great though.

Today I've only ate twice. I'm trying to get my body and mind in better shape so I can move out. My parents are still mad at me for some bad decisions I made the last couple years, and so they are being kind of emotionally unsupportive of me, and I think this is affecting me. However, I'm not sure my health is good enough, or will be in the next week or two in order to move into this place. My sinuses are all dried up and it occured to me today how dehydrated I must be. Expecially since it's getting sunnier, I will be increasing my water intake. I started drinking at least a gallon/day. Is this good? ### JanSz — 2017-05-11T03:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-213838>

> [Chaostheory said:](/goto/post?id=213836)
>
> My sinuses are all dried up and it occured to me today how dehydrated I must be. Expecially since it's getting sunnier, I will be increasing my water intake. I started drinking at least a gallon/day.

Need to drink a gallon or more of water a day may indicate some problem.
But it may depend on circumstances.

I have not seen it explained sufficiently (so I could understand and use),
but
hydration status
and
amount of drinking
are two different states
relatively loosely correlated.

I still remember seeing video of famous here girl, previously extremely fat, holding full gallon of water. Constantly drinking between sentences, eventually reaching for the next gallon.

That is one may drink excessive amounts of water, pee out even more, and end being dehydrated, not able to hold on to all that water.
Other person's body may be rather frugal with water but be sufficiently hydrated.

One needs to have electrolytes and hormones in order and balanced to be able to use water to be hydrated.
How to maintain that status with conscious activities, I am not sure.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) often tells us to be hydrated, and observe bun/creatine ratio to find if we are hydrated, but I do not remember seeing explanation of what to do to get there,
other that drinking water, see lots of sun, observe circadian rhythm and stay away from nnEMF.
I think there is more to it.

In the mean time I am watching
sodium
potasium
magnesium
chloride
CO2
aldosterone
adh
renin
Renin-Angiotensin-Aldosterone

but not really sure what to do with all that

//

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-05-17T14:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-214188>

Seasonal produce Chaostheory and eat your meals outdoors in the uv light. We have so many great farmer's markets to choose from here. If you are not able to grow your own garden, which I highly suggest doing so, seek out the local farmers and get to know them.

I'm not keen at all on you not being able to wear your glasses at the register either.... You will be looking directly at the blue light screen there and have the toxic blue lights up above as well. Is it worth your health? You have to decide. If you make the decision to go ahead, there are other blue light blocking glasses that are reasonably priced that you can choose from. and wear them at the register. Gunnars block about 70% of the blue light and can be purchased at Best Buy, or on Amazon. Be sure to keep your skin covered indoors as well and when you take your breaks, go outside and take your covering off and glasses and expose your eyes and skin to the sunlight and this will help to mitigate the effects of the indoor blue light /nnemf.

Meijer has a lot of organic mustards to choose from and they now carry Wild Planet and other wild caught sardines that are in spring water. Their prices are also very reasonable there.

[https://www.amazon.com/Gunnar-Optik...9913&sr=8-1&keywords=gunnars+computer+glasses](https://www.amazon.com/Gunnar-Optiks-INT-00101-Intercept-Advanced-Glasses/dp/B00CAUTK0E/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1495019913&sr=8-1&keywords=gunnars+computer+glasses)

### Chaostheory — 2017-05-18T03:04:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-214231>

I picked up some stone ground mustard and oysters tonight. Oysters have a huge antidepressant effect for me.

I'm going to the farmer's market tomorrow morning, and starting CT in earnest tomorrow. Once I let myself get used to the water, it feels interesting, and my body starts to generate some heat. I think my discomfort with it is just related to anxiety/emotional regulation.

I'm also going to slow down a bit with everything, including the sunbathing (except I started doing it nude the last couple days and I think it's helpful), but I feel like I've taken some aspects of this protocol a bit too quickly. I just was reading Jack's "first thing to read" post and yeah...I definitely am doing things out of order but I had to to prove to myself it was working. Now I can maybe slow it down. I just have to do this balancing act carefully, especially considering my family.

With the new job, I'm not sure it's right for me, but it's a paycheck. I might keep applying for other jobs, the one problem I run into is lack of confidence given that I have a patchy working record and I didn't finish college, but I'm putting that behind me. I was ill and really did try my best.

Edit: Two more questions, before I go back to real life again...I need to get my own cell phone, so if I choose to move out in the future I will have a way to communicate. What do you guys suggest? Do the cheaper 3rd party cell phone companies that piggyback off of the others generate more emf? Lol I'll try looking it up if no one knows.

### Chaostheory — 2017-11-22T12:42:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-228618>

Hi guys. Just updating you...I'm still here. Started working again, at a group home for the developmentally disabled...figured this would give me some perspective on my life. This could work out well for me if I still want to do the whole move to florida thing. I just have to get through the michigan winter now.

Also, I went back to the integrative doc. Seemed wisest thing to do.

I was wondering how many posts we have to have before we can ask questions on the "ask jack?" section. I just had some silly question I thought of for him, based off of a facebook quiz I just took. Lol I need a life...

### Chaostheory — 2018-01-28T06:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-of-feeling-geriatric.19123/post-233658>

Ok, today I finally made my body relax somehow. I drank half a gallon of reverse osmosis water while in the grocery store, and my relaxation system in my body turned on. I was drinking a lot of coconut water, eating bananas, and upped my mineral supplement I'd been taking so who knows what really happened. Also boss got me a space heater and I used it for first time last night. I also stopped myself from overeating the last 2 days and didn't eat til the early afternoon. Unfortunately haven't been able to repeat again...but I will soon.

I need some help on how to stay as optimal as possible while living in the climate that i'm in. I basically live a 3rd shift lifestyle as I have to get up sometimes to redirect a certain resident. Then during the day I just started working a few hours at a local daycare in their kitchen. I have time to go outside in the sun since there's an hour break, and I got a gym memebership that includes tanning and a pretty cool lie-down massage machine (I did a little too late today and have been wired all night). The group home I'm working in also has a nice yard for earthing. I would have started all this earlier, but I let a negative coworker make me feel bad for taking the job when I didn't have my shit together.

I'm still needing to take amino acid replacement therapy, and haven't even started the protocol the doctor wants me to do. Not sure I will need it after what happened today. I'm going to start really paying attention to diet and environment more and see what happens.

### JanSz — 2018-01-28T15:08:05+0200

When able you may want to check your body's (breath condensate) for deuterium.
Desirable is to be under 130ppm (D/H)

Eat lots of fat, pork and beef lard, butter, coconut oil they will lower your deuterium.
Egg yolks are low in deuterium
Do not drink coconut water it is high in deuterium.
Solids in the green salads you eat are low in deuterium, but juice made of it is high.

Aquafina made by Pepsi is the only known to me RO water that was tested and found low in deuterium (139.5ppp (D/H), that is much much lower than any known to me natural waters (that usually are very expensive).
I have send for testing RO water sold by Costco, will publish results when get them. That water is the cheapest that I know of.

Body makes matrix water out of the fat you eat. 1 volume of fat make 1.1 volume of water.
When you eat enough fat your need for drinking water should be less.
Think of 1.5 -2 liters/day

Eat Redmont salt, at least 2 teaspoons a day (but no more than 12 tablespoons (twelve Tbsp).
It eases kidneys energy use, and adjusts positively many micronutrients.
Body, when adjusting to low available salt recirculates existing salt, that makes kidneys work overtime.
Up to 60% energy used by kidneys may be spend on that work.

KIE of D to O, N, and C.
(hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon)
(less common) isotopes are used by our bodies to adjust biological processes

### AJ The Iceman — 2017-02-23T18:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-ir-lighting.19127/post-209139>

In the low cortisol thread I saw a brief mentioning of how the majority of IR bulbs on the market have virtually no IR but mostly red. I have purchased:

Sperti sun tanning lamp
Sunlite LED UV light at 365nm
Black Duracell CFLs
Solarglow 160 or Exo Terra Sunray (which one is better)
Sunray has crazy high lux but better color temp regarding 3000k limit set by Kruse (I have no clue about it)
Im thinking UVB exposure from sperti would be sufficient and dont need it all day.
ufo660's
rubylux red's
now before i buy the IR bulbs, i want to get some clarification.
Ive seen how important IR light is through scanning the various threads at a glance, especially to balance the desired artificial spectrum at certain points in the day. What bulbs produce sufficient IR in the desired spectrums/intensities.

also, is there any benefit from just using red engine paint on incandescent bulbs to use as red light?
i ask this because im under the impression that incandescent bulbs are not polarized, and in that sense,
may be more in line with natural red spectrum light.

It would be nice to combine all these things with a proper automated sequence for anyone unable to move, change jobs. maybe this is not a good idea because people will be falsely lulled into slightly better health but not optimal.

# Thread: Red Goggles, In-Door Light, Iodine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-goggles-in-door-light-iodine.19131/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Samuel Brunic — 2017-02-24T04:51:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-goggles-in-door-light-iodine.19131/post-209184>

I have been listening to Dr. Kruse for years.
I have lots of little questions. I would appreciate any advice or thoughts.

**Which googles block the most blue light?**
I have red, orange and amber UVEX goggles and I wear one at night. I hear most people use orange/amber but they don't seem to block much blue light-if I see blue, then blue gets through is my thinking. They all block out a blue LED I have. Red seems to block out more blue light. Dr. Kruse has said that red is the antidote to blue.
I also have JINS with amber lenses but they don't seem to block much. BluTech lenses are $400! I use NightShift, f.lux obviously.

**What kind of in-door lighting set-up do you have?**
I am trying to get UV, full spectrum and IR light in-doors. I have a full spectrum plant food bulb that is only visible. I need to find an IR bulb.
There are [mercury vapor UV bulbs](https://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-Intensity-Self-Ballasted/dp/B00101JIFG/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1487903589&sr=8-5&keywords=reptile+uv+bulbs) for reptiles-**is this harmful to our eyes?** I keep hearing UV is bad for our eyes i.e. nail curing lamps? I'm looking at the Exo Terra Solar-Glo High Intensity Self-Ballasted Uv/Heat Mercury Vapor Lamp.

**Thoughts on iodine supplementation?**
I take a few drops of Lugol's in a drink every few days. Dr. Kruse talks about structured water in the CSF around iodine so I take it as a supplement. I don't believe kelp or seafood has enough. I eat fresh wild salmon daily. How do you get your iodine?

### Debntx — 2017-02-27T03:44:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-goggles-in-door-light-iodine.19131/post-209358>

i have no advice - just thoughts - i am new here as well - edit: educated thoughts much better-

another option for computer is iristech software for eye protection-which i think is better - i use iris mini and set it low - i use the paid version lifetime is $2 (i think) worth it to me - just keep my monitor set at 2300K - since i do nothing that requires anything exact in the way of color rendering

I use the uvex orange goggles at night and at work but recently i have been toying with the idea about using various levels of blocking blue for different times of the day at work under fluorescent lighting-starting out with light yellow - a graduated blocking of blue - pulling out the big guns at night if needed - i just ordered the carbonshades - and dewalt red safety glasses on a whim - will see how they do -I remember Dr Kruse saying that Red is the antidote to blue but I didn't make that connection until you wrote it above in regards to using a "redder" goggle to block more of the blue light - even though i did order some - that thought was not part of my reasoning process. i also rec'd a quote for some bluTech lenses of $299 and i think i will pass on that- Additionally, i would say nightshift does NOT do enough to block blue but i did learn something about a feature on my iphone - ios10 - about setting your color filter on your phone - and i set mine on red - but it looks funky so i will have to play around with it - edit: maybe it looks funky for a reason

at work- i use the nightlight Red- reptile bulb since i'm at work i can get away with that but not sure about the other options for UV light -

As for iodine i used to use Iodoral 12.5 mg - but in the last year started using Nature's Way Kelp 1-2 capsules after reading a little of Dr Wilson's work -

I also read about sauna therapy from Dr Wilson's site but now I use my pocket sauna not to sweat out toxins but to "pre-treat" myself before I head into work under my fluorescent light working conditions using rubylux red light array - 5-10mins only and i don't actually do it daily but a few times a week - i got the idea from a podcast where Dr Kruse spoke of a study about mice pre-treated with red light... and that gave me the idea... i am not a mouse but that idea may just work for me.

good luck to you.

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-27T12:02:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-goggles-in-door-light-iodine.19131/post-209366>

> [Samuel Brunic said:](/goto/post?id=209184)
>
> I have been listening to Dr. Kruse for years.
> I have lots of little questions. I would appreciate any advice or thoughts.
>
> **Which googles block the most blue light?**
> I have red, orange and amber UVEX goggles and I wear one at night. I hear most people use orange/amber but they don't seem to block much blue light-if I see blue, then blue gets through is my thinking. They all block out a blue LED I have. Red seems to block out more blue light. Dr. Kruse has said that red is the antidote to blue.
> I also have JINS with amber lenses but they don't seem to block much. BluTech lenses are $400! I use NightShift, f.lux obviously.

I bought a pair of red, but intuitively didn't like them. A clue came when I found this article
[http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO\_IOP\_ light.pdf](http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO_IOP_%20light.pdf) about red increasing intra ocular pressure. From the article it seems that darkness naturally increases IOP, but adding red increases it further, with green having the opposite effect, i.e. in this scenario green is the antidote to red, and in natural sunlight green is present. Red glasses may not have the chronological disadvantage of blue and green at night, i.e. be chronologically neutral, but may have other photodynamic disadvantages (such as increasing my risk of glaucoma) if used chronically as a lifestyle choice.

### Mountain Man — 2017-03-01T20:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sleep-wow.19132/post-209485>

The entire earth is not "dirty EMF's". Transformers are never buried so it is very easy to avoid those. As far as power lines you just need to stay away from them. You can buy a meter to detect EMF's and check the location of where you bury a grounding stake to be sure it is okay. The earth provides a very powerful ground and you have to be close to buried power lines to get dirty EMF's. If you are not able to do this another option is ground your shoes with one of several options on the market and go walking in a park or out in the country well away from any buildings, buried power lines or other power sources.

No, that is not a problem. An EMF meter tests both (E) electrical and (M) magnetic (F) fields. If you are not able to purchase an EMF meter and they are very available on the internet at low cost then you should not be doing any grounding.

### Scompy — 2017-03-04T19:29:36+0200

For clarity, I'm wanting to show the subtlety of what I'm saying. If I am grounded to the earth, and I am also in a magnetic field at the same time (like lets say a 60 millGauss field) due to a wire running through soil where I am standing grounded to the earth, yes, I am grounded. However, the AC generated magnetic field would still be penetrating my body doing some damage.

### JoeT — 2017-03-04T20:17:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sleep-wow.19132/post-209624>

Excellent point Scompy. To drive it home....I've had an EMF expert recently advise me to find an area with .1 mg or less to ground in. Obtaining an accurate measurement down to .1 requires a dedicated magnetic meter...which start at $275-$300.

In addition, grounding should also be done in an RF environment of .01 mw/m2 or less. Such a measurement can be easily taken w/ a Cornet though.

### Mountain Man — 2017-03-04T21:14:35+0200

However, the AC generated magnetic field would still be penetrating my body doing some damage.

That's why you need an EMF meter to make sure you are not in an AC generated magnetic field. If you are in a home with the electricity turned on at the breaker box you are in higher than a .1 MG environment even if everything like TV, computer, space heaters, etc are turned off so that is not an achievable goal unless you shut off all electricity to the entire house at the box. That means you will have nothing electrical including your gas furnace functional.

You do not need to be grounded 100% of the time in fact even 15 minutes a day can work wonders for your health. So while flying or driving you do not worry about it. There are loose straps that go under a car that ground it by attaching to the body of the car with a conducting strap that is long enough to scrap along on the street below if you want to go that far.

### Mountain Man — 2017-03-06T17:31:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sleep-wow.19132/post-209761>

You would not be able to ground yourself in a plane since you would be so high above the ground, have no access to the outside and are traveling too fast anyway. In a car it is not the EMF of the car that is the issue it is the whether the road underneath the car is grounded without EMF pollution from power lines and other electrical sources below. Usually when people ground a car they do not ground themselves so the purpose is just to dissipate static electricity from the body of the car.

### Mountain Man — 2017-03-07T21:19:31+0200

For purposes of health a floating ground is very different than an actual ground connected to the earth and it is not accurate to refer to them as equivalent. In the Wikipedia.org article for instance it states "Floating grounds can be dangerous if they are caused by failure to properly ground equipment that was designed to require grounding". Humans evolved with an actual connection to the earth not a floating ground. I have not seen any published research on whether there are any benefits or dangers for people grounded to a plane.

l’d hypothesize that this situation describes an overloaded building-ground circuit. In that case, I’d further hypothesize that increasing such a building’s connection to earth’s electric field—i.e. lowering that AC **impedance** (& DC _resistance_)—would cause the excess dirty electricity to dissipate more efficiently &/or completely for any given moment in time.
I’d speculate that augmenting/supplementing the total wire gauge\[-to-length ratio\] connecting the building’s ground circuit to the directly earthed electrode(s) would accomplish this effect. Additionally, I’d presume adding to the total volume (_and surface area_) of ground-contacting electrodes bonded to the system would effect the same outcome. Can any physics savant weigh in on this possibility?

# Thread: best treatment for fungal skin infections
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-treatment-for-fungal-skin-infections.19135/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Smith — 2017-02-25T01:22:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-treatment-for-fungal-skin-infections.19135/post-209256>

What is the best treatment for fungal skin infections other than ketoconazole or prescription medicines?

### JanSz — 2017-02-25T16:43:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-treatment-for-fungal-skin-infections.19135/post-209271>

> [John Smith said:](/goto/post?id=209256)
>
> What is the best treatment for fungal skin infections other than ketoconazole or prescription medicines?

ketoconazole

interesting beast

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketoconazole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketoconazole)
Antiandrogenic and antiglucocorticoid
CYP17A1 inhibitor

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CYP17A1\_inhibitor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CYP17A1_inhibitor)
A **CYP17A1 inhibitor** is a type of [drug](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drug) which [inhibits](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enzyme_inhibitor) the [enzyme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enzyme) [CYP17A1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CYP17A1). It may inhibit both of the functions of the enzyme, [17α-hydroxylase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/17%CE%B1-hydroxylase) and [17,20-lyase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/17,20-lyase), or may be selective for inhibition of one of these two functions (generally 17,20-lyase). These drugs **prevent the conversion of [pregnane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pregnane) [steroids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steroid) into [androgens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Androgen) like [testosterone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Testosterone) and therefore are [androgen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Androgen) [biosynthesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biosynthesis) inhibitors and functional [antiandrogens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antiandrogen).** Examples of CYP17A1 inhibitors include the older drug [ketoconazole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ketoconazole) and the newer drugs [abiraterone acetate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abiraterone_acetate), [orteronel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orteronel), [galeterone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galeterone), and [seviteronel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seviteronel).

''''''''''''
My friend, with late stages of prostate cancer, (when cancer is able to make its own testosterone)
is considering
[abiraterone acetate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abiraterone_acetate)

tough decision.

//

# Thread: High keytones on organic acids test and high fumaric and Malic acid metabolites
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-keytones-on-organic-acids-test-and-high-fumaric-and-malic-acid-metabolites.19136/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2017-02-25T01:35:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-keytones-on-organic-acids-test-and-high-fumaric-and-malic-acid-metabolites.19136/post-209257>

I have been dealing with low cortisol for a few years now so I finally decided to do the Great Plains OAT test. I have been struggling with low cholesterol (159) and low downstreams hormones like pregneolone and so forth.I eat a high protein ( salmon) and high good fat diet. I don't ever have sugar cravings so I was surprised to see some yeast markers ( tartaric acid 5.9 with reference range being <4.5 and arabinose 34 with ref range being <29) and a lot of bacterial markers.

2 hydroxyphenylacetic was 1.1 with reference range being .06-.66.
4 Hydroxybenzoic was 2.2 with ref range being <1.3
5 hydroxyhippuric was 19 with reference range being .79-17
DHPPA (beneficial bacteria was good..right at mean
hippuric acid was also fine at 380 ref range being <613

Clostridia markers:

4 hydroxyphenylacetic was high at 24 with ref range being <19
All other markers actually very low including HPHPA, 4-cresol, and 3 indoleacetic

So now what really blew me away was the high keytones.
3 Hydroxybutyric was 470 with ref range being 3.1
Acetoacetic 814 with ref range being <10
4 hydroxybutyric 8 with ref range <4.8
Adipic also high at 44 ref range .04-3.8
Suberic 16 with ref range .18-2.2
sebacic 4.2 ref range <.24
So all metabolites are high under the keytone and fatty acid oxidation except ethylamalonic and methylsuccinic
Mitochondrial markers are high as well.
Fumaric 2 ref range being <.94
Malic high at 2.2 with reg range being .06-1.8

dopamine is actually high with HVA being 4.3 ref range (.8 to 3.6)
VMA low at 1.1 (.46 to 3.7)
So this probably because bacteria are messing with the dopamine B hydroxylase enzyme and dopamine is not broken down.
Ok so B2 was low and so was b6. methymalonic acid also high at 8 ref range being <2.3

So I am trying to understand where the glich is in the system. Can yeast and bacteria cause such havoc on mitochondria. Dr Kruse mentioned eye, skin, gut , and lung surface is what needs to be protected to keep mitochondria healthy.
So is it just a matter of adding in carnitine, more coq10, and some magnesium or is something majorly awry here? I did the micronutrient test last year and carnitine was sufficient , magnesium was great, coq 10 was at the top tip of chart. Choline was low so I have added in choline bitartrate for a year now. B2 was low so been taking 50 mg of that as well. All this time I was wondering whyyyyy I am low though so possibly enterobacter species of bacteria eating up b2. Had a stool test in 2015 which showed nothing else except excessive enterobacter. Did some herbs but I guess it wasnt eradicated fully.
So is the glich in the digestion and assimilation of fatty acids possibly due to low bile as bacteria are deconjugating bile and not enough carnitine to produce acetyl coA? Or is it in krebs cycle where some vitamin deficiency like perhaps B3 is allowing malic and fumaric acid to back up? Appears to be both. I have added in some ox bile to aid with digestion of fats but again why cant I digest fats? All of this goes back to bacteria?
Now if looking at quantum aspect of things...red light to increase cytochrome oxidase C enzyme. Will this help? I do block blue light as much as I can but will get even more strict with it. Seems to be complex 2 where FAD enters is where the issues lie?!
My main concern is the excessive keytones and what this actually means and how to correct?
Anyone did an OAT test or had any issues with fatty acid metabolism?

**\--------------------------------------------------------
Post link to that video.**

All waters that are generally available for drinking, expensive water and inexpensive water, can be put in one category.
Among them
only RO water have significantly lower Deuterium content.
Any other water in that group, when looking at their deuterium content, is not even close to RO.

No wonder, RO water is man made water, it does not happen naturally.

When discussing RO water
it is easy to get misunderstand.
Water obtained from the other side of RO membrane is not ready for consumption.
It is immaterial what ph it have at that point with exception of quality control.
Before water is ready for consumption there are numerous other steps,
some of them manage minerals and ph. Potassium, sodium, magnesium.
\------------

DDW Deuterium Depleted Water
is a man made water.
More like a medicine.
I do not think it is meant as a supply of electrolytes.
I see zero for sodium content on a Preventa 25ppm label.
It should be drank on advice of informed person.

When @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) introduced us to deuterium he did that with referencing to work of László G. Boros.
[https://www.cignaturehealth.com/](https://www.cignaturehealth.com/)
László G. Boros uses Preventa for his DDW. (And claims no knowledge of quality of other waters that claim to be DDW, but hopes that people will get informed about the idea and use it).
[https://preventa.hu/about-preventa/the-preventa-quality](https://preventa.hu/about-preventa/the-preventa-quality)

\-----
Most of the therapeutic use of DDW starts with 105ppm.
For number of reasons 105ppm is obtained by mixing
1 part of 25ppm DDW (contains no minerals)
and
2 parts of ordinary drinking water (this is also a source of minerals)
at this stage there is likely little worry about getting short on minerals
but as therapy progresses DDW part may increase and at certain point one should begin to monitor and optimize minerals.

natural amount of D (150 ppm, equivalent to 16.8 mmol/L) using fractional distillation 20

ing water.**

**181.5 mg/L of MgCl2⋅6H2O, and**
**262.5 mg/L of CaCl2⋅2H2O.**

D concentration was determined by infrared spectroscopy with a precision of ±3ppm with a Foxboro Miran 1A CVF IR spectrophotometer
(at a 4-μm wavelength, in a 0.2-mm CaF2 cell).

### Marina C — 2018-08-23T20:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-ranges.19141/post-249208>

I think its better to test for thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) to confirm Hashi's rather than use antibodies. Antibodies cause the issues but because they appear in such a large variety it is hard to use them to test for it as the test could just come back with higher numbers if you're fighting an unrelated illness. They do test for anitbodies to help confirm, but it is better to test TSH first. Here's a few links with some information I was able to find:

[https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/hashimotos-disease/symptoms-causes/syc-20351855](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/hashimotos-disease/symptoms-causes/syc-20351855)

[https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/endocrine-diseases/hashimotos-disease](https://www.niddk.nih.gov/health-information/endocrine-diseases/hashimotos-disease)

[http://www.genetex.com/TSH-receptor-antibody-GTX33561.html](http://www.genetex.com/TSH-receptor-antibody-GTX33561.html)

[https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/hashimotos-disease/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20351860](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/hashimotos-disease/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20351860)

# Thread: Hello, thank you for having me :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-thank-you-for-having-me.19147/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thijs Bierman — 2017-02-27T12:28:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-thank-you-for-having-me.19147/post-209367>

Hello Everybody,

I have been a member of this forum since october last year but did not really get into it since a couple a weeks.
Mr. Kruse's posts on FB have been very informative ( And a bit overwhelming) and i have decided to dive into the wealth of knowledge.

I am a 39 year old male from Amsterdam, The Netherlands and i've been struggling with ADD symptoms like poor executive function & concentration for my whole life. In the last three years new symptoms started to show up; infected root canal, weight loss, anxiety and loose stools among many others.

All my physician could find was a parasite called Blasto Hominis. i decided to skip the prescribed anti-biotics because i knew that this would only damage my micro biome and make pathogens more resistant. Instead i decided on a hair mineral test. This showed high lead, high copper, suspected and low chromium. I did one round of andy cutler chelation with ALA but this made me very sick and i stopped. Right now i am taking Allicine daily because i suspected pathogens are responsible for my GI tract issues (High copper, low iron)

I have tried many things to combat my symptoms. Diet, Supplements, Nootropics, Yoga, Chi Kung and meditation. Although some of these help temporarily or to some extent and i am seeing some improvements i can not seem to get on the better side. Especially my work (in sales) and relationships are suffering. At this time i can not even be bothered anymore to go into work...

Mr Kruse's information about EMF's, Circadian Rhythm, location, sunlight, diet, seasonal eating, magnetism etc make sense to me. I hope these provide the answers i am looking for.

Thank you for having me. It will take some time before i am up to date with the information provided here and i will try to contribute the best i can. If there are people here with the same health issues i would appreciate hearing about your successes with these protocols.

Kind regards,

Thijs

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-27T16:33:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-thank-you-for-having-me.19147/post-209372>

Blasto hominus grows when sunlight is absent......where you live the sun is sparse. It is also a protozoan that rarely causes gut problems.
_Blastocystis_ is one of the most common human parasites in the world and has a global distribution. Blastocystitis are free living, they have to find their food on their own. These protozoa may be HOLOZOIC (protozoa that feeds on other microbes: bacteria or smaller protozoans). This is why they grow in the shadow of light. They eat other things that can capture the light. In general, the protozoa that feed on a large variety of microbes thrive everywhere, while the ones that are selective for their food are quite limited in their distribution. Most come from pigs and chicken guts polluting the water networks and it carries a fecal to oral infection.
Nature presents us with a series of great opportunities brilliantly disguised as impossible situations.
**Awareness of this fact**, alone doesn't enlist change.

**You cannot get well in the same environment you got ill within. It’s a stark and unpleasant message that most people don't want to ponder. I understand environment as firstly the macrocosm, secondly the microcosm. Worlds within worlds, Wheels within wheels. Waves within particles, particles within waves.**

The good news keeps on coming for the sun!!!!! [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/28213657/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/28213657/)

**I understand environment as firstly the macrocosm, secondly the microcosm. Worlds within worlds, Wheels within wheels. Waves within particles, particles within waves ->**As above, So below, As Within, So Without.

Nature will provide me with the answers. I just have some studying to do

### JanSz — 2017-03-01T00:01:49+0200
